questions.explanation: Under the group (composite) depreciation method per ASC 360-10-35-8, no gain or loss is recognized on the retirement of individual assets within the group. The difference between the proceeds and the asset cost is debited (or credited) to accumulated depreciation. Entry: Debit Cash $3,000, Debit Accumulated Depreciation $2,000 (plug = $5,000 - $3,000); Credit Furniture $5,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35-8によるグループ（総合）償却法では、グループ内の個別資産の除却で損益を認識しない。売却代金と資産原価の差額は減価償却累計額に借方（または貸方）計上。仕訳：借方 現金$3,000、借方 減価償却累計額$2,000（差額=$5,000-$3,000）、貸方 備品$5,000。

questions.stem: A company has a broadcasting license carried at $500,000 as an indefinite-life intangible asset. Due to new regulations, the license will not be renewed and has a remaining legal life of 5 years. What accounting change is required?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize an immediate impairment loss of $500,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Begin amortizing the license over 5 years and test for impairment using the recoverability test for finite-life assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue to test for impairment annually without amortization until expiration

choices.label: D
choices.text: Retroactively restate prior periods to reflect amortization from the acquisition date

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-30-35-17, when an indefinite-life intangible asset is subsequently determined to have a finite life, the asset is tested for impairment under the indefinite-life framework at the time of reclassification. Then, the carrying amount (after any impairment) is amortized prospectively over the remaining useful life. Going forward, impairment testing follows the finite-life asset approach (recoverability test per ASC 360-10-35-17). This is treated as a change in accounting estimate, applied prospectively.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-30-35-17により、不確定耐用年数の無形資産がその後有限と判断された場合、再分類時に不確定耐用年数の枠組みで減損テストを実施。その後、帳簿価額（減損後）を残余耐用年数にわたり将来に向かって償却。以降の減損テストは有限耐用年数資産のアプローチ（ASC 360-10-35-17の回収可能性テスト）に従う。会計上の見積りの変更として将来に向かって適用。

questions.stem: Timber Corp. owns a 100-acre tract of timberland with a total carrying amount of $1,000,000. Timber sells 30 acres for $400,000. What gain or loss should Timber recognize on the partial disposal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Gain of $100,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain of $400,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loss of $300,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: No gain or loss is recognized on partial disposals

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-40-3, when a portion of a long-lived asset is disposed of, the carrying amount of the disposed portion is removed and any difference between proceeds and carrying amount is recognized as a gain or loss. Carrying amount of 30 acres = (30/100) x $1,000,000 = $300,000. Proceeds = $400,000. Gain = $400,000 - $300,000 = $100,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-40-3により、長期性資産の一部が処分された場合、処分部分の帳簿価額を除却し、売却代金との差額を損益として認識。30エーカーの帳簿価額=(30/100) x $1,000,000=$300,000。売却代金=$400,000。利益=$400,000-$300,000=$100,000。

questions.stem: Construct Corp. has two qualifying assets under construction during the year. Asset A had weighted-average accumulated expenditures of $1,200,000 and Asset B had $800,000. Construct Corp. had the following debt: a $1,000,000 construction loan at 8% specifically for Asset A, and general borrowings of $3,000,000 at 6%. What is the total capitalized interest?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20-30, interest capitalization is computed for each qualifying asset. Asset A: specific borrowing of $1,000,000 at 8% covers $1,000,000 of the $1,200,000 weighted-average accumulated expenditures. Interest on specific debt = $1,000,000 x 8% = $80,000. Excess expenditures for A = $1,200,000 - $1,000,000 = $200,000, which uses the weighted-average rate on general borrowings (6%). Interest on excess = $200,000 x 6% = $12,000. Asset B: no specific borrowing, so the entire $800,000 uses the general rate. Interest = $800,000 x 6% = $48,000. Total avoidable interest = $80,000 + $12,000 + $48,000 = $140,000. Actual interest = ($1,000,000 x 8%) + ($3,000,000 x 6%) = $260,000. Since avoidable interest ($140,000) is less than actual interest ($260,000), capitalized interest = $140,000.

questions.stem: CloudFirst Corp. pays $240,000 for a 3-year cloud computing arrangement that is determined to be a hosting arrangement (service contract), not a software license. Under ASC 350-40 (as updated by ASU 2018-15), how should CloudFirst account for this arrangement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize $240,000 as an intangible asset and amortize over 3 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expense $240,000 immediately in the first year

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize $80,000 per year as a prepaid expense and expense ratably over the hosting term

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalize $240,000 as property, plant, and equipment

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-40-15-4A (as updated by ASU 2018-15), a cloud computing arrangement that is a service contract (hosting arrangement) is not a software license and the fees are not capitalized as an intangible asset. The arrangement is accounted for as a service contract. If paid upfront, the $240,000 is recorded as a prepaid expense and expensed ratably over the 3-year hosting period ($80,000 per year). Implementation costs may be capitalized separately under ASC 350-40-25-16 if they meet certain criteria.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-40-15-4A（ASU 2018-15改正）により、サービス契約（ホスティング）であるクラウドコンピューティング契約はソフトウェアライセンスではなく、料金は無形資産として資産計上しない。サービス契約として会計処理。前払いの場合、$240,000は前払費用として記録し、3年間のホスティング期間にわたり均等に費用化（$80,000/年）。実装費用はASC 350-40-25-16の要件を満たせば別途資産計上可能。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, PetroEnergy recorded an asset retirement obligation of $500,000 (present value) using a credit-adjusted risk-free rate of 5%. At the end of Year 2, PetroEnergy revises the estimated future retirement cost upward, increasing the ARO liability by $100,000 (present value). What is the accretion expense for Year 2 (before recording the revision)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 410-20-35-3, accretion expense is calculated by multiplying the beginning ARO balance by the credit-adjusted risk-free rate. Year 1: Accretion = $500,000 x 5% = $25,000. Ending ARO balance Year 1 = $500,000 + $25,000 = $525,000. Year 2: Accretion expense (before revision) = $525,000 x 5% = $26,250. The $100,000 revision is recorded at the end of Year 2 and will affect Year 3 accretion.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20-35-3により、割増費用は期首ARO残高に信用調整リスクフリーレートを乗じて計算。Year 1：割増費用=$500,000 x 5%=$25,000。Year 1末ARO残高=$500,000+$25,000=$525,000。Year 2：割増費用（改定前）=$525,000 x 5%=$26,250。$100,000の改定はYear 2末に記録され、Year 3の割増費用に影響する。

questions.subtopic: Deferred Payment Purchase - Cash Price Equivalent
questions.stem: Cedar Corp. acquires equipment by signing a non-interest-bearing note requiring a single payment of $50,000 in 3 years. No established cash price for the equipment is available, but the prevailing market interest rate for similar notes is 8%. The present value of $1 at 8% for 3 periods is 0.7938. At what amount should Cedar initially record the equipment?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-30-25-8, when equipment is acquired with a non-interest-bearing note and no cash price is available, the asset is recorded at the present value of the note using the prevailing market rate. PV = $50,000 x 0.7938 = $39,690. The $10,310 difference between the face amount ($50,000) and present value ($39,690) is a discount that is recognized as interest expense over the 3-year note term. The asset is recorded at $39,690, not its face amount. ($46,296 uses a 1-period factor in error; $38,000 applies a flat 24% to the face amount in error.)
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-30-25-8により、無利息手形で設備を取得し現金価格が不明な場合、市場実勢利率を用いた手形の現在価値で資産を計上する。PV = $50,000 x 0.7938 = $39,690。額面$50,000と現在価値$39,690の差額$10,310は割引額として3年間の手形期間にわたり利息費用として認識する。資産は額面ではなく$39,690で計上。（$46,296は誤って1期間の係数を使用、$38,000は額面に一律24%を適用した誤り。）

questions.stem: Birchwood Inc. purchases machinery with an invoice price of $200,000 under terms 2/10, n/30. Birchwood pays within the discount period. It also incurs $6,000 freight-in and $4,000 installation costs. Under the net method, at what amount should the machinery be capitalized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-30-1, the cost of machinery includes the net purchase price plus all costs necessary to bring it to working condition. Cash discounts available are treated as a reduction of cost (whether taken or not, under the net/preferred method). Net invoice price = $200,000 x (1 - 0.02) = $196,000. Add freight-in $6,000 and installation $4,000. Capitalized cost = $196,000 + $6,000 + $4,000 = $206,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-30-1により、機械の取得原価は正味購入価格に稼働状態にするための必要費用を加える。現金割引は（取得しても・しなくても、純額法では）原価の減額として処理。正味請求額 = $200,000 x (1 - 0.02) = $196,000。運送費$6,000と据付費$4,000を加算。資産計上額 = $196,000 + $6,000 + $4,000 = $206,000。

questions.stem: Aspen Corp. acquires a specialized machine by issuing 5,000 shares of its $10 par common stock. On the acquisition date, the stock has a readily determinable fair value of $32 per share, and an independent appraisal values the machine at $170,000. At what amount should Aspen record the machine?
questions.explanation: When PP&E is acquired by issuing equity securities that have a readily determinable fair value, the asset is recorded at the fair value of the securities issued, as this is the more objective and reliable measure of the transaction's value. Fair value of stock issued = 5,000 shares x $32 = $160,000. The machine is recorded at $160,000. The $50,000 par value (5,000 x $10) and the appraisal value are not used when an active market price for the shares exists.
questions.explanationJa: 市場価格が容易に決定できる株式を発行してPP&Eを取得する場合、取引価値のより客観的で信頼性の高い指標である発行株式の公正価値で資産を計上する。発行株式の公正価値 = 5,000株 x $32 = $160,000。機械は$160,000で計上。額面$50,000（5,000 x $10）や鑑定評価額は、株式の活発な市場価格が存在する場合は使用しない。

questions.stem: Granite Co. self-constructs a machine for its own use. Direct materials cost $80,000 and direct labor cost $60,000. The machine required 2,000 direct labor hours. Granite normally applies variable overhead at $5 per direct labor hour to production. If Granite had instead purchased the machine, it would have paid $165,000. At what amount should the self-constructed machine be capitalized (incremental/full cost concept, but not exceeding purchase price)?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-30-1, a self-constructed asset is capitalized at direct materials, direct labor, and an allocable portion of overhead. Direct materials $80,000 + direct labor $60,000 + applied overhead (2,000 hrs x $5 = $10,000) = $150,000. Because the computed cost ($150,000) is below the external purchase price ($165,000), no write-down to a lower amount is required and no loss is recognized. The asset is capitalized at $150,000. (If accumulated construction cost had exceeded fair value/purchase price, the excess would be expensed as a loss.)
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-30-1により、自家建設資産は直接材料費、直接労務費、配賦間接費で資産計上する。直接材料$80,000 + 直接労務$60,000 + 配賦間接費（2,000時間 x $5 = $10,000）= $150,000。計算原価$150,000が外部購入価格$165,000を下回るため、減額や損失認識は不要。資産は$150,000で計上。（建設累計原価が公正価値・購入価格を超えていれば超過分を損失計上する。）

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest - Interest Earned on Borrowed Funds
questions.stem: Summit Corp. borrows $1,000,000 at 9% specifically to construct a building. Before the funds are needed for construction, Summit temporarily invests $400,000 of the loan proceeds and earns $12,000 in interest income during the period. Weighted-average accumulated expenditures equal $1,000,000. What amount of interest should Summit capitalize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20-30-10, interest earned on the temporary investment of borrowed construction funds does NOT reduce the amount of interest cost eligible for capitalization (for general construction projects under U.S. GAAP). Avoidable interest = specific borrowing $1,000,000 x 9% = $90,000. The $12,000 of investment income is recognized separately as interest income in the income statement and does not offset capitalized interest. Capitalized interest = $90,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20-30-10により、借入建設資金の一時運用で得た利息収入は、（米国GAAPの一般的な建設プロジェクトでは）資産化適格利息費用を減額しない。回避可能利息 = 特定借入$1,000,000 x 9% = $90,000。運用益$12,000は別途利息収益として損益計算書に認識され、資産化利息と相殺しない。資産化利息 = $90,000。

questions.subtopic: Capitalization Period - When Interest Capitalization Stops
questions.stem: Under ASC 835-20, the capitalization of interest on a qualifying asset should cease when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The asset is substantially complete and ready for its intended use

choices.label: B
choices.text: Construction expenditures are fully paid in cash

choices.label: C
choices.text: The construction loan is fully repaid

choices.label: D
choices.text: The asset begins generating revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20-25-5, the interest capitalization period ends when the asset is substantially complete and ready for its intended use. This is independent of when the loan is repaid, when expenditures are paid, or when revenue is first generated. If an asset is completed in parts and each part can be used independently, capitalization ceases for each part as it is completed. After the capitalization period ends, interest is expensed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20-25-5により、利息資産化期間は資産が実質的に完成し意図した用途に使用可能になった時点で終了する。これは借入金の返済時期、支出の支払時期、収益発生時期とは独立。資産が部分ごとに完成し各部分が独立して使用できる場合、各部分の完成時に資産化を停止。資産化期間終了後の利息は費用処理する。

questions.stem: Equipment costing $90,000 with no salvage value and a useful life of 6 years is depreciated using the 150% declining balance method. What is the depreciation expense in Year 2?
questions.explanation: The 150% declining balance rate = 1.5 x (1/6) = 0.25 (25%). Year 1 depreciation = $90,000 x 25% = $22,500. Book value end of Year 1 = $90,000 - $22,500 = $67,500. Year 2 depreciation = $67,500 x 25% = $16,875. Under declining balance methods, salvage value is not subtracted from the base, but the asset is not depreciated below salvage value (here $0).
questions.explanationJa: 150%定率法の償却率 = 1.5 x (1/6) = 0.25（25%）。Year 1減価償却 = $90,000 x 25% = $22,500。Year 1末帳簿価額 = $90,000 - $22,500 = $67,500。Year 2減価償却 = $67,500 x 25% = $16,875。定率法では残存価額を償却基礎から差し引かないが、残存価額（ここでは$0）以下には償却しない。

questions.stem: An asset costs $64,000 with a $4,000 salvage value and a 4-year life. The company uses double-declining balance but switches to straight-line in the year that straight-line yields a higher (or equal) expense. What is the depreciation expense in Year 3?
questions.explanation: DDB rate = 2 x (1/4) = 50%. Year 1: $64,000 x 50% = $32,000; BV = $32,000. Year 2: $32,000 x 50% = $16,000; BV = $16,000. Year 3 DDB amount would be $16,000 x 50% = $8,000. Year 3 straight-line on remaining depreciable base over remaining life = ($16,000 - $4,000) / 2 years = $6,000. The company switches when straight-line exceeds DDB; here DDB ($8,000) still exceeds straight-line ($6,000), so DDB applies. Year 3 depreciation = $8,000.

questions.stem: A building costing $1,200,000 with no salvage value and a 40-year straight-line life is placed in service on October 1, Year 1. The company depreciates real property using the full-month (actual months in service) convention. What is the Year 1 depreciation expense?
questions.explanation: Annual straight-line depreciation = $1,200,000 / 40 = $30,000. Under the full-month convention, the asset is depreciated for the actual number of months in service during the year. From October 1 to December 31 = 3 months. Year 1 depreciation = $30,000 x 3/12 = $7,500.
questions.explanationJa: 年間定額償却 = $1,200,000 / 40 = $30,000。実月数法では、年度中の実際の使用月数で償却する。10月1日から12月31日 = 3か月。Year 1減価償却 = $30,000 x 3/12 = $7,500。

questions.subtopic: Impairment Loss Allocation Within Asset Group
questions.stem: An asset group consists of equipment (carrying amount $300,000) and a building (carrying amount $700,000); total carrying amount $1,000,000. The group fails the recoverability test, and the fair value of the group is $760,000. A measured impairment loss of $240,000 is allocated pro rata based on relative carrying amounts. How much of the loss is allocated to the building?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35-28, an impairment loss for an asset group is allocated to the long-lived assets of the group on a pro rata basis using the relative carrying amounts (but no individual asset is reduced below its own fair value). Total impairment = $1,000,000 - $760,000 = $240,000. Building's share = ($700,000 / $1,000,000) x $240,000 = 0.70 x $240,000 = $168,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35-28により、資産グループの減損損失は相対的帳簿価額に基づき長期性資産に比例配分する（ただし個別資産を自身の公正価値以下には減額しない）。減損合計 = $1,000,000 - $760,000 = $240,000。建物への配分 = ($700,000 / $1,000,000) x $240,000 = 0.70 x $240,000 = $168,000。

questions.stem: Under ASC 360-10-35-21, which of the following is NOT, by itself, an indicator that a long-lived asset (held and used) should be tested for impairment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A routine annual increase in the asset's market value

choices.label: B
choices.text: A significant adverse change in the business climate that could affect the asset's value

choices.label: C
choices.text: A current-period operating loss combined with a history of losses associated with the asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: A significant decrease in the market price of the asset

questions.explanation: ASC 360-10-35-21 lists events that indicate the carrying amount of a long-lived asset may not be recoverable, including a significant decrease in market price, a significant adverse change in business climate, and a current-period operating loss combined with a history of losses. An increase in market value is favorable and is NOT an impairment indicator; in fact, U.S. GAAP prohibits writing assets up. Only declines or adverse changes trigger impairment testing.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35-21は長期性資産の帳簿価額が回収不能の可能性を示す事象を列挙し、市場価格の著しい下落、事業環境の著しい不利な変化、当期の営業損失と損失の累積履歴を含む。市場価値の上昇は好材料で減損の兆候ではない。米国GAAPは資産の評価増を禁止する。下落・不利な変化のみが減損テストのトリガーとなる。

questions.stem: Under ASC 360-10-45-9, which of the following conditions must be met for a long-lived asset to be classified as held for sale?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The sale must be completed within the current fiscal year regardless of circumstances

choices.label: B
choices.text: The asset is available for immediate sale in its present condition and the sale is probable within one year

choices.label: C
choices.text: Management has only begun evaluating whether to sell the asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: The asset must already have a signed, binding sale contract

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-45-9, held-for-sale classification requires that: (1) management commits to a plan to sell, (2) the asset is available for immediate sale in its present condition, (3) an active program to locate a buyer has begun, (4) the sale is probable within one year, (5) the asset is actively marketed at a reasonable price, and (6) it is unlikely the plan will change. A signed contract is not required, the sale need not close in the current fiscal year, and merely evaluating a sale is insufficient.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-45-9により、売却目的保有への分類には、(1)経営者が売却計画を確約、(2)現状で即時売却可能、(3)買主探索の積極的プログラムを開始、(4)1年以内の売却が可能性高い、(5)合理的価格で積極的に売却活動、(6)計画変更の可能性が低い、を満たす必要がある。署名済み契約は不要、当期中の売却完了は不要、売却の検討段階だけでは不十分。

questions.subtopic: ARO - Capitalized Asset Retirement Cost Depreciation
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Quarry Co. places an asset in service and recognizes an asset retirement obligation with a present value (fair value) of $80,000. The related plant asset has a cost of $500,000 (excluding the ARO) and a 10-year useful life with no salvage value (straight-line). What is the total Year 1 depreciation expense, including the capitalized asset retirement cost?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 410-20-25-5, the fair value of the ARO ($80,000) is capitalized as part of the carrying amount of the related long-lived asset (asset retirement cost). Total depreciable cost = $500,000 + $80,000 = $580,000. Straight-line depreciation over 10 years = $580,000 / 10 = $58,000 per year. (Accretion expense on the ARO liability is a separate charge and is not part of depreciation.)
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20-25-5により、AROの公正価値（$80,000）は関連長期性資産の帳簿価額の一部（資産除去原価）として資産計上する。減価償却対象原価合計 = $500,000 + $80,000 = $580,000。10年の定額償却 = $580,000 / 10 = $58,000/年。（ARO負債の割増費用は別建ての費用で減価償却には含まれない。）

questions.stem: At the end of an asset's life, Delta Mining's asset retirement obligation has accreted to a carrying amount of $250,000. Delta settles the obligation by paying a third party $235,000 to perform the remediation. What gain or loss does Delta recognize on settlement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 410-20-40-1, when an asset retirement obligation is settled for an amount different from its carrying amount, the entity recognizes a gain or loss on settlement. The ARO liability carrying amount is $250,000 and the actual settlement cost is $235,000. Since the company pays less than the recorded liability, it recognizes a gain = $250,000 - $235,000 = $15,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20-40-1により、資産除去債務を帳簿価額と異なる金額で決済する場合、決済損益を認識する。ARO負債の帳簿価額は$250,000、実際の決済費用は$235,000。計上負債より少なく支払うため、利得 = $250,000 - $235,000 = $15,000を認識。

questions.stem: Acquirer Co. purchases 100% of Target Co. for $9,000,000 cash. The fair values of Target's identifiable assets total $11,000,000 and the fair values of its liabilities total $3,500,000. What amount of goodwill should Acquirer recognize?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $0; recognize a bargain purchase gain

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-30-1, goodwill = consideration transferred - fair value of identifiable net assets acquired. Fair value of identifiable net assets = identifiable assets $11,000,000 - liabilities $3,500,000 = $7,500,000. Goodwill = $9,000,000 - $7,500,000 = $1,500,000. Because consideration exceeds the net assets acquired, goodwill (not a bargain purchase gain) is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-30-30-1により、のれん = 移転対価 - 取得した識別可能純資産の公正価値。識別可能純資産の公正価値 = 識別可能資産$11,000,000 - 負債$3,500,000 = $7,500,000。のれん = $9,000,000 - $7,500,000 = $1,500,000。対価が取得純資産を上回るため、のれん（割安購入益ではない）を認識する。

questions.stem: Buyer Corp. acquires all of Seller Corp.'s net assets for $4,200,000 cash. After a reassessment confirming all identifiable assets and liabilities are correctly measured, the fair value of identifiable assets acquired is $6,000,000 and liabilities assumed is $1,400,000. How should Buyer account for the difference?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $400,000 bargain purchase gain in earnings on the acquisition date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize $400,000 of negative goodwill amortized over 40 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reduce the recorded value of acquired non-current assets by $400,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record $400,000 of goodwill

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-30-25-2 and 805-30-25-4, a bargain purchase occurs when the fair value of identifiable net assets acquired exceeds the consideration transferred. Net assets = $6,000,000 - $1,400,000 = $4,600,000. Consideration = $4,200,000. Bargain purchase amount = $4,600,000 - $4,200,000 = $400,000. After reassessing the measurement, the acquirer recognizes the excess as a gain in earnings on the acquisition date. Negative goodwill amortization is not permitted under current GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-30-25-2および25-4により、取得した識別可能純資産の公正価値が移転対価を超える場合、割安購入が生じる。純資産 = $6,000,000 - $1,400,000 = $4,600,000。対価 = $4,200,000。割安購入額 = $4,600,000 - $4,200,000 = $400,000。測定を再評価した後、取得企業は超過額を取得日に利得として損益に認識する。負ののれんの償却は現行GAAPでは認められない。

questions.stem: After a business combination, an acquirer must assign the $3,000,000 of acquired goodwill to its reporting units. Under ASC 350-20-35-41, the goodwill is assigned to reporting units based on:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The relative book values of the reporting units

choices.label: B
choices.text: The reporting units expected to benefit from the synergies of the combination, even if no other acquired assets are assigned to them

choices.label: C
choices.text: An equal split among all reporting units of the entity

choices.label: D
choices.text: The reporting unit with the highest revenue

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20-35-41 and 35-42, goodwill acquired in a business combination is assigned to one or more reporting units expected to benefit from the synergies of the combination. This is done as of the acquisition date using a methodology consistent with the objective. A reporting unit may be assigned goodwill even if no other assets or liabilities of the acquired entity are assigned to it. Allocation is not based on relative book values, equal splits, or revenue alone.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-20-35-41および35-42により、企業結合で取得したのれんは、結合のシナジーから便益を受けると見込まれる1つ以上の報告単位に配分する。これは取得日時点で目的に整合する方法で行う。被取得企業の他の資産・負債が配分されていない報告単位にものれんを配分しうる。配分は相対簿価、均等分割、収益のみには基づかない。

questions.stem: In a business combination, the acquirer identifies an order or production backlog arising from purchase orders that are cancelable by customers. Under ASC 805-20, how should this backlog be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It cannot be recognized separately because the orders are cancelable, so it is subsumed into goodwill

choices.label: B
choices.text: It is recognized separately from goodwill if it arises from contractual or other legal rights, even if cancelable

choices.label: C
choices.text: It is recognized only if the backlog exceeds 10% of annual revenue

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is expensed immediately as a marketing-related cost

questions.explanation: Under ASC 805-20-55-23, an order or production backlog acquired in a business combination meets the contractual-legal criterion and is recognized as an intangible asset separately from goodwill, even if the purchase or sales orders are cancelable. Meeting the contractual-legal criterion does not depend on whether the contracts are cancelable. There is no quantitative threshold for recognition.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805-20-55-23により、企業結合で取得した受注残・生産残（バックログ）は契約・法律上の基準を満たし、たとえ注文がキャンセル可能であってものれんと区別して無形資産として認識する。契約・法律上の基準の充足はキャンセル可能性に依存しない。認識のための数値基準はない。

questions.stem: Under ASC 350-30-35-6, the method of amortizing a finite-life intangible asset should reflect the pattern in which the asset's economic benefits are consumed. If that pattern cannot be reliably determined, which method is used?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Straight-line method

choices.label: B
choices.text: Double-declining balance method

choices.label: C
choices.text: The asset is not amortized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sum-of-the-years'-digits method

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-30-35-6, the amortization method for a finite-life intangible asset should reflect the pattern in which the economic benefits of the asset are consumed or used up. If that pattern cannot be reliably determined, the straight-line method is used as the default. An intangible with a finite life must be amortized; not amortizing applies only to indefinite-life intangibles.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-30-35-6により、確定耐用年数の無形資産の償却方法は、資産の経済的便益が消費される（使い切られる）パターンを反映すべき。そのパターンを信頼性をもって決定できない場合は、デフォルトとして定額法を使用する。確定耐用年数の無形資産は償却が必須で、非償却は不確定耐用年数の無形資産にのみ適用される。

questions.stem: Vertex Co. acquires a finite-life intangible asset for $500,000 with a 10-year useful life. A third party has committed to purchase the intangible at the end of its useful life for $80,000. What is the annual amortization expense?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-30-35-8, the amount of a finite-life intangible asset to be amortized is its cost less its residual value. Residual value is assumed to be zero unless a third party has committed to purchase the asset at the end of its useful life or it can be determined from an active market. Here a third-party commitment exists for $80,000. Amortizable base = $500,000 - $80,000 = $420,000. Annual amortization = $420,000 / 10 = $42,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-30-35-8により、確定耐用年数の無形資産の償却対象額は取得原価から残存価額を控除した額。残存価額は、第三者が耐用年数末に資産を購入する約束をしている場合または活発な市場から決定できる場合を除き、ゼロと仮定する。ここでは$80,000の第三者購入約束がある。償却基礎 = $500,000 - $80,000 = $420,000。年間償却 = $420,000 / 10 = $42,000。

questions.stem: Vista Corp. holds a finite-life customer-list intangible with a carrying amount of $360,000. Triggered by a major customer loss, Vista estimates undiscounted future cash flows of $330,000 and a fair value of $290,000. What impairment loss should Vista recognize?
questions.explanation: Finite-life intangible assets are tested for impairment using the same two-step model as other long-lived assets under ASC 360-10-35. Step 1 (recoverability): carrying amount $360,000 > undiscounted cash flows $330,000, so the asset is impaired. Step 2 (measurement): impairment loss = carrying amount - fair value = $360,000 - $290,000 = $70,000.
questions.explanationJa: 確定耐用年数の無形資産は、他の長期性資産と同じくASC 360-10-35の2段階モデルで減損テストする。ステップ1（回収可能性）：帳簿価額$360,000 > 割引前CF$330,000のため減損あり。ステップ2（測定）：減損損失 = 帳簿価額 - 公正価値 = $360,000 - $290,000 = $70,000。

questions.stem: Under ASC 985-20, costs of developing software to be sold, leased, or otherwise marketed are capitalized:

choices.label: A
choices.text: From the point technological feasibility is established until the product is available for general release to customers

choices.label: B
choices.text: From the beginning of the project until completion

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only after the product is released to customers

choices.label: D
choices.text: Never; all such costs are expensed as research and development

questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20-25-1 through 25-3, costs incurred to develop software for sale are charged to research and development expense until technological feasibility is established. From the point technological feasibility is established until the product is available for general release to customers, production costs are capitalized. After general release, capitalized costs are amortized, and subsequent maintenance and customer support costs are expensed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 985-20-25-1〜25-3により、販売用ソフトウェアの開発費は、技術的実現可能性が確立するまでは研究開発費として費用処理する。技術的実現可能性確立時点から製品が顧客に一般リリース可能になるまでの制作費は資産計上する。一般リリース後は資産計上額を償却し、その後の保守・顧客サポート費は費用処理する。

questions.subtopic: Software for Sale - Amortization Greater of Two Methods
questions.stem: TechSoft capitalized $900,000 of software development costs for a product to be sold. In Year 1, the product generates $600,000 of current revenue, and total estimated revenue (current and future) is $3,000,000. The remaining economic life is 4 years (straight-line). Under ASC 985-20-35, what is the Year 1 amortization expense?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20-35-1, annual amortization of capitalized software-for-sale costs is the GREATER of: (1) the revenue method: (current revenue / total estimated revenue) x capitalized cost = ($600,000 / $3,000,000) x $900,000 = $180,000; or (2) the straight-line method over remaining economic life = $900,000 / 4 = $225,000. The greater amount is $225,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 985-20-35-1により、販売用資産化ソフトウェアの年間償却は次のいずれか大きい方：(1)収益法：（当期収益 / 見積総収益）x 資産化原価 = ($600,000 / $3,000,000) x $900,000 = $180,000、または(2)残存経済耐用年数の定額法 = $900,000 / 4 = $225,000。大きい方は$225,000。

questions.subtopic: Internal-Use Software - Capitalizable vs Expensed Costs
questions.stem: During the application development stage of an internal-use software project, NovaCorp incurs: $200,000 for coding and configuration, $40,000 for data conversion from the old system, and $25,000 for training employees on the new system. Under ASC 350-40, what amount should be capitalized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-40-25-2 and 350-40-25-3, only certain application development stage costs are capitalized: coding, software configuration, interface development, testing, and installation. Data conversion costs ($40,000) and training costs ($25,000) are expensed as incurred, even if they occur during the application development stage. Therefore, only the $200,000 coding and configuration is capitalized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-40-25-2および25-3により、アプリケーション開発段階の特定費用のみ資産計上する：コーディング、構成設定、インターフェース開発、テスト、インストール。データ変換費用（$40,000）と研修費用（$25,000）は、開発段階に発生しても費用処理する。したがって、コーディング・構成の$200,000のみ資産計上。

questions.subtopic: Long-Lived Asset Disposal - Discontinued Operations Distinction
questions.stem: Riverside Corp. sells a single piece of manufacturing equipment that does not represent a strategic shift in operations. The equipment is sold at a loss. Under ASC 360-10 and ASC 205-20, how should the loss be presented?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a component of income from continuing operations

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a discontinued operation, net of tax, below continuing operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a direct adjustment to retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: As an item of other comprehensive income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 205-20-45-1B, a disposal is reported as a discontinued operation only if it represents a strategic shift that has (or will have) a major effect on the entity's operations and financial results. Selling a single piece of equipment that is not a strategic shift does not qualify as a discontinued operation. The gain or loss on disposal is reported within income from continuing operations, not net of tax below the line.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 205-20-45-1Bにより、処分が非継続事業として報告されるのは、企業の事業・財務結果に重大な影響を与える（または与える見込みの）戦略的転換を表す場合のみ。戦略的転換に当たらない単一設備の売却は非継続事業に該当しない。処分損益は、税引後で区分線下に表示せず、継続事業からの利益の中で報告する。

questions.stem: A fire destroys Maple Corp.'s warehouse, which had a cost of $700,000 and accumulated depreciation of $420,000. Maple receives $350,000 from its insurance company. What gain or loss should Maple recognize on the involuntary conversion?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 610-30 and ASC 360-10-40, an involuntary conversion (e.g., destruction by fire with insurance proceeds) is accounted for by removing the asset's carrying amount and recognizing a gain or loss for the difference between the insurance proceeds and the carrying amount. Carrying amount = $700,000 - $420,000 = $280,000. Insurance proceeds = $350,000. Gain = $350,000 - $280,000 = $70,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 610-30およびASC 360-10-40により、非自発的転換（火災による滅失と保険金受取等）は、資産の帳簿価額を除却し、保険金と帳簿価額の差額を損益として認識する。帳簿価額 = $700,000 - $420,000 = $280,000。保険金 = $350,000。利得 = $350,000 - $280,000 = $70,000。

questions.subtopic: Land Held for Investment vs Operations
questions.stem: Horizon Corp. owns two parcels of land: Parcel 1 ($300,000) is the site of its operating headquarters, and Parcel 2 ($450,000) is held for long-term capital appreciation with no current use. How should each parcel be classified and is either depreciated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Parcel 1 as PP&E and Parcel 2 as a long-term investment; neither is depreciated

choices.label: B
choices.text: Both as PP&E; both depreciated over 40 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: Both as long-term investments; neither depreciated

choices.label: D
choices.text: Parcel 1 as PP&E (depreciated) and Parcel 2 as an investment (not depreciated)

questions.explanation: Land used in operations (Parcel 1) is classified as PP&E. Land held for long-term appreciation and not used in operations (Parcel 2) is classified as a long-term investment, not PP&E. Land is not depreciated regardless of classification because it has an indefinite useful life. Therefore, neither parcel is depreciated; only the classification differs based on intended use.
questions.explanationJa: 営業に使用される土地（Parcel 1）はPP&Eに分類。長期的値上がり目的で営業に使用しない土地（Parcel 2）は長期投資に分類し、PP&Eではない。土地は耐用年数が無期限のため、分類にかかわらず減価償却しない。したがってどちらも減価償却せず、使用目的に基づき分類のみ異なる。

questions.stem: Pinnacle Corp. purchases land for $2,000,000 and immediately begins constructing a building on it. During the construction period, weighted-average accumulated expenditures (including the land) are $2,500,000. The applicable capitalization rate is 7%. Regarding interest on the land cost, which statement is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interest capitalized on the land expenditures is added to the cost of the building (not the land), because the land is being developed for the building

choices.label: B
choices.text: No interest may be capitalized on land expenditures under any circumstances

choices.label: C
choices.text: Interest on land is always capitalized to the land account

choices.label: D
choices.text: Interest on land must be expensed because land is not a depreciable asset

questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20-30-9, if land is purchased and developed for a particular use (such as constructing a building), the land qualifies as an asset under development and is included in the qualifying asset base. The interest cost capitalized on expenditures for the land is added to the cost of the structure (building) being constructed, not to the land account, because the land is being developed for the structure. If land is developed for sale as lots, the interest would be capitalized as part of the land.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20-30-9により、特定の用途（建物の建設等）のために土地を取得・開発する場合、その土地は開発中資産として適格資産の基礎に含まれる。土地の支出に対して資産化される利息費用は、土地が建物のために開発されているため、土地勘定ではなく建設中の建物の原価に加える。土地を区画分譲用に開発する場合は、利息は土地の一部として資産化される。

questions.subtopic: Spare Parts and Standby Equipment Classification
questions.stem: Industrial Co. purchases a major spare component (a backup turbine) for $400,000 to be used only if its primary turbine fails. The backup turbine has a useful life of 12 years. How should Industrial account for this major spare part?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize as PP&E and depreciate over its useful life

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record as inventory and expense when placed into service

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense immediately as a repairs and maintenance cost

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record as a prepaid expense and amortize over 12 months

questions.explanation: Major spare parts and standby (backup) equipment that an entity expects to use for more than one period qualify as property, plant, and equipment under ASC 360-10. The backup turbine has a 12-year useful life and provides standby service capacity, so it is capitalized as PP&E and depreciated over its useful life. Minor spare parts that are consumed quickly are typically carried as inventory, but a major standby component is a long-lived asset.
questions.explanationJa: 1期間を超えて使用する見込みの主要なスペアパーツや待機（バックアップ）設備は、ASC 360-10の有形固定資産に該当する。バックアップタービンは耐用年数12年で待機サービス能力を提供するため、PP&Eとして資産計上し耐用年数にわたり減価償却する。短期間で消費される少額スペアパーツは通常棚卸資産として計上するが、主要な待機構成品は長期性資産。

questions.stem: Under ASC 835-20-15-5, an investor accounting for an investment under the equity method may capitalize interest on the investment when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The investee has activities in progress necessary to commence its planned principal operations and is using funds to acquire qualifying assets for those operations

choices.label: B
choices.text: The investee pays regular dividends

choices.label: C
choices.text: The investment is classified as available-for-sale

choices.label: D
choices.text: The investor owns less than 20% of the investee

questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20-15-5(c), investments accounted for by the equity method qualify for interest capitalization while the investee has activities in progress necessary to commence its planned principal operations, provided the investee's activities include the use of funds to acquire qualifying assets for its operations. Once the investee begins its principal operations, the qualifying status ends. Dividend-paying status, AFS classification, and ownership below 20% (which generally precludes equity-method accounting) are not the criteria.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20-15-5(c)により、持分法で会計処理する投資は、被投資企業が計画した主要な事業を開始するために必要な活動を進行中で、かつその活動が事業用の適格資産取得のための資金使用を含む場合、利息資産化の対象となる。被投資企業が主要事業を開始すると適格性は終了する。配当支払の有無、AFS分類、20%未満の保有（一般に持分法適用を排除）は基準ではない。

questions.subtopic: Depreciation - Fractional Year SYD Across Calendar Years
questions.stem: An asset costing $110,000 with a $10,000 salvage value and a 5-year life is purchased on July 1, Year 1. The company uses sum-of-the-years'-digits depreciation and allocates the first year over the partial period. What is the depreciation expense for the calendar year ended December 31, Year 2?
questions.explanation: Depreciable base = $110,000 - $10,000 = $100,000. SYD denominator = 5+4+3+2+1 = 15. First full SYD year (5/15) = $100,000 x 5/15 = $33,333; second full SYD year (4/15) = $100,000 x 4/15 = $26,667. Because the asset was acquired July 1, each SYD 'year' straddles two calendar years. Calendar Year 2 = last half of SYD-Year-1 + first half of SYD-Year-2 = ($33,333 x 1/2) + ($26,667 x 1/2) = $16,667 + $13,333 = $30,000.

questions.subtopic: Asset Exchange - Loss Recognition When Lacking Commercial Substance
questions.stem: Coastal Co. exchanges a machine (cost $90,000, accumulated depreciation $50,000, fair value $32,000) for a similar machine. The exchange lacks commercial substance and no cash is exchanged. What gain or loss, if any, should Coastal recognize, and at what amount is the new machine recorded?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $8,000 loss; new machine recorded at $32,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: No loss; new machine recorded at $40,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: $8,000 gain; new machine recorded at $40,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: $8,000 loss; new machine recorded at $40,000

questions.explanation: When an exchange lacks commercial substance, gains are deferred but losses are recognized immediately if the fair value of the asset given up is less than its book value (an indicated impairment). Book value of machine given = $90,000 - $50,000 = $40,000. Fair value = $32,000, which is below book value, so an $8,000 loss ($40,000 - $32,000) is recognized. The new machine is recorded at the fair value of the asset given up = $32,000 (book value less the recognized loss).
questions.explanationJa: 交換に商業的実質がない場合、利得は繰り延べるが、引渡資産の公正価値が帳簿価額を下回る（減損の兆候）ときは損失を即時認識する。引渡機械の帳簿価額 = $90,000 - $50,000 = $40,000。公正価値 = $32,000で帳簿価額を下回るため、$8,000の損失（$40,000 - $32,000）を認識。新機械は引渡資産の公正価値 = $32,000（帳簿価額から認識損失を控除した額）で計上する。

questions.subtopic: Government Grant Related to Asset (Deferred Income Approach)
questions.stem: Solaris Inc. receives a $300,000 government grant to help fund the purchase of equipment costing $1,000,000 with a 10-year life. Solaris elects the deferred income method, recognizing the grant as deferred income and amortizing it over the equipment's life. What is the net effect on Year 1 pre-tax income from depreciation and grant amortization combined?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $70,000 net expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: $100,000 net expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: $30,000 net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: $130,000 net expense

questions.explanation: For-profit entities commonly account for asset-related government grants by analogy to IAS 20. Under the deferred income method, the grant is recorded as deferred income and amortized to income over the asset's life, while the asset is depreciated on its gross cost. Annual depreciation = $1,000,000 / 10 = $100,000 (expense). Annual grant amortization = $300,000 / 10 = $30,000 (income). Net effect on pre-tax income = $100,000 expense - $30,000 income = $70,000 net expense.
questions.explanationJa: 営利企業は資産関連の政府助成金をIAS 20に類推して会計処理することが多い。繰延収益法では、助成金を繰延収益として計上し資産の耐用年数にわたり収益に振り替え、資産は総額原価で減価償却する。年間減価償却 = $1,000,000 / 10 = $100,000（費用）。年間助成金振替 = $300,000 / 10 = $30,000（収益）。税引前利益への純影響 = 費用$100,000 - 収益$30,000 = 純費用$70,000。

questions.stem: Vantage Manufacturing pays $60,000 to relocate and rearrange its existing production machinery to improve workflow efficiency. The rearrangement is expected to provide benefits over future periods. Under U.S. GAAP, how should these rearrangement costs be treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize and amortize over the period expected to benefit if future benefits are probable and measurable; otherwise expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always expense immediately as a period cost with no exceptions

choices.label: C
choices.text: Add to the cost of the land on which the factory sits

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize as a reduction of accumulated depreciation

questions.explanation: Rearrangement, reinstallation, and relocation costs that provide probable future economic benefits beyond the current period may be capitalized as a deferred cost and amortized over the periods expected to benefit. If the future benefit is not probable or cannot be reliably measured, the costs are expensed as incurred. They are not added to land, and they are not netted against accumulated depreciation.
questions.explanationJa: 再配置・再据付・移設費用で、当期を超える将来の経済的便益が見込まれるものは、繰延費用として資産計上し便益が見込まれる期間にわたり償却できる。将来便益が見込めない、または信頼性をもって測定できない場合は発生時に費用処理する。これらは土地原価に加算せず、減価償却累計額と相殺もしない。

questions.stem: Apex Group performs its annual goodwill impairment test on December 31. On August 15, a significant adverse legal ruling causes the fair value of one reporting unit to drop sharply. Under ASC 350-20, what is Apex required to do?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Test that reporting unit's goodwill for impairment between annual tests because a triggering event occurred

choices.label: B
choices.text: Take no interim action and rely solely on the next scheduled annual test on December 31

choices.label: C
choices.text: Immediately write off all goodwill of the entity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortize the goodwill prospectively starting August 15

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20-35-30, goodwill of a reporting unit must be tested for impairment between annual tests if an event occurs or circumstances change that would more likely than not reduce the fair value of the reporting unit below its carrying amount. A significant adverse legal ruling is such a triggering event, so Apex must perform an interim impairment test for the affected reporting unit instead of relying only on the scheduled annual test. Goodwill is not amortized (except under the private company alternative), and a triggering event does not automatically result in writing off all goodwill.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-20-35-30により、報告単位の公正価値が帳簿価額を下回る可能性が高くなる事象・状況変化が生じた場合、年次テストの間でものれんの減損テストを行わなければならない。重大な不利な法的判決はそのトリガーイベントであり、Apexは予定された年次テストを待たず、影響を受ける報告単位について中間減損テストを実施しなければならない。のれんは（非公開会社の代替的処理を除き）償却せず、トリガーイベントが自動的に全のれんの償却を意味するわけではない。

questions.stem: Harlow Corp. acquired a tract of land for $400,000 to be used as a future plant site. Harlow also paid $12,000 in closing costs, assumed $8,000 of delinquent property taxes owed by the seller, paid $15,000 to raze an old shed on the property (receiving $3,000 in salvage proceeds), and paid a $10,000 special assessment to the city for sewer connections. At what amount should Harlow record the land?
questions.explanation: The cost of land includes all expenditures necessary to acquire it and prepare it for its intended use: purchase price $400,000 + closing costs $12,000 + delinquent taxes assumed $8,000 + razing costs $15,000 − salvage proceeds $3,000 + special assessment $10,000 = $442,000. Demolition costs (net of salvage) are part of land when the land was acquired with the intent to remove the existing structure, and special assessments for permanent improvements maintained by the government are also charged to land. $432,000 results from omitting the net razing cost of $12,000; $445,000 results from failing to offset the $3,000 salvage proceeds; $430,000 results from excluding both the razing cost and the salvage offset.
questions.explanationJa: 土地の取得原価には、取得と使用準備に直接必要なすべての支出を含めます（ASC 360-10-30）。購入価格$400,000 + 決済費用$12,000 + 売主の滞納固定資産税の引受$8,000 + 旧建物の取壊費用$15,000 − 廃材売却収入$3,000 + 下水道特別賦課金$10,000 = $442,000。土地を建物撤去目的で取得した場合、取壊費用（純額）は土地原価に算入されます。また政府が維持する恒久的改良に対する特別賦課金も土地原価です。$432,000は取壊純費用$12,000の脱漏、$445,000は廃材収入$3,000の控除漏れ、$430,000は両方を除外した誤りです。

questions.stem: Kestrel Inc. purchased an existing office building for $850,000 and paid $5,000 for title insurance. Before occupying the building, Kestrel spent $95,000 on renovations necessary to prepare the building for its intended use. During the renovation period, vandals broke windows, and Kestrel paid $10,000 to repair the vandalism damage. At what amount should Kestrel capitalize the building?
questions.explanation: The cost of a purchased building includes the purchase price plus all costs necessary to bring it to the condition for its intended use: $850,000 + $5,000 title insurance + $95,000 renovation = $950,000. Repairs of vandalism damage are not a necessary cost of readying the asset—they are neither normal nor necessary, so the $10,000 is expensed as incurred. $960,000 incorrectly capitalizes the vandalism repair; $945,000 incorrectly omits the title insurance; $860,000 incorrectly excludes the renovation costs that were necessary for the intended use.
questions.explanationJa: 購入した建物の取得原価は、購入価格に意図した用途で使用可能な状態にするために必要なすべての費用を加えた額です：$850,000 + 権原保険$5,000 + 使用前の改修費$95,000 = $950,000。改修期間中の破壊行為（vandalism）による修繕$10,000は、資産を使用可能にするために「正常かつ必要」な支出ではないため発生時に費用処理します。$960,000は破壊行為修繕費の誤資産化、$945,000は権原保険の脱漏、$860,000は必要な改修費の除外による誤りです。

questions.stem: Pinewood Manufacturing self-constructs a piece of production equipment for its own use. Which of the following costs should be excluded from the capitalized cost of the self-constructed equipment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Direct labor of employees working on the construction

choices.label: B
choices.text: Variable manufacturing overhead directly attributable to the construction

choices.label: C
choices.text: Interest cost incurred during the construction period on borrowings, up to avoidable interest

choices.label: D
choices.text: Abnormal amounts of wasted material and labor caused by construction inefficiencies

questions.explanation: The cost of a self-constructed asset includes direct materials, direct labor, and overhead directly attributable to the construction, plus capitalized interest during the construction period under ASC 835-20. However, abnormal amounts of wasted material, labor, or other resources resulting from inefficiencies do not add value to the asset and are charged to expense in the period incurred. Direct labor, directly attributable variable overhead, and avoidable interest during construction are all properly includable in the asset's cost.
questions.explanationJa: 自家建設資産の取得原価には、直接材料費、直接労務費、建設に直接帰属する製造間接費、および建設期間中の資本化利子（ASC 835-20の回避可能利子の範囲内）が含まれます。一方、建設の非効率に起因する異常な仕損（材料・労務の浪費）は資産の価値を高めないため、発生した期の費用として処理します。直接労務費、直接帰属する変動製造間接費、建設期間中の回避可能利子はいずれも適切に資産計上できる費用です。

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest - Specific and General Borrowings
questions.stem: Tidewater Corp. is constructing a new distribution center. Weighted-average accumulated expenditures for the year were $1,500,000. Tidewater has a construction loan of $900,000 at 6% specifically obtained for the project, and other general debt outstanding all year of $3,000,000 at 9% (actual interest of $270,000). What amount of interest should Tidewater capitalize for the year?
questions.explanation: Avoidable interest is computed by applying the specific borrowing rate to expenditures up to the specific borrowing, and the weighted-average rate on other borrowings to the excess: ($900,000 × 6%) + (($1,500,000 − $900,000) × 9%) = $54,000 + $54,000 = $108,000. Total actual interest ($54,000 + $270,000 = $324,000) exceeds avoidable interest, so $108,000 is capitalized. $135,000 results from applying 9% to the full $1,500,000; $90,000 results from applying 6% to the full $1,500,000; $54,000 results from capitalizing only the interest on the specific construction loan and ignoring the excess expenditures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20の回避可能利子は、加重平均累積支出のうち特定借入額までは特定借入利率、超過部分には他の借入の加重平均利率を適用して計算します：($900,000 × 6%) + (($1,500,000 − $900,000) × 9%) = $54,000 + $54,000 = $108,000。実際利子総額$324,000が回避可能利子を上回るため、資本化額は$108,000です。$135,000は全額に9%を適用した誤り、$90,000は全額に6%を適用した誤り、$54,000は特定借入分のみ資本化し超過支出分を無視した誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Basket Purchase - Relative Fair Value Allocation
questions.stem: Sycamore Co. acquired land, a building, and machinery from a bankrupt competitor for a single lump-sum payment of $2,700,000. Independent appraisals indicate fair values of $1,000,000 for the land, $1,800,000 for the building, and $200,000 for the machinery. At what amount should Sycamore record the building?
questions.explanation: In a lump-sum (basket) purchase, the total cost is allocated to the individual assets based on their relative fair values. Total appraised value = $1,000,000 + $1,800,000 + $200,000 = $3,000,000. The building receives $2,700,000 × ($1,800,000 / $3,000,000) = $1,620,000. $1,800,000 incorrectly records the building at its full appraised value, which would overstate total assets above the actual cost paid; $1,500,000 results from an even allocation error; $900,000 applies the building's ratio to only a portion of the price.
questions.explanationJa: 一括購入では、支払総額を各資産の相対的公正価値の比率で配分します。鑑定評価額合計 = $1,000,000 + $1,800,000 + $200,000 = $3,000,000。建物への配分額 = $2,700,000 × ($1,800,000 / $3,000,000) = $1,620,000。鑑定評価額$1,800,000をそのまま計上すると実際の支払額を超えて資産が過大計上されます。$1,500,000や$900,000は配分比率の適用誤りによる値です。

questions.subtopic: Exchange with Commercial Substance - Boot Received
questions.stem: Ridgeline Corp. exchanged a machine (original cost $80,000, accumulated depreciation $30,000, fair value $62,000) for newer equipment with a fair value of $52,000 plus $10,000 in cash. The exchange has commercial substance. What amount of gain should Ridgeline recognize on the exchange?
questions.explanation: When a nonmonetary exchange has commercial substance, the transaction is measured at fair value and the full gain or loss is recognized immediately. The gain equals the fair value of the asset given up minus its carrying amount: $62,000 − ($80,000 − $30,000) = $62,000 − $50,000 = $12,000. The new equipment is recorded at its fair value of $52,000. $0 would apply only if the exchange lacked commercial substance and no boot were received; $10,000 confuses the cash received with the gain; $1,935 results from incorrectly applying the proportional gain recognition formula ($12,000 × $10,000/$62,000), which applies only when the exchange lacks commercial substance.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質（commercial substance）を有する非貨幣性資産の交換では、取引を公正価値で測定し、損益の全額を即時認識します（ASC 845-10-30）。利得 = 引渡資産の公正価値$62,000 − 帳簿価額($80,000 − $30,000 = $50,000) = $12,000。受入設備は公正価値$52,000で計上します。$0は商業的実質を欠きbootを受け取らない場合の処理、$10,000は受取現金と利得の混同、$1,935は商業的実質を欠く場合にのみ適用される比例認識（$12,000 × $10,000/$62,000）の誤適用です。

questions.subtopic: Exchange Lacking Commercial Substance - Partial Gain on Boot Received
questions.stem: Meridian Co. exchanged equipment with a carrying amount of $40,000 and a fair value of $48,000 for a similar asset with a fair value of $40,000 plus $8,000 in cash. The exchange lacks commercial substance. What amount of gain should Meridian recognize?
questions.explanation: When an exchange lacks commercial substance and boot (monetary consideration) of less than 25% of the total fair value is received, a proportional amount of the gain is recognized: total gain × (boot received / total consideration received). Total gain = $48,000 − $40,000 = $8,000. Boot ratio = $8,000 / $48,000 = 1/6. Recognized gain = $8,000 × 1/6 = $1,333 (rounded). The boot of $8,000 is 16.7% of the $48,000 total, below the 25% threshold, so full fair value accounting does not apply. $8,000 would be the full gain, recognized only if the exchange had commercial substance or boot were 25% or more; $0 ignores the partial recognition required when boot is received; $6,667 is the deferred portion of the gain rather than the recognized portion.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質を欠く交換で総対価の25%未満のboot（現金）を受け取った場合、利得の一部を比例的に認識します：総利得 × (受取boot ÷ 受取対価総額)。総利得 = $48,000 − $40,000 = $8,000。boot比率 = $8,000 ÷ $48,000 = 1/6。認識利得 = $8,000 × 1/6 ≒ $1,333。bootは総額の16.7%で25%基準未満のため、全額公正価値処理は適用されません。$8,000は商業的実質がある場合またはbootが25%以上の場合の全額認識、$0はboot受領時に必要な部分認識の無視、$6,667は認識額ではなく繰延部分です。

questions.subtopic: Exchange Lacking Commercial Substance - Boot Paid
questions.stem: Larkspur Inc. exchanged a delivery van (original cost $60,000, accumulated depreciation $25,000, fair value $42,000) for a similar van, paying $5,000 cash in addition. The exchange lacks commercial substance. At what amount should Larkspur record the van received?
questions.explanation: When an exchange lacks commercial substance and boot is paid, no gain is recognized; the asset received is recorded at the carrying amount of the asset surrendered plus the cash paid: ($60,000 − $25,000) + $5,000 = $35,000 + $5,000 = $40,000. The implied gain of $7,000 ($42,000 fair value − $35,000 carrying amount) is deferred and embedded in the lower carrying amount of the new asset. $47,000 ($42,000 + $5,000) applies fair value accounting, which is appropriate only when the exchange has commercial substance; $42,000 is the fair value of the asset given up, not the recorded amount; $35,000 omits the cash paid.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質を欠く交換でbootを支払った場合、利得は認識せず、受入資産は引渡資産の帳簿価額に支払現金を加えた額で計上します：($60,000 − $25,000) + $5,000 = $40,000。含み益$7,000（公正価値$42,000 − 帳簿価額$35,000）は繰り延べられ、新資産の低い計上額に織り込まれます。$47,000は商業的実質がある場合の公正価値ベース（$42,000 + $5,000）、$42,000は引渡資産の公正価値そのもの、$35,000は支払現金の加算漏れです。

questions.stem: Crestline Corp. purchased a stamping machine for $310,000 with an estimated salvage value of $10,000. The machine is expected to operate for a total of 60,000 machine hours over its life. During Year 1, the machine operated for 12,000 hours. What is Year 1 depreciation expense under the units-of-production method?
questions.explanation: Under the units-of-production method, the depreciation rate per unit of activity is (cost − salvage value) / total estimated activity: ($310,000 − $10,000) / 60,000 hours = $5.00 per hour. Year 1 depreciation = $5.00 × 12,000 hours = $60,000. $62,000 results from failing to deduct the salvage value ($310,000 / 60,000 × 12,000); $50,000 and $75,000 result from using incorrect hour fractions of the depreciable base.
questions.explanationJa: 生産高比例法では、単位当たり償却率 = (取得原価 − 残存価額) ÷ 総見積稼働量で計算します：($310,000 − $10,000) ÷ 60,000時間 = $5.00/時間。Year 1の減価償却費 = $5.00 × 12,000時間 = $60,000。$62,000は残存価額の控除漏れ（$310,000 ÷ 60,000 × 12,000）、$50,000と$75,000は稼働割合の適用誤りによる値です。

questions.stem: Bayfield Inc. purchased equipment on January 1, Year 1 for $200,000. The equipment has an estimated salvage value of $20,000 and a useful life of 8 years. Using the double-declining balance method, what is depreciation expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: The double-declining balance rate is 2 / 8 years = 25%, applied to the beginning carrying amount without deducting salvage value. Year 1: $200,000 × 25% = $50,000. Year 2: ($200,000 − $50,000) × 25% = $150,000 × 25% = $37,500. Salvage value is ignored in the annual computation but serves as a floor below which the carrying amount cannot fall. $45,000 incorrectly deducts salvage from the base ($180,000 × 25%); $50,000 is the Year 1 amount; $33,750 results from compounding the salvage-adjusted base into the second year ($135,000 × 25%).
questions.explanationJa: 定率法（200%定率法）の償却率は 2 ÷ 8年 = 25%で、残存価額を控除しない期首帳簿価額に適用します。Year 1: $200,000 × 25% = $50,000。Year 2: ($200,000 − $50,000) × 25% = $37,500。残存価額は年次計算では無視されますが、帳簿価額がそれを下回らないための下限として機能します。$45,000は残存価額を控除した基礎額の誤り（$180,000 × 25%）、$50,000はYear 1の金額、$33,750は残存価額控除後の基礎額を2年目に引き継いだ誤りです。

questions.stem: Oakhurst Co. purchased machinery on January 1, Year 1 for $150,000 with a salvage value of $30,000 and a useful life of 5 years. Using the sum-of-the-years'-digits method, what is depreciation expense for Year 3?
questions.explanation: The sum of the years' digits for a 5-year life is 5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 15. The depreciable base is $150,000 − $30,000 = $120,000. Year 3 uses the fraction 3/15 (remaining life at the beginning of Year 3 over the sum): $120,000 × 3/15 = $24,000. $40,000 is the Year 1 amount ($120,000 × 5/15); $32,000 is the Year 2 amount ($120,000 × 4/15); $16,000 is the Year 4 amount ($120,000 × 2/15).

questions.subtopic: Change in Depreciation Estimate - Life and Salvage Revised
questions.stem: Glenview Corp. purchased equipment on January 1, Year 1 for $360,000 with no salvage value and an 8-year useful life, depreciated straight-line. At the beginning of Year 4, Glenview determines that the equipment will last only 5 more years from that date and will have a salvage value of $25,000. What is depreciation expense for Year 4?
questions.explanation: A change in estimated useful life or salvage value is a change in accounting estimate, accounted for prospectively. Accumulated depreciation through Year 3 = ($360,000 / 8) × 3 = $135,000, leaving a carrying amount of $225,000. The remaining depreciable amount is $225,000 − $25,000 new salvage = $200,000, spread over the 5 remaining years: $200,000 / 5 = $40,000. $45,000 ignores the new salvage value ($225,000 / 5); $41,875 spreads the revised depreciable cost over the original 8-year life (($360,000 − $25,000) / 8); $25,000 incorrectly divides the remaining base by the original life.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数・残存価額の見直しは会計上の見積りの変更であり、将来に向かって（prospectively）処理します（ASC 250-10-45-17）。Year 3末までの減価償却累計額 = ($360,000 ÷ 8) × 3 = $135,000、帳簿価額 = $225,000。残存償却基礎額 = $225,000 − 新残存価額$25,000 = $200,000を残り5年で配分：$200,000 ÷ 5 = $40,000。$45,000は新残存価額の無視、$41,875は改訂後の基礎額を当初8年で割った誤り、$25,000は残存基礎額を当初年数で割った誤りです。

questions.stem: Which of the following expenditures related to existing machinery should be expensed as incurred?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Replacement of a major motor that extends the machine's useful life by five years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Routine annual maintenance and lubrication performed under a service contract

choices.label: C
choices.text: Installation of an attachment that increases the machine's hourly output capacity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Modification of the machine required to produce a newly designed product line

questions.explanation: Routine maintenance merely keeps an asset in its existing operating condition and provides no benefit beyond the current period, so it is expensed as incurred. Expenditures that extend useful life (the motor replacement), increase capacity (the output attachment), or adapt the asset to a new use (the product-line modification) provide future economic benefits and are capitalized, either as an addition to the asset's cost or, for life-extending expenditures, sometimes as a debit to accumulated depreciation.
questions.explanationJa: 経常的な保守・注油は資産を現状の稼働状態に維持するだけで当期を超える便益をもたらさないため、発生時に費用処理します。一方、耐用年数を延長する支出（モーター交換）、生産能力を増加させる支出（能力増強アタッチメント）、新用途への適応支出（新製品ライン向け改造）は将来の経済的便益をもたらすため資本的支出として資産計上します（耐用年数延長型は減価償却累計額の借方処理も実務上認められます）。

questions.subtopic: Impairment Loss Measurement - Held and Used
questions.stem: Driftwood Corp. has manufacturing equipment with a carrying amount of $800,000. Due to a significant adverse change in the business climate, Driftwood tests the equipment for impairment. The sum of undiscounted expected future cash flows is $750,000, the present value of those cash flows is $640,000, and the equipment's fair value determined from market prices for similar assets is $620,000. What impairment loss should Driftwood recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360, the recoverability test compares the carrying amount ($800,000) with undiscounted future cash flows ($750,000); because the carrying amount is not recoverable, an impairment loss is measured as the excess of carrying amount over fair value: $800,000 − $620,000 = $180,000. When a market-based fair value is available, it is used rather than the present value of entity-specific cash flows. $50,000 incorrectly measures the loss using undiscounted cash flows; $160,000 uses the present value of cash flows ($640,000) even though an observable fair value exists; $0 would apply only if undiscounted cash flows had exceeded the carrying amount.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360の保有使用資産の減損では、第1段階で帳簿価額$800,000と割引前将来キャッシュ・フロー$750,000を比較し、回収不能と判定されたら第2段階で帳簿価額と公正価値の差額を減損損失として測定します：$800,000 − $620,000 = $180,000。市場ベースの公正価値が入手可能な場合は、企業固有キャッシュ・フローの現在価値ではなくそれを使用します。$50,000は割引前キャッシュ・フローとの差額という測定誤り、$160,000は公正価値があるのに現在価値$640,000を使った誤り、$0は回収可能性テストを通過した場合の処理です。

questions.stem: Stonebridge Co. owns a production facility with a carrying amount of $900,000. An impairment indicator exists. The undiscounted expected future cash flows from the facility total $950,000, and the facility's fair value is $700,000. What impairment loss, if any, should Stonebridge recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360, an impairment loss for a held-and-used asset is recognized only if the carrying amount is not recoverable—that is, only if it exceeds the sum of undiscounted future cash flows. Here, undiscounted cash flows of $950,000 exceed the carrying amount of $900,000, so the asset passes the recoverability test and no impairment loss is recognized, even though fair value ($700,000) is below carrying amount. $200,000 (carrying amount minus fair value) would be recorded only if the recoverability test had failed; $250,000 and $50,000 are differences between other figure pairs that have no significance once the test is passed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360では、保有使用資産の減損損失は帳簿価額が回収不能な場合、すなわち割引前将来キャッシュ・フロー合計を上回る場合にのみ認識します。本問では割引前キャッシュ・フロー$950,000 > 帳簿価額$900,000であり回収可能性テストを通過するため、公正価値$700,000が帳簿価額を下回っていても減損損失は認識しません（$0）。$200,000（帳簿価額−公正価値）はテストに不合格だった場合の測定額です。これはIFRSと異なり、米国基準特有の2段階構造です。

questions.stem: On October 1, Year 1, Fairmont Corp. committed to a plan to sell a warehouse and properly classified it as held for sale. The warehouse has a carrying amount of $420,000, a fair value of $400,000, and estimated costs to sell of $30,000. What loss should Fairmont recognize upon classification as held for sale?
questions.explanation: A long-lived asset classified as held for sale is measured at the lower of its carrying amount or fair value less costs to sell. Fair value less costs to sell = $400,000 − $30,000 = $370,000, which is below the $420,000 carrying amount, so a loss of $420,000 − $370,000 = $50,000 is recognized. Depreciation also ceases upon held-for-sale classification. $20,000 compares carrying amount only with fair value, ignoring costs to sell; $30,000 is just the costs to sell; $0 ignores the required write-down.
questions.explanationJa: 売却目的保有（held for sale）に分類された長期性資産は、帳簿価額と「公正価値−売却費用」のいずれか低い方で測定します（ASC 360-10-35-43）。公正価値−売却費用 = $400,000 − $30,000 = $370,000 < 帳簿価額$420,000のため、損失 = $420,000 − $370,000 = $50,000を認識します。また分類時点で減価償却は中止されます。$20,000は売却費用を無視して公正価値とのみ比較した誤り、$30,000は売却費用そのもの、$0は必要な評価減の無視です。

questions.subtopic: Held for Sale - Subsequent Recovery Limitation
questions.stem: In Year 1, Westgate Co. classified a building as held for sale and wrote it down from its carrying amount of $420,000 to its fair value less costs to sell of $370,000. At the end of Year 2, while the building is still held for sale, its fair value less costs to sell has increased to $450,000. What gain should Westgate recognize in Year 2?
questions.explanation: A subsequent increase in fair value less costs to sell of a held-for-sale asset is recognized as a gain, but only up to the cumulative impairment losses previously recognized. The cumulative prior write-down was $50,000 ($420,000 − $370,000), so the recovery gain is limited to $50,000, bringing the carrying amount back to $420,000—not to the $450,000 current fair value less costs to sell. $80,000 would write the asset up above its pre-impairment carrying amount, which is prohibited; $30,000 is the excess above the original carrying amount that cannot be recognized; $0 ignores the permitted recovery for held-for-sale assets.
questions.explanationJa: 売却目的保有資産の「公正価値−売却費用」がその後回復した場合、過去に認識した減損損失の累計額を上限として利得を認識します（ASC 360-10-35-40）。過去の評価減累計は$50,000（$420,000 − $370,000）のため、回復利得は$50,000に制限され、帳簿価額は$420,000まで戻ります（$450,000までは戻せません）。$80,000は減損前帳簿価額を超える評価増で禁止、$30,000は認識できない超過部分、$0は売却目的保有資産に認められる回復処理の無視です。なお保有使用資産では減損の戻入れ自体が禁止されている点と対比されます。

questions.subtopic: ARO - Total First-Year Expense (Accretion plus Depreciation)
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Cobalt Energy placed a storage facility in service and recognized an asset retirement obligation with a fair value of $200,000, using a credit-adjusted risk-free rate of 8%. The related asset retirement cost is depreciated straight-line over the facility's 10-year life with no salvage value. What total expense related to the ARO and the asset retirement cost should Cobalt recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Recognizing an ARO produces two recurring charges: (1) accretion expense on the liability, $200,000 × 8% = $16,000, and (2) depreciation of the capitalized asset retirement cost, $200,000 / 10 years = $20,000. Total Year 1 expense = $16,000 + $20,000 = $36,000. $20,000 includes only the depreciation component; $16,000 includes only the accretion component; $40,000 incorrectly doubles the depreciation rather than adding accretion.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20では、ARO認識後に毎期2つの費用が生じます：(1) 負債の時の経過による増加額（accretion expense）= $200,000 × 8% = $16,000、(2) 資産計上した除去費用（asset retirement cost）の減価償却費 = $200,000 ÷ 10年 = $20,000。Year 1の合計費用 = $16,000 + $20,000 = $36,000。$20,000は減価償却のみ、$16,000はaccretionのみで、いずれも片方の構成要素の見落としです。$40,000は減価償却の二重計上による誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Depletion - Cost of Goods Sold vs Inventory
questions.stem: Galena Mining acquired a mineral property for $7,200,000 and incurred $800,000 of development costs. Galena expects to incur restoration costs of $500,000 (recognized as part of the depletion base) and estimates the land's residual value after restoration at $500,000. Estimated recoverable reserves are 4,000,000 tons. During Year 1, Galena extracted 600,000 tons and sold 500,000 tons. What amount of depletion should be included in Year 1 cost of goods sold?
questions.explanation: The depletion base = acquisition cost $7,200,000 + development $800,000 + restoration $500,000 − residual value $500,000 = $8,000,000. The depletion rate = $8,000,000 / 4,000,000 tons = $2.00 per ton. Total depletion for extraction = 600,000 × $2.00 = $1,200,000, but only the portion related to tons sold flows to cost of goods sold: 500,000 × $2.00 = $1,000,000. The remaining $200,000 (100,000 unsold tons) stays in inventory. $1,200,000 is total depletion capitalized to extracted units, not COGS; $1,062,500 results from omitting the residual value from the computation ($8,500,000 / 4,000,000 × 500,000); $900,000 results from an incorrect rate.
questions.explanationJa: 減耗償却基礎額 = 取得原価$7,200,000 + 開発費$800,000 + 原状回復費$500,000 − 土地の残存価値$500,000 = $8,000,000。減耗率 = $8,000,000 ÷ 4,000,000トン = $2.00/トン。採掘分の総減耗額は600,000トン × $2.00 = $1,200,000ですが、売上原価には販売分のみが計上されます：500,000トン × $2.00 = $1,000,000。未販売の100,000トン分$200,000は棚卸資産に残ります。$1,200,000は採掘分全額をCOGSとした誤り、$1,062,500は残存価値の控除漏れ、$900,000は率の計算誤りです。

questions.subtopic: Goodwill Impairment - Loss Less Than Goodwill Balance
questions.stem: Brookfield Corp.'s consumer products reporting unit has a carrying amount of $4,500,000, including goodwill of $800,000. The reporting unit's fair value is determined to be $4,000,000. Under ASC 350 (post-ASU 2017-04), what goodwill impairment loss should Brookfield recognize?
questions.explanation: Under the one-step goodwill impairment test, the loss equals the excess of the reporting unit's carrying amount over its fair value, limited to the carrying amount of goodwill: $4,500,000 − $4,000,000 = $500,000, which is less than the $800,000 goodwill balance, so the full $500,000 is recognized. $800,000 would write off all goodwill even though the shortfall is smaller; $0 would apply only if fair value equaled or exceeded the carrying amount; $300,000 is the residual goodwill after impairment, not the loss itself.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2017-04後のASC 350では、のれんの減損損失は報告単位の帳簿価額が公正価値を超過する額とし、のれんの帳簿価額を上限とします（1ステップテスト）。超過額 = $4,500,000 − $4,000,000 = $500,000 < のれん残高$800,000のため、$500,000全額を減損損失として認識します。$800,000は超過額が小さいのにのれん全額を償却する誤り、$0は公正価値が帳簿価額以上の場合の処理、$300,000は減損後ののれん残高（$800,000 − $500,000）であり損失額ではありません。

questions.subtopic: Goodwill Impairment - Loss Capped at Goodwill Balance
questions.stem: Lakeshore Inc.'s industrial reporting unit has a carrying amount of $6,000,000, including goodwill of $700,000. A quantitative impairment test determines the reporting unit's fair value to be $5,000,000. What goodwill impairment loss should Lakeshore recognize under ASC 350?
questions.explanation: The goodwill impairment loss is the amount by which the reporting unit's carrying amount exceeds its fair value ($6,000,000 − $5,000,000 = $1,000,000), but the loss cannot exceed the carrying amount of goodwill allocated to the reporting unit. Because goodwill is only $700,000, the loss is capped at $700,000. The remaining $300,000 shortfall is not recognized against other assets under the goodwill test (other long-lived assets are tested separately under ASC 360 before the goodwill test). $1,000,000 ignores the goodwill cap; $0 ignores the failed test; $300,000 is the uncapped excess over the goodwill balance.
questions.explanationJa: のれんの減損損失は報告単位の帳簿価額の公正価値超過額（$6,000,000 − $5,000,000 = $1,000,000）ですが、その報告単位に配分されたのれんの帳簿価額が上限となります（ASC 350-20-35-3C）。のれん残高は$700,000のため損失は$700,000に制限されます。残りの$300,000はのれんテストでは認識されません（他の長期性資産はのれんテストの前にASC 360で別途減損テストを実施）。$1,000,000は上限の無視、$0はテスト不合格の無視、$300,000は上限超過部分です。

questions.subtopic: Indefinite-Life Intangible Impairment - No Recoverability Test
questions.stem: Summit Media holds an indefinite-life broadcast license with a carrying amount of $900,000. At the annual impairment test date, the license's fair value is $760,000, and the undiscounted future cash flows expected from the license total $1,000,000. What impairment loss should Summit recognize?
questions.explanation: Indefinite-life intangible assets (other than goodwill) are tested for impairment under ASC 350-30 by comparing carrying amount directly with fair value—there is no undiscounted cash flow recoverability test. Because fair value ($760,000) is below carrying amount ($900,000), an impairment loss of $140,000 is recognized, even though undiscounted cash flows exceed the carrying amount. $0 incorrectly applies the ASC 360 recoverability screen, which is available only for assets being amortized or depreciated (finite-life or long-lived assets held and used); $100,000 and $240,000 compare the wrong figure pairs.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数を確定できない無形資産（のれん以外）の減損テストは、ASC 350-30に基づき帳簿価額と公正価値を直接比較します。割引前キャッシュ・フローによる回収可能性テスト（ASC 360の第1段階）は適用されません。公正価値$760,000 < 帳簿価額$900,000のため、減損損失$140,000を認識します。割引前キャッシュ・フロー$1,000,000が帳簿価額を上回っていても結論は変わりません。$0は耐用年数確定資産（償却資産）にのみ適用される回収可能性テストの誤適用です。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Novatech Corp. incurred the following costs: laboratory research aimed at discovering new technology, $120,000; design, construction, and testing of a preproduction prototype, $90,000; quality control testing during commercial production, $50,000; legal fees to obtain a patent on the new technology, $30,000; and market research for the new product, $25,000. What amount should Novatech report as research and development expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 730, R&D includes laboratory research aimed at new knowledge ($120,000) and the design, construction, and testing of preproduction prototypes ($90,000), for a total of $210,000. Quality control during commercial production is a period production cost, not R&D; legal fees to obtain a patent are capitalized as part of the patent's cost; and market research is a selling-related expense excluded from R&D. $315,000 includes all five items; $240,000 incorrectly adds the patent legal fees; $260,000 incorrectly adds quality control costs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730の研究開発費には、新知識の発見を目的とした実験的研究$120,000と、量産前プロトタイプの設計・製作・テスト$90,000が含まれ、合計$210,000です。商業生産中の品質管理テストはR&Dではなく製造期間費用、特許取得のための弁護士費用は特許権の取得原価として資産計上、市場調査は販売関連費用でR&Dから除外されます（ASC 730-10-55）。$315,000は5項目全部の合算、$240,000は特許法務費用の誤算入、$260,000は品質管理費の誤算入です。

questions.subtopic: R&D Equipment with Alternative Future Uses
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Helix Labs purchased testing equipment for $400,000 to be used in a current research project. The equipment has a 5-year useful life, no salvage value, and alternative future uses in other research projects and in production. The equipment was used exclusively on the research project during Year 1. What amount related to this equipment should Helix include in research and development expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Equipment acquired for R&D activities that has alternative future uses is capitalized and depreciated; the depreciation for the periods the equipment is used in R&D is classified as R&D expense. Year 1 depreciation = $400,000 / 5 = $80,000, charged to R&D expense. $400,000 would be correct only if the equipment had no alternative future uses, in which case the entire cost is expensed immediately; $0 ignores that the depreciation must be classified as R&D while the asset is used in research; $320,000 is the year-end carrying amount, not an expense.
questions.explanationJa: 代替的な将来の用途（alternative future uses）を有するR&D用設備は資産計上して減価償却し、研究活動に使用した期間の減価償却費をR&D費用に分類します（ASC 730-10-25-2(a)）。Year 1の減価償却費 = $400,000 ÷ 5年 = $80,000をR&D費用に計上します。$400,000は代替用途がない場合（取得時全額費用処理）の金額、$0は研究使用期間の償却費のR&D分類の無視、$320,000は期末帳簿価額であり費用ではありません。

questions.subtopic: Software to Be Sold - Costs Capitalized After Technological Feasibility
questions.stem: Quantum Soft is developing a software product to be sold to customers. During Year 1, it incurred $300,000 of costs before establishing technological feasibility, $500,000 of coding and testing costs after technological feasibility was established but before the product was available for general release, and $100,000 of customer support and maintenance costs after general release. Under ASC 985-20, what amount should Quantum capitalize as software development costs?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20, costs incurred before technological feasibility is established are expensed as R&D ($300,000). Costs incurred after technological feasibility but before general release—coding, testing, and producing product masters—are capitalized ($500,000). Costs after general release, such as maintenance and customer support, are expensed ($100,000). Therefore, only $500,000 is capitalized. $800,000 incorrectly capitalizes the pre-feasibility R&D costs; $900,000 capitalizes everything; $600,000 incorrectly capitalizes the post-release support costs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 985-20（市場販売目的ソフトウェア）では、技術的実現可能性（technological feasibility）確立前のコスト$300,000はR&D費用として処理、確立後から製品の一般リリースまでのコーディング・テスト費用$500,000は資産計上、リリース後の保守・カスタマーサポート費用$100,000は費用処理します。したがって資産計上額は$500,000のみです。$800,000は実現可能性確立前コストの誤資産化、$900,000は全額資産化、$600,000はリリース後コストの誤資産化です。

questions.stem: Under ASC 350-40, amortization of capitalized internal-use software costs should begin when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The application development stage begins

choices.label: B
choices.text: The software is ready for its intended use, regardless of whether it has been placed in service

choices.label: C
choices.text: Management commits funding to the software project

choices.label: D
choices.text: The post-implementation/operation stage costs are first incurred

questions.explanation: ASC 350-40-35-6 requires amortization of internal-use software to begin when the software is ready for its intended use—that is, after all substantial testing is completed—regardless of whether the software will be placed in service in planned stages that may extend beyond a reporting period.
