The start of the application development stage marks when capitalization begins, not amortization; management commitment of funding occurs in the preliminary project stage; and the post-implementation stage relates to training and maintenance costs, which are expensed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-40-35-6により、内部利用目的ソフトウェアの償却は「ソフトウェアが意図した用途に使用可能となった時点」、すなわち実質的なテストがすべて完了した時点から開始します。段階的に稼働させる計画であっても、使用可能になれば償却を開始します。アプリケーション開発段階の開始は資産計上の開始時点であり償却開始ではありません。経営者による資金コミットメントは予備プロジェクト段階の事象、ポスト実装・運用段階の費用（研修・保守）は発生時費用処理です。

questions.subtopic: Franchise - Initial Fee Amortization and Continuing Fees
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Brava Foods paid an initial franchise fee of $240,000 for a franchise with a 10-year useful life. The franchise agreement also requires Brava to pay a continuing franchise fee equal to 5% of annual franchise revenue. Franchise revenue for Year 1 was $600,000. What total franchise-related expense should Brava report for Year 1?
questions.explanation: The initial franchise fee is capitalized as an intangible asset and amortized over its useful life: $240,000 / 10 years = $24,000 per year. Continuing franchise fees are operating expenses of the period in which the related revenue is earned: $600,000 × 5% = $30,000. Total Year 1 franchise expense = $24,000 + $30,000 = $54,000. $24,000 omits the continuing fee; $30,000 omits the amortization; $270,000 incorrectly expenses the entire initial fee immediately plus the continuing fee.
questions.explanationJa: フランチャイズの初期加盟料は無形資産として計上し耐用年数で償却します：$240,000 ÷ 10年 = 年間$24,000。継続的フランチャイズ料（ロイヤルティ）は対応する収益が稼得された期の営業費用です：$600,000 × 5% = $30,000。Year 1の合計費用 = $24,000 + $30,000 = $54,000。$24,000は継続料の脱漏、$30,000は償却費の脱漏、$270,000は初期加盟料の全額即時費用化（$240,000 + $30,000）という誤りです。

questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 5, Vexel Corp.'s patent had a carrying amount of $180,000 with a remaining useful life of 6 years. On January 2, Year 5, Vexel paid $60,000 in legal fees to successfully defend the patent against an infringement suit. What amount of patent amortization expense should Vexel report for Year 5?
questions.explanation: Legal fees incurred in successfully defending a patent are capitalized because they establish the holder's legal rights and benefit future periods. The new carrying amount of $180,000 + $60,000 = $240,000 is amortized over the remaining 6-year useful life: $240,000 / 6 = $40,000 per year. $30,000 amortizes only the pre-defense carrying amount ($180,000 / 6); $90,000 expenses the legal fees immediately and adds the $30,000 amortization, which is the treatment for an unsuccessful defense; $60,000 has no valid computational basis here.
questions.explanationJa: 特許権の侵害訴訟で勝訴した場合の弁護士費用は、権利の法的有効性を確立し将来便益をもたらすため資産計上します。新帳簿価額 = $180,000 + $60,000 = $240,000を残存耐用年数6年で償却：$240,000 ÷ 6 = 年間$40,000。$30,000は防御費用を加算しない償却額、$90,000は敗訴時の処理（弁護士費用$60,000を即時費用化 + 償却$30,000）です。敗訴の場合は防御費用を費用処理し、特許自体の減損も検討する必要があります。

questions.subtopic: Trademark - Useful Life Shorter Than Legal Life
questions.stem: Halcyon Brands purchased a registered trademark for $96,000. The trademark's registration has a remaining legal life of 10 years and is renewable indefinitely at minimal cost. However, management plans to phase out the branded product line in 8 years and will not renew the trademark thereafter. What annual amortization expense should Halcyon record?
questions.explanation: A recognized intangible asset is amortized over its useful life to the reporting entity, which reflects the period over which the asset is expected to contribute to cash flows. Although the trademark is legally renewable indefinitely, management's plan to discontinue the product in 8 years limits the useful life to 8 years: $96,000 / 8 = $12,000 per year. $9,600 uses the 10-year legal life; $0 treats the trademark as indefinite-life, which is inappropriate because the entity-specific useful life is limited; $4,800 has no valid basis.
questions.explanationJa: 無形資産は「報告企業にとっての耐用年数」、すなわちキャッシュ・フローへの貢献が見込まれる期間で償却します（ASC 350-30-35-2〜3）。商標は法的には低コストで無期限更新可能ですが、経営者が8年後に製品ラインを廃止し更新しない計画のため、企業固有の耐用年数は8年に限定されます：$96,000 ÷ 8 = 年間$12,000。$9,600は法的残存期間10年の誤用、$0は耐用年数不確定（indefinite）扱いの誤りです。更新意思と能力がある場合のみ更新期間を耐用年数に含められます。

questions.stem: Axion Corp. acquired in-process research and development (IPR&D) in a business combination and recognized it as an indefinite-life intangible asset at its acquisition-date fair value of $3,000,000. Two years later, the R&D project is successfully completed and the resulting technology is placed in service. How should Axion account for the IPR&D asset upon completion of the project?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Write off the entire $3,000,000 to expense because R&D costs must be expensed

choices.label: B
choices.text: Assign the asset a finite useful life and begin amortizing it over that life

choices.label: C
choices.text: Continue carrying it as an indefinite-life asset subject only to annual impairment testing

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reclassify the amount to goodwill

questions.explanation: IPR&D acquired in a business combination is capitalized at fair value and treated as an indefinite-life intangible asset during the development period, tested for impairment annually. When the project is completed, the asset's useful life becomes determinable: the entity assigns a finite useful life and amortizes the asset over the period it is expected to contribute to cash flows. Writing it off to expense applies only to internally incurred R&D costs under ASC 730 (or if the acquired project is abandoned); continuing indefinite-life treatment is inappropriate once the development is complete; reclassification to goodwill is never appropriate for a separately recognized identifiable asset.
questions.explanationJa: 企業結合で取得したIPR&Dは公正価値で資産計上し、開発期間中は耐用年数不確定の無形資産として年次減損テストを行います。プロジェクトが成功裏に完了した時点で耐用年数が確定可能となるため、確定耐用年数を付して償却を開始します（ASC 350-30-35-17A）。全額費用化は内部発生のR&D費用（ASC 730）またはプロジェクト放棄時の処理、不確定耐用年数の継続は開発完了後には不適切、のれんへの振替は個別に認識された識別可能資産には行いません。

questions.stem: Juniper Corp. incurred the following costs for a new corporate site: land purchase price $600,000; perimeter fencing $40,000; paved parking lots $70,000; permanent landscaping (earth-moving and contouring with indefinite life) $20,000; and outdoor lighting systems $15,000. What total amount should Juniper classify as depreciable land improvements?
questions.explanation: Land improvements with limited useful lives are depreciable: fencing $40,000 + parking lots $70,000 + outdoor lighting $15,000 = $125,000. Permanent landscaping with an indefinite life (earth-moving, contouring) is added to the non-depreciable land account, as is the $600,000 purchase price. $145,000 incorrectly includes the permanent landscaping; $110,000 omits the lighting; $85,000 omits the parking lots.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数が限定される土地改良（land improvements）は減価償却の対象です：フェンス$40,000 + 舗装駐車場$70,000 + 屋外照明設備$15,000 = $125,000。一方、恒久的な造園（整地・地形造成等で耐用年数が無期限のもの）$20,000は減価償却しない土地勘定に算入します（購入価格$600,000も同様）。$145,000は恒久的造園の誤算入、$110,000は照明の脱漏、$85,000は駐車場の脱漏です。

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest - Conditions to Begin Capitalization
questions.stem: Under ASC 835-20, which of the following is NOT a condition required for interest capitalization on a qualifying asset to begin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expenditures for the asset have been made

choices.label: B
choices.text: Activities necessary to get the asset ready for its intended use are in progress

choices.label: C
choices.text: A borrowing specifically designated for the construction of the asset has been arranged

choices.label: D
choices.text: Interest cost is being incurred

questions.explanation: Interest capitalization begins when three conditions are all met: (1) expenditures for the asset have been made, (2) activities necessary to get the asset ready for its intended use are in progress, and (3) interest cost is being incurred. A specific construction borrowing is not required—interest on general borrowings is capitalizable using the weighted-average rate, so an entity with any outstanding debt can capitalize interest even without project-specific financing.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20-25-3により、利子の資本化は次の3条件がすべて満たされた時点で開始します：(1) 資産に対する支出が行われていること、(2) 資産を意図した用途に使用可能にするために必要な活動が進行中であること、(3) 利子費用が発生していること。建設のための特定借入の存在は要件ではありません。一般借入の利子も加重平均利率を用いて資本化できるため、プロジェクト固有の資金調達がなくても、何らかの有利子負債があれば資本化は可能です。

questions.subtopic: Nonreciprocal Transfer - Donation of Asset to Charity
questions.stem: Cypress Corp. donated a parcel of land to a charitable organization. The land had a carrying amount of $100,000 and a fair value of $150,000 at the date of donation. What gain, if any, should Cypress recognize on the donation?
questions.explanation: A nonreciprocal transfer of a nonmonetary asset to another entity is recorded at the fair value of the asset given up, with a gain or loss recognized for the difference between fair value and carrying amount. Cypress records contribution expense of $150,000 and recognizes a gain of $150,000 − $100,000 = $50,000. $150,000 is the contribution expense, not the gain; $100,000 is the carrying amount removed from the books; $0 incorrectly applies carrying-amount accounting, which does not apply to nonreciprocal transfers measured at fair value.
questions.explanationJa: 非貨幣性資産の非対価性移転（nonreciprocal transfer、寄付等）は引き渡した資産の公正価値で記録し、公正価値と帳簿価額の差額を損益として認識します（ASC 845-10-30-1）。Cypressは寄付費用$150,000を計上するとともに、利得 = $150,000 − $100,000 = $50,000を認識します。仕訳は「寄付費用150,000 / 土地100,000・利得50,000」です。$150,000は寄付費用の額、$100,000は除却される帳簿価額、$0は帳簿価額ベース処理の誤適用です。

questions.stem: Falcon Corp. acquired 100% of Target Inc. in a business combination. Falcon paid $5,000,000 cash to Target's shareholders and also paid $200,000 in legal and investment-banking fees directly related to the acquisition. The fair value of Target's identifiable net assets was $4,400,000. What amount of goodwill should Falcon recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 805, acquisition-related costs such as legal, accounting, and investment-banking fees are expensed as incurred and are not part of the consideration transferred. Goodwill = consideration transferred $5,000,000 − fair value of identifiable net assets $4,400,000 = $600,000. The $200,000 of fees is charged to expense in the period of the acquisition. $800,000 incorrectly adds the acquisition costs to consideration; $400,000 incorrectly subtracts them; $0 ignores the excess of consideration over net assets.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 805では、法務・会計・投資銀行手数料などの取得関連コストは発生時に費用処理し、移転対価には含めません（ASC 805-10-25-23）。のれん = 移転対価$5,000,000 − 識別可能純資産の公正価値$4,400,000 = $600,000。手数料$200,000は取得した期の費用です。$800,000は取得関連コストを対価に加算した誤り（旧基準SFAS 141の処理）、$400,000は対価から控除した誤りです。

questions.stem: Pemberton Corp. owns a factory containing asbestos that, under current law, must be removed and disposed of in a special manner whenever the factory undergoes major renovation or is demolished. Pemberton has no current plans to renovate or demolish the factory, but the building will not last forever. Under ASC 410-20, how should Pemberton account for the asbestos removal obligation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize no liability until renovation or demolition activities actually begin

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disclose the obligation but recognize no liability because the timing of settlement is uncertain

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a liability only if removal is expected within the next 12 months

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a liability at fair value currently if the fair value can be reasonably estimated, because the obligation to perform is unconditional

questions.explanation: ASC 410-20 addresses conditional asset retirement obligations: even though the timing and method of settlement are conditional on a future event (renovation or demolition), the legal obligation to perform the asbestos removal is unconditional and exists today. The entity must recognize a liability at fair value when incurred if fair value can be reasonably estimated, incorporating the uncertainty about timing and method into the measurement (for example, through expected present value techniques). Deferring recognition until activity begins, merely disclosing, or applying a 12-month threshold all contradict the recognition requirements for conditional AROs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20（FIN 47を統合）は条件付資産除去債務を扱います。決済の時期・方法が将来の事象（改修や解体）に条件付けられていても、アスベスト除去を実施する法的義務自体は無条件に現在存在します。したがって公正価値を合理的に見積れる場合は、発生時に公正価値で負債を認識しなければなりません。時期・方法の不確実性は期待現在価値法などにより測定に織り込みます。活動開始まで認識を遅らせる処理、開示のみの処理、12か月基準はいずれも条件付AROの認識要件に反します。

questions.stem: Tarragon Corp. purchased a production machine and incurred the following costs: invoice price $150,000; freight-in $6,000; insurance during transit $2,400; installation $10,000; test runs prior to placing the machine in service $3,600; and training of machine operators $8,000. What amount should Tarragon capitalize as the cost of the machine?
questions.explanation: All costs necessary to acquire the asset and bring it to the condition and location for its intended use are capitalized: invoice price $150,000 + freight-in $6,000 + transit insurance $2,400 + installation $10,000 + test runs $3,600 = $172,000. Employee training costs of $8,000 are a period expense because they relate to the employees, not to readying the asset itself. The $180,000 figure incorrectly includes training; $168,400 incorrectly excludes the test-run costs, which are part of readying the asset; $160,000 omits freight, insurance, and test runs.
questions.explanationJa: 資産を意図した用途で使用できる状態・場所にするために必要な支出はすべて取得原価に算入する。請求書価格$150,000＋運送費$6,000＋輸送中保険料$2,400＋据付費$10,000＋試運転費$3,600＝$172,000。従業員研修費$8,000は資産ではなく従業員に関連する支出なので期間費用とする。$180,000は研修費を誤って含めた金額、$168,400は資産の準備に必要な試運転費を誤って除外した金額、$160,000は運送費・保険料・試運転費を漏らした金額である。

questions.subtopic: Lump-Sum Purchase - Allocation and First-Year Depreciation
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Verdant Co. acquired land, a building, and equipment in a single transaction for $900,000. Appraised fair values were: land $250,000, building $500,000, equipment $250,000. The building has a 30-year useful life and the equipment a 5-year useful life, both with no salvage value, depreciated straight-line. What is Verdant's total depreciation expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Allocate the $900,000 price by relative fair values (total appraised $1,000,000): land $900,000 × 25% = $225,000; building $900,000 × 50% = $450,000; equipment $900,000 × 25% = $225,000. Depreciation: building $450,000 / 30 = $15,000; equipment $225,000 / 5 = $45,000; total $60,000. Land is not depreciated. The $66,667 figure incorrectly depreciates the unallocated appraised values ($500,000/30 + $250,000/5); $67,500 incorrectly adds depreciation on the allocated land cost; $45,000 omits the building depreciation.

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest - Limited to Actual Interest Incurred
questions.stem: Wexford Corp. is constructing a qualifying asset. Weighted-average accumulated expenditures for the year were $3,000,000, and the appropriate capitalization rate is 10%. Total interest actually incurred on all borrowings during the year was $240,000. What amount of interest should Wexford capitalize for the year?
questions.explanation: Avoidable interest is $3,000,000 × 10% = $300,000, but under ASC 835-20 the amount capitalized cannot exceed total interest actually incurred during the period. Because actual interest was only $240,000, capitalized interest is limited to $240,000. The $300,000 amount ignores the actual-interest ceiling; $60,000 is merely the excess of avoidable over actual interest; capitalizing nothing is wrong because the asset qualifies and interest was incurred.
questions.explanationJa: 回避可能利息は$3,000,000×10%＝$300,000だが、ASC 835-20では資本化額は当期に実際に発生した利息総額を上限とする。実際の発生利息が$240,000しかないため、資本化額は$240,000に制限される。$300,000は実際発生利息という上限を無視した金額、$60,000は回避可能利息と実際利息の差額にすぎない。適格資産であり利息も発生しているため、資本化額ゼロも誤りである。

questions.stem: Brindle Corp. is constructing a warehouse that is a qualifying asset under ASC 835-20. Midway through the year, Brindle intentionally suspended substantially all construction activities for four months while management re-evaluated the project's design, although interest on the construction loan continued to accrue. How should Brindle treat the interest incurred during the four-month suspension?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize it, because interest capitalization continues as long as the loan is outstanding

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize it, because the suspension was shorter than one year

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense it, because interest capitalization ceases when activities necessary to ready the asset are intentionally suspended for an extended period

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer it as a separate asset and amortize it after construction resumes

questions.explanation: Interest is capitalized only while activities necessary to get the asset ready for its intended use are in progress. When an entity intentionally suspends substantially all construction activities for an extended period, capitalization ceases and interest incurred during the suspension is expensed. Brief, normal interruptions (such as weather delays) do not stop capitalization, but a deliberate four-month halt does. The notion that capitalization continues whenever the loan is outstanding confuses incurring interest with qualifying for capitalization, and there is no one-year grace period or separate deferral asset for such interest.
questions.explanationJa: 利息の資本化は、資産を意図した用途に使用できる状態にするための活動が進行している期間に限られる。建設活動の実質的すべてを意図的に長期間中断した場合、資本化は停止し、中断期間中の利息は費用処理する。悪天候などによる短期間の通常の中断は資本化を妨げないが、4か月にわたる意図的な中断は資本化停止に該当する。「借入金が残っている限り資本化が続く」という考えは利息の発生と資本化要件を混同しており、1年間の猶予や別個の繰延資産として処理する規定も存在しない。

questions.stem: Calloway Inc. purchased equipment on October 1, Year 1 for $120,000. The equipment has an estimated salvage value of $10,000 and a 5-year useful life. Calloway uses the double-declining balance method. What is depreciation expense for Year 2?
questions.explanation: The DDB rate is 2/5 = 40%, applied to beginning book value (salvage is ignored in the computation but acts as a floor). Year 1 (3 months): $120,000 × 40% × 3/12 = $12,000. Year 2: ($120,000 − $12,000) × 40% = $43,200. The $48,000 figure applies 40% to the original cost without reducing it for Year 1 depreciation; $28,800 incorrectly assumes a full year of Year 1 depreciation ($48,000) before computing Year 2; $39,600 incorrectly deducts salvage value from the depreciation base.
questions.explanationJa: 定率法（200%）の償却率は2/5＝40%で、期首帳簿価額に適用する（残存価額は計算には用いず下限としてのみ機能する）。Year 1（3か月分）：$120,000×40%×3/12＝$12,000。Year 2：($120,000−$12,000)×40%＝$43,200。$48,000はYear 1の償却を控除せず取得原価に40%を掛けた金額、$28,800はYear 1を誤って通年（$48,000）として計算した場合の金額、$39,600は残存価額を誤って償却基礎から控除した金額である。

questions.stem: An entity acquires a machine at the beginning of the year and expects to use it evenly over its useful life. Assuming the same cost, salvage value, and useful life under each method, which depreciation method will generally produce the highest depreciation expense in the first year?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Straight-line

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sum-of-the-years'-digits

choices.label: C
choices.text: Units-of-production, assuming even usage over the asset's life

choices.label: D
choices.text: Double-declining balance

questions.explanation: Double-declining balance applies twice the straight-line rate to the full cost (salvage is ignored in the base), so its first-year charge exceeds the other methods. Sum-of-the-years'-digits is also accelerated but applies its largest fraction to cost less salvage, producing a smaller first-year amount than DDB. Straight-line spreads cost evenly, and units-of-production with even usage approximates straight-line, so neither front-loads expense.
questions.explanationJa: 200%定率法は定額法の2倍の率を取得原価全額（残存価額は基礎から控除しない）に適用するため、初年度の償却費が最も大きくなる。級数法も加速償却だが、最大の係数を「取得原価−残存価額」に適用するため初年度の金額は定率法より小さい。定額法は原価を均等配分し、利用が平準的な場合の生産高比例法は定額法に近似するため、いずれも初年度に費用が偏らない。

questions.subtopic: Change in Depreciation Method - Accounting Treatment
questions.stem: At the beginning of Year 4, Mercer Co. changed its depreciation method for a class of equipment from double-declining balance to straight-line because the new method better reflects the expected pattern of consumption of benefits. How should Mercer account for this change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle, applied prospectively

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a change in accounting principle, applied retrospectively to all prior periods presented

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a correction of an error, with a prior-period adjustment to beginning retained earnings

choices.label: D
choices.text: By recognizing the cumulative effect of the change in current-period net income

questions.explanation: Under ASC 250, a change in depreciation method is a change in accounting estimate effected by a change in accounting principle. It is accounted for prospectively: the remaining book value is depreciated under the new method over the remaining useful life. Retrospective restatement applies to pure changes in principle, not to this hybrid; treating it as an error correction is wrong because no error occurred; and the cumulative-effect-in-income approach was eliminated by current GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 250上、減価償却方法の変更は「会計方針の変更によりもたらされる会計上の見積りの変更」に該当し、将来に向かって（prospectively）処理する。すなわち残存帳簿価額を新しい方法で残存耐用年数にわたり償却する。遡及修正再表示は純粋な会計方針の変更に適用されるものでこのハイブリッドには適用されない。誤謬が生じたわけではないので誤謬の訂正としての処理も誤り。累積的影響額を当期損益に計上する方法は現行GAAPで廃止されている。

questions.subtopic: Depreciation After Impairment - New Cost Basis
questions.stem: At the end of Year 3, Dorset Corp. recognized an impairment loss on equipment held for use, reducing its carrying amount from $480,000 to its fair value of $300,000. At that date, the equipment had a remaining useful life of 6 years and no salvage value. Dorset uses straight-line depreciation. What is depreciation expense for Year 4?
questions.explanation: After an impairment of an asset held for use, the reduced carrying amount becomes the asset's new cost basis, and restoration of the loss is prohibited. Depreciation is computed on the new basis: $300,000 / 6 years = $50,000. The $80,000 figure incorrectly depreciates the pre-impairment carrying amount ($480,000 / 6); $30,000 incorrectly depreciates only the impairment loss ($180,000 / 6); $96,000 spreads the old basis over 5 years instead of the remaining 6.
questions.explanationJa: 保有使用目的の資産について減損を認識した後は、減額後の帳簿価額が新たな原価基礎となり、減損損失の戻入れは禁止される。減価償却は新基礎で計算する：$300,000÷6年＝$50,000。$80,000は減損前の帳簿価額（$480,000÷6）で誤って計算した金額、$30,000は減損損失額のみ（$180,000÷6）を誤って償却した金額、$96,000は旧基礎を残存6年ではなく5年で配分した金額である。

questions.subtopic: Held for Sale - Cessation of Depreciation
questions.stem: Larkin Co. owns a building with annual straight-line depreciation of $48,000. On April 1, Year 5, the building met all criteria for classification as held for sale and was so classified. The building remained unsold at December 31, Year 5. What amount of depreciation expense should Larkin recognize on the building for Year 5?
questions.explanation: A long-lived asset is depreciated while held and used, but depreciation ceases once the asset is classified as held for sale. Larkin depreciates the building only for January through March: $48,000 × 3/12 = $12,000. Recognizing nothing ignores the three months before classification; $36,000 incorrectly depreciates the nine months after classification instead of the three months before; a full year of $48,000 ignores the held-for-sale classification entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 長期性資産は保有使用中は減価償却するが、売却目的保有に分類された時点で減価償却を中止する。したがって1月〜3月の3か月分のみ計上する：$48,000×3/12＝$12,000。ゼロとする処理は分類前の3か月分を無視している。$36,000は分類前の3か月ではなく分類後の9か月分を誤って償却した金額。$48,000の通年計上は売却目的保有への分類を完全に無視している。

questions.stem: Under ASC 845, a nonmonetary exchange has commercial substance when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity's future cash flows are expected to change significantly as a result of the exchange

choices.label: B
choices.text: The fair values of the assets exchanged are approximately equal

choices.label: C
choices.text: Monetary consideration (boot) of at least 25% of the fair value of the exchange is included

choices.label: D
choices.text: The assets exchanged are of a similar nature and used in the same line of business

questions.explanation: An exchange has commercial substance if the entity's future cash flows are expected to change significantly — that is, the configuration (risk, timing, or amount) of the cash flows of the asset received differs significantly from that of the asset given up, or the entity-specific value of the affected operations changes significantly. Approximate equality of fair values says nothing about cash-flow configuration. Boot of 25% or more causes the entire transaction to be treated as monetary, which is a separate rule, not the definition of commercial substance. Similarity of the assets actually suggests the exchange may lack commercial substance.
questions.explanationJa: 交換に商業的実質があるのは、交換の結果として企業の将来キャッシュ・フローが大きく変化すると見込まれる場合である。すなわち、受取資産のキャッシュ・フローの構成（リスク・時期・金額）が引渡資産と大きく異なるか、影響を受ける事業の企業固有価値が大きく変化する場合をいう。交換資産の公正価値がほぼ等しいことはキャッシュ・フロー構成とは無関係である。25%以上のbootは取引全体を貨幣性取引として扱う別個のルールであり、商業的実質の定義ではない。資産が同種であることはむしろ商業的実質を欠く可能性を示唆する。

questions.subtopic: Nonmonetary Exchange - Fair Value of Asset Received More Clearly Evident
questions.stem: Pemrose Co. exchanged a custom-built piece of equipment for a machine in a transaction with commercial substance. The fair value of the equipment given up could not be reliably determined, but the machine received has a readily determinable fair value of $62,000. The equipment given up had a carrying amount of $50,000. At what amount should Pemrose record the machine received?
questions.explanation: In an exchange with commercial substance, the asset received is recorded at the fair value of the asset given up unless the fair value of the asset received is more clearly evident. Because the equipment's fair value is not reliably determinable, the machine is recorded at its own fair value of $62,000, and Pemrose recognizes a $12,000 gain ($62,000 − $50,000 carrying amount). Recording $50,000 (carrying amount) would only be appropriate if no fair value were determinable or the exchange lacked commercial substance with no boot; $12,000 is the gain, not the asset's cost; $56,000 is an unsupported average of the two amounts.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質のある交換では、受取資産は原則として引渡資産の公正価値で計上するが、受取資産の公正価値の方がより明確な場合は受取資産の公正価値を用いる。引渡した設備の公正価値が信頼性をもって測定できないため、機械は自身の公正価値$62,000で計上し、利得$12,000（$62,000−帳簿価額$50,000）を認識する。帳簿価額$50,000での計上は公正価値が全く測定できない場合や商業的実質を欠きbootがない場合の処理である。$12,000は利得であって資産の取得原価ではない。$56,000は両者の単純平均で根拠がない。

questions.subtopic: Asset Sale - SYD Depreciation Updated to Disposal Date
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Quillon Corp. purchased equipment for $100,000 with a $10,000 salvage value and a 4-year useful life, depreciated by the sum-of-the-years'-digits method. On July 1, Year 3, Quillon sold the equipment for $35,000. What gain or loss should Quillon recognize on the sale?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $7,000 gain

choices.label: B
choices.text: $2,000 loss

questions.explanation: The depreciable base is $90,000 and the SYD denominator is 4+3+2+1 = 10. Year 1: $90,000 × 4/10 = $36,000. Year 2: $90,000 × 3/10 = $27,000. Year 3 (6 months): $90,000 × 2/10 × 6/12 = $9,000. Accumulated depreciation at July 1, Year 3 is $72,000, so carrying amount is $28,000 and the gain is $35,000 − $28,000 = $7,000. A $2,000 loss results from omitting the half-year of Year 3 depreciation (carrying amount $37,000); a $16,000 gain results from taking a full year of Year 3 depreciation; a $25,000 gain incorrectly compares proceeds to the $10,000 salvage value.
questions.explanationJa: 償却基礎は$90,000、級数の分母は4+3+2+1＝10。Year 1：$90,000×4/10＝$36,000。Year 2：$90,000×3/10＝$27,000。Year 3（6か月分）：$90,000×2/10×6/12＝$9,000。売却日（Year 3の7月1日）時点の減価償却累計額は$72,000、帳簿価額は$28,000となり、利得は$35,000−$28,000＝$7,000。$2,000の損失はYear 3の半年分の償却を漏らした場合（帳簿価額$37,000）、$16,000の利得はYear 3を通年で償却した場合、$25,000の利得は売却収入を残存価額$10,000と誤って比較した場合の金額である。

questions.stem: Norlund Corp.'s patent had a carrying amount of $240,000 when a competitor sued, challenging the patent's validity. Norlund incurred $50,000 in legal fees defending the patent and lost the lawsuit, rendering the patent worthless. What total amount should Norlund charge to expense (including any loss) as a result of these events?
questions.explanation: Legal fees for a successful patent defense are capitalized, but fees for an unsuccessful defense are expensed as incurred. In addition, losing the suit makes the patent worthless, so its $240,000 carrying amount must be written off as an impairment loss. Total charge: $50,000 + $240,000 = $290,000. Charging only $50,000 ignores the required write-off of the now-worthless patent, and charging only $240,000 incorrectly capitalizes legal fees from a failed defense.
questions.explanationJa: 特許権の防御訴訟に勝訴した場合の訴訟費用は資産計上するが、敗訴した場合の費用は発生時に費用処理する。さらに敗訴により特許が無価値となったため、帳簿価額$240,000を減損損失として全額切り下げる必要がある。費用計上総額は$50,000＋$240,000＝$290,000。$50,000のみの計上は無価値となった特許の切下げを無視しており、$240,000のみの計上は敗訴した防御費用を誤って資産計上している。

questions.stem: Selwyn Publishing purchased a copyright for $80,000. The copyright's remaining legal life is 50 years, but Selwyn estimates that the work will generate revenues for only 8 more years. What is Selwyn's annual amortization expense for the copyright?
questions.explanation: A finite-life intangible is amortized over the shorter of its legal life and its useful (economic) life. Here the useful life of 8 years is shorter than the 50-year legal life, so annual amortization is $80,000 / 8 = $10,000. Amortizing over 50 years ($1,600 per year) incorrectly uses the legal life; expensing the full $80,000 immediately is wrong because a purchased copyright is a capitalizable asset; recording no amortization treats the copyright as indefinite-lived, which is inconsistent with the 8-year revenue estimate.
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数が確定できる無形資産は、法的耐用年数と経済的耐用年数のいずれか短い期間で償却する。本問では経済的耐用年数8年が法的残存期間50年より短いため、年間償却額は$80,000÷8＝$10,000。50年で償却する$1,600は法的耐用年数を誤って使用している。購入した著作権は資産計上すべきものなので$80,000の即時費用化は誤り。償却しない処理は著作権を非確定耐用年数とみなすことになり、収益獲得期間8年という見積りと矛盾する。

questions.stem: Under ASC 350-30, which of the following best describes how an entity may use a qualitative assessment when testing an indefinite-life intangible asset (other than goodwill) for impairment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The entity may first assess qualitative factors; a quantitative fair value test is required only if it is more likely than not that the asset is impaired

choices.label: B
choices.text: A qualitative assessment is permitted only for goodwill, not for other indefinite-life intangibles

choices.label: C
choices.text: The entity must perform an undiscounted cash flow recoverability test before any qualitative assessment

choices.label: D
choices.text: A qualitative assessment permanently replaces the quantitative test once elected

questions.explanation: An entity has the option to first assess qualitative factors to determine whether it is more likely than not (a likelihood of more than 50%) that an indefinite-life intangible asset is impaired. Only if that threshold is met must the entity compute fair value and compare it with the carrying amount. The qualitative option is available for indefinite-life intangibles as well as goodwill. The undiscounted recoverability test applies to long-lived assets under ASC 360, not to indefinite-life intangibles. The election is made each period for each asset and does not permanently eliminate the quantitative test.
questions.explanationJa: 企業はまず定性的要因を評価し、非確定耐用年数の無形資産が減損している可能性が50%超（more likely than not）かどうかを判断するオプションを有する。この閾値を満たす場合にのみ、公正価値を算定して帳簿価額と比較する定量テストが必要となる。定性評価オプションはのれんだけでなく非確定耐用年数の無形資産にも認められている。割引前キャッシュ・フローによる回収可能性テストはASC 360の長期性資産に適用されるものであり、非確定耐用年数の無形資産には適用されない。この選択は資産ごと・期間ごとに行うものであり、定量テストを恒久的に置き換えるものではない。

questions.subtopic: R&D Performed Under Contract for Others
questions.stem: During Year 1, Halverson Labs incurred $300,000 of costs on research projects for its own benefit and $200,000 of costs performing research under a contract for a customer, which is reimbursable under the contract. What amount should Halverson report as research and development expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: ASC 730 requires R&D costs to be expensed as incurred, but it explicitly excludes R&D conducted for others under a contractual arrangement. Costs of contract research performed for a customer are accounted for as contract costs (cost of services), not R&D expense. Therefore R&D expense is only the $300,000 incurred for Halverson's own projects. The $500,000 amount incorrectly includes the reimbursable contract work; $200,000 reflects only the contract work, which is the portion that is excluded; reporting nothing ignores the company's own research spending.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730は研究開発費の発生時費用処理を要求するが、契約に基づき他者のために行う研究開発は適用範囲から明示的に除外されている。顧客のために行う受託研究の費用は契約原価（役務原価）として処理し、研究開発費には含めない。したがって研究開発費は自社プロジェクトに要した$300,000のみである。$500,000は償還可能な受託研究を誤って含めた金額、$200,000はむしろ除外すべき受託研究部分のみの金額であり、ゼロとする処理は自社の研究支出を無視している。

questions.stem: Trevane Corp. spent $400,000 on research and development activities that resulted in a new product design, for which it was granted a patent. Trevane also paid $30,000 in legal and registration fees to secure the patent. What amount should Trevane capitalize as the cost of the patent?
questions.explanation: R&D costs must be expensed as incurred even if they ultimately lead to a patentable product, so the $400,000 cannot be capitalized. However, legal and registration fees to obtain the patent are directly attributable costs of the intangible and are capitalized: $30,000. Capitalizing $430,000 or $400,000 improperly capitalizes R&D; capitalizing nothing ignores the registration costs, which do qualify for capitalization.
questions.explanationJa: 研究開発費は、最終的に特許につながったとしても発生時に費用処理しなければならないため、$400,000は資産計上できない。一方、特許取得のための法務費用・登録費用は無形資産に直接帰属する原価として資産計上する：$30,000。$430,000や$400,000の計上は研究開発費を不適切に資産化しており、ゼロとする処理は資産計上が認められる登録費用を無視している。

questions.subtopic: Software for Sale - Costs After General Release
questions.stem: Veristone Software has completed development of a software product to be sold to customers, and the product is now available for general release. How should Veristone account for the costs of duplicating the software, producing training materials, and physically packaging the product for distribution?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense them as research and development costs

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalize them as software development costs and amortize using the greater-of method

choices.label: C
choices.text: Expense them immediately as selling expenses

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalize them as inventory and charge them to cost of sales when the units are sold

questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20, once a product is available for general release, capitalization of software development costs ceases. Costs incurred to duplicate the software, prepare training materials, and package the product are production costs that are capitalized as inventory and charged to cost of sales when revenue from the units is recognized. They are not R&D (development is complete), they are not amortizable software development costs (capitalization has ceased), and expensing them immediately as selling costs would mismatch them with the related sales revenue.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 985-20では、製品が一般リリース可能となった時点でソフトウェア開発費の資産化は終了する。ソフトウェアの複製、トレーニング教材の作成、物理的なパッケージングの費用は製造原価であり、棚卸資産として計上し、販売時に売上原価へ振り替える。開発は完了しているため研究開発費ではなく、資産化期間が終了しているため償却対象のソフトウェア開発費でもない。販売費として即時費用化すると関連する売上収益との対応が損なわれる。

questions.stem: Cobalt Corp.'s internal-use software has been in operation for two years. During the current year, Cobalt incurred: $100,000 for an upgrade that adds significant new functionality; $40,000 for user training on the upgraded system; and $60,000 for routine maintenance. What amount should Cobalt capitalize for the current year?
questions.explanation: In the post-implementation/operation stage, internal training and maintenance costs are expensed as incurred. However, costs of upgrades and enhancements are capitalized if it is probable they will result in additional functionality — which the $100,000 upgrade does. Therefore only $100,000 is capitalized. The $200,000 amount incorrectly capitalizes everything; $140,000 incorrectly capitalizes training; capitalizing nothing ignores the functionality-adding upgrade, which qualifies for capitalization.
questions.explanationJa: 稼働後（post-implementation/operation）段階では、研修費用と保守費用は発生時に費用処理する。一方、アップグレードや機能拡張の費用は、追加的な機能をもたらす可能性が高い場合に資産計上する。本問の$100,000のアップグレードは重要な新機能を追加するためこれに該当し、資産計上額は$100,000のみとなる。$200,000は全額を誤って資産化した金額、$140,000は研修費を誤って資産化した金額であり、ゼロとする処理は資産計上要件を満たす機能追加アップグレードを無視している。

questions.stem: Skyway Airlines is required to perform a major airframe overhaul on each aircraft every five years. Which of the following methods of accounting for planned major maintenance activities is prohibited under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expensing the overhaul costs as incurred (direct expensing method)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Accruing the estimated cost of the next overhaul ratably as a liability before the overhaul is performed (accrue-in-advance method)

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalizing the overhaul cost when incurred and amortizing it to the date of the next overhaul (deferral method)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Identifying the overhaul component of the asset's cost and depreciating it separately to the first overhaul date (built-in overhaul method)

questions.explanation: ASC 360-10-25-5 prohibits the accrue-in-advance method because the obligation to perform future maintenance does not meet the definition of a liability before the maintenance occurs — the entity has not yet received services or incurred a present obligation. Direct expensing, the deferral method, and the built-in overhaul method are all acceptable approaches for planned major maintenance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-25-5は、将来のオーバーホール費用を実施前に負債として段階的に引き当てる方法（accrue-in-advance法）を禁止している。整備が実施される前の時点では、企業はまだ役務の提供を受けておらず現在の債務も負っていないため、負債の定義を満たさないからである。発生時費用処理（直接費用化法）、実施時に資産計上して次回オーバーホールまで償却する繰延法、取得原価のうちオーバーホール対応部分を区分して初回オーバーホール時期まで個別に償却するbuilt-in overhaul法は、いずれも計画的大規模整備について認められる方法である。

questions.stem: Meridian Air uses the deferral method to account for major engine inspections, which regulations require every four years. At the beginning of Year 1, Meridian paid $240,000 for a major inspection of an engine. What amount of expense related to this inspection should Meridian recognize in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the deferral method, the cost of a major inspection or overhaul is capitalized when incurred and amortized over the period until the next scheduled inspection. Here: $240,000 / 4 years = $60,000 per year. Expensing the full $240,000 immediately describes the direct expensing method, not the deferral method; recognizing no expense ignores the required amortization; $120,000 incorrectly amortizes over two years rather than the four-year inspection cycle.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延法では、大規模検査・オーバーホールの費用を発生時に資産計上し、次回の予定検査までの期間にわたり償却する。本問では$240,000÷4年＝年$60,000。$240,000の即時全額費用化は繰延法ではなく直接費用化法の処理である。費用ゼロとする処理は必要な償却を無視しており、$120,000は4年の検査サイクルではなく2年で誤って償却した金額である。

questions.subtopic: Impairment Testing - Order of Testing Multiple Asset Types
questions.stem: A reporting unit's carrying amount includes goodwill, indefinite-life intangible assets, and long-lived assets (PP&E and finite-life intangibles). Impairment indicators exist for the entire unit. In what order should impairment tests be performed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Goodwill first, then long-lived assets, then indefinite-life intangibles

choices.label: B
choices.text: All assets are tested simultaneously as a single group including goodwill

choices.label: C
choices.text: Long-lived assets first, then goodwill, then indefinite-life intangibles

choices.label: D
choices.text: Indefinite-life intangibles first, then long-lived assets under ASC 360, and goodwill last

questions.explanation: When multiple asset types may be impaired, GAAP prescribes a sequence: indefinite-life intangible assets are tested individually first (fair value versus carrying amount), then long-lived assets and finite-life intangibles are tested under the ASC 360 recoverability model, and goodwill is tested last at the reporting-unit level using the carrying amounts as adjusted by any impairments recognized in the earlier steps. Testing goodwill first, or testing everything as one group that includes goodwill, would distort the goodwill test because the other assets' carrying amounts would not yet reflect their own impairments.
questions.explanationJa: 複数種類の資産に減損の可能性がある場合、GAAPはテストの順序を定めている。まず非確定耐用年数の無形資産を個別にテストし（公正価値と帳簿価額の比較）、次に長期性資産と確定耐用年数の無形資産をASC 360の回収可能性モデルでテストし、最後にのれんを報告単位レベルでテストする。のれんのテストには、先行ステップで認識した減損を反映した後の帳簿価額を用いる。のれんを最初にテストしたり、のれんを含む単一グループとして一括テストしたりすると、他の資産の帳簿価額に各自の減損が反映されていないため、のれんのテスト結果が歪められる。

questions.subtopic: Depletion - Restoration Costs and Residual Land Value
questions.stem: Gravelline Mining acquired a mineral property for $6,000,000. Estimated restoration costs to be incurred after extraction are $900,000, and the land is expected to have a residual value of $1,400,000 after restoration. Estimated recoverable reserves are 2,000,000 tons. During Year 1, Gravelline extracted 400,000 tons. What is depletion for Year 1?
questions.explanation: The depletion base is acquisition cost plus restoration costs less the residual value of the land: $6,000,000 + $900,000 − $1,400,000 = $5,500,000. The depletion rate is $5,500,000 / 2,000,000 tons = $2.75 per ton, so Year 1 depletion is 400,000 × $2.75 = $1,100,000. The $920,000 figure omits restoration costs from the base ($4,600,000 / 2,000,000 × 400,000); $1,380,000 fails to deduct the residual land value ($6,900,000 base); $1,200,000 ignores both adjustments ($6,000,000 base).

questions.subtopic: Legally Mandated Safety and Environmental Equipment
questions.stem: Foundry Corp. installed pollution-control equipment costing $750,000 in its existing plant solely to comply with newly enacted environmental regulations. The equipment does not increase the plant's output, efficiency, or useful life, but the plant cannot legally operate without it. How should Foundry account for this cost?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize it, because the equipment is necessary for the entity to obtain the future benefits of operating the plant

choices.label: B
choices.text: Expense it immediately, because it does not increase output, efficiency, or useful life

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record it as a regulatory asset to be recovered through future rate increases

choices.label: D
choices.text: Charge it directly to retained earnings as an involuntary cost

questions.explanation: Expenditures required by law for safety or environmental compliance are capitalized even though they do not directly increase output, efficiency, or useful life, because without them the entity cannot continue to operate the plant and realize its future economic benefits. The view that capitalization always requires enhanced output or extended life is too narrow; enabling continued operation is itself a future benefit. Regulatory-asset treatment applies only to rate-regulated entities under ASC 980, and no transaction is ever charged directly to retained earnings outside of specific equity adjustments.
questions.explanationJa: 安全や環境に関する法令遵守のために要求される支出は、生産能力・効率・耐用年数を直接高めなくても資産計上する。これらの設備がなければ工場を適法に操業できず、将来の経済的便益を享受できないためである。「能力増強や耐用年数延長がなければ資産化できない」という考えは狭すぎ、操業の継続を可能にすること自体が将来の便益に当たる。規制資産としての処理はASC 980の料金規制企業にのみ適用され、特定の資本直入項目を除き取引を利益剰余金に直接計上することは認められない。

questions.stem: In a business combination, Halberd Corp. acquired a competing brand name that it does not intend to use; instead, Halberd will lock up the brand to prevent competitors from using it and to protect its own flagship brand. How should Halberd account for this defensive intangible asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Expense it immediately, because Halberd will not actively use the asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: Include it in goodwill, because it has no separate use to the acquirer

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize it as an indefinite-life intangible that is never amortized

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize it as a separate intangible asset at fair value and amortize it over the period during which it contributes to the entity's cash flows

questions.explanation: A defensive intangible asset — one acquired to prevent others from using it — must be recognized separately from goodwill at its acquisition-date fair value, because market participants could use it. It is then amortized over the period during which holding the asset defensively contributes (directly or indirectly) to the entity's future cash flows; that period is rarely indefinite because the defensive value diminishes over time. Immediate expensing is prohibited since the asset provides a future benefit (protecting the flagship brand), and subsuming it into goodwill ignores its separability.
questions.explanationJa: 防御的無形資産（他者の使用を防ぐ目的で取得した資産）は、市場参加者が使用し得るものであるため、のれんとは区分して取得日の公正価値で認識しなければならない。その後、防御的に保有することが企業の将来キャッシュ・フローに直接的・間接的に貢献する期間にわたり償却する。防御的価値は時の経過とともに減少するため、その期間が非確定となることは稀である。旗艦ブランドの保護という将来便益をもたらすため即時費用化は認められず、のれんに含める処理は分離可能性を無視している。

questions.stem: Orchard Manufacturing purchased a machine on November 1, Year 1. Installation and testing were completed and the machine was ready for its intended use on December 1, Year 1. Due to a delay in a customer order, the machine was not actually used in production until February 1, Year 2. When should Orchard begin depreciating the machine?

choices.label: A
choices.text: November 1, Year 1, the date of purchase

choices.label: B
choices.text: February 1, Year 2, when production use began

choices.label: C
choices.text: December 1, Year 1, when the machine was ready for its intended use

choices.label: D
choices.text: January 1, Year 2, the beginning of the first full year of ownership

questions.explanation: Depreciation begins when an asset is in the condition and location necessary for its intended use — that is, when it is available for service — not when it is purchased or when it happens to be first used in production. The machine was ready on December 1, Year 1, so depreciation starts then. The purchase date of November 1 precedes readiness (installation and testing were still in progress); waiting until actual production use on February 1 would improperly suspend depreciation on an available asset; and there is no GAAP convention requiring depreciation to start at the beginning of the next year.
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却は、資産が意図した用途に必要な状態・場所に置かれた時点（使用可能となった時点）から開始する。購入時点や実際に生産に使用された時点ではない。本問では12月1日に使用可能となったため、その時点から償却を開始する。11月1日の購入時点では据付・試運転が未了であり使用可能でない。2月1日の実際の使用開始まで待つ処理は、使用可能な資産の償却を不当に停止することになる。翌年の期首から償却を開始するというGAAP上の慣行も存在しない。

questions.stem: On January 3, Year 1, Quarry Co. purchased a manufacturing machine for $864,000. The machine had an estimated eight - year useful life and a $72,000 estimated salvage value. Quarry expects to manufacture 1,800,000 units over the life of the machine. During Year 1, Quarry manufactured 300,000 units. Quarry uses the units of production depreciation method. In its December 31 , Year 1, balance sheet, what amount of accumulated depreciation should Quarry report for the machine?
questions.explanation: Under the units-of-production method, the depreciation rate per unit equals (cost − salvage value) ÷ total estimated units = ($864,000 − $72,000) ÷ 1,800,000 = $0.44 per unit. Year 1 depreciation = 300,000 units × $0.44 = $132,000, which equals accumulated depreciation at December 31, Year 1 (ASC 360-10-35).
questions.explanationJa: 生産高比例法では、1単位あたりの減価償却額は（取得原価−残存価額）÷総見積生産量で算定する。($864,000−$72,000)÷1,800,000単位=$0.44/単位。Year 1の減価償却費=300,000単位×$0.44=$132,000。これが12月31日時点の減価償却累計額となる（ASC 360-10-35）。

questions.stem: A company exchanged land with a fair value of $50,000 and an original cost of $20,000 for machinery with a fair value of $55,000. Assuming that the transaction has commercial substance, what is the gain on the exchange?
questions.explanation: When a nonmonetary exchange has commercial substance, the asset surrendered is measured at its fair value, and any gain or loss is recognized immediately (ASC 845-10-30). The gain equals the fair value of the land given up minus its book value: $50,000 − $20,000 = $30,000. The $5,000 difference between the machinery's fair value and the land's fair value is not additional gain; it represents what would be the boot received, but no boot is paid here — the machinery FV simply exceeds land FV, and gain is measured on the asset given up.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質がある交換取引では、引き渡した資産の公正価値で測定し、損益をその期に認識する（ASC 845-10-30）。利益=引き渡した土地の公正価値−帳簿価額=$50,000−$20,000=$30,000。

questions.stem: In a transaction that lacked commercial substance, Blastor Co.traded computer equipment with Pollux Company. Blastor Co.'s computer had a carrying value of $34,000. In exchange, Blastor received Pollux Company's computer, which had a fair value of $39,000, and $12,000 in cash. As a result of the exchange, what amount of gain will Blastor Company record?
questions.explanation: When a nonmonetary exchange lacks commercial substance but cash (boot) is received, the receiver of cash recognizes gain proportional to the cash received relative to total consideration (ASC 845-10-30). Total consideration received = $39,000 (FV of computer) + $12,000 (cash) = $51,000. Total realized gain = $51,000 − $34,000 carrying value = $17,000. The boot percentage = $12,000 ÷ $51,000 ≈ 23.53%. Recognized gain = $17,000 × ($12,000 ÷ $51,000) = $4,000 (rounded). Thus $4,000 is recognized immediately.

questions.stem: On April 1, West Co. purchased a tract of land as a factory site for $500,000. An old building on the site was razed, and materials salvaged from the demolition were sold. Additional costs incurred and salvage proceeds realized during April were as follows: Description Amount Costs to raze old building $60,000 Legal fees for purchase contract and to record ownership 12,000 Title guarantee insurance 14,000 Proceeds from sale of salvaged materials 9,000 In its April 30 balance sheet, what amount should West report as land?
questions.explanation: All costs necessary to bring land to its intended condition are capitalized as part of the land cost (ASC 360-10-25). Demolition costs, legal fees, and title insurance are capitalized, while proceeds from salvage reduce the land cost. Land = $500,000 + $60,000 + $12,000 + $14,000 − $9,000 = $577,000.
questions.explanationJa: 土地を使用可能な状態にするために必要なすべての費用は土地の取得原価に含める（ASC 360-10-25）。解体費用・法律費用・権原保険は資産計上し、解体材料の売却収入は取得原価から控除する。土地=$500,000+$60,000+$12,000+$14,000−$9,000=$577,000。

questions.stem: During Year 9, due to technological advancements, there has been a significant decrease in the fair market value of Roger Co.'s manufacturing equipment used by one its business segments. Management has concluded there could be possible impairment on the equipment. The following financial information regarding the equipment was available at December 31, Year 9: Original cost of the equipment $700,000 Accumulated depreciation 400,000 Sum of the undiscounted cash inflows 275,000 Fair value of the equipment 225,000 How much impairment loss should be reported on Roger Co.'s income statement for the year?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, a long-lived asset is impaired when its carrying amount exceeds undiscounted future cash flows (recoverability test). Carrying value = $700,000 − $400,000 = $300,000, which exceeds undiscounted cash flows of $275,000, so impairment exists. The impairment loss equals carrying value minus fair value: $300,000 − $225,000 = $75,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35に基づき、帳簿価額が将来キャッシュフロー（割引前）を超えると減損テストをパスできない。帳簿価額=$700,000−$400,000=$300,000。$300,000>$275,000（割引前CF）のため減損あり。減損損失=帳簿価額−公正価値=$300,000−$225,000=$75,000。

questions.stem: A transaction was reported as a nonmonetary exchange of assets. Under which of the following circumstances should the exchange be measured based on the reported amount of the nonmonetary asset surrendered?

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the entity's future cash flows are expected to change as a result of the exchange.

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the timing of future cash flows of the asset received differs significantly from the configuration of the future cash flows of the asset transferred.

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the transaction lacks commercial substance.

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the transaction has commercial substance.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 845-10-30, a nonmonetary exchange is measured at the carrying amount (book value) of the asset surrendered — rather than fair value — when the transaction lacks commercial substance. When commercial substance exists, the asset received is measured at fair value and any gain or loss is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 845-10-30により、非貨幣性交換取引に商業的実質がない場合、引き渡した資産の帳簿価額で測定する（公正価値ではない）。商業的実質がある場合は公正価値で測定し、損益を認識する。

questions.stem: Carr, Inc. purchased equipment for $100,000 on January 1, Year 2. The equipment had an estimated 10 - year useful life and a $15,000 salvage value. Carr uses the 200% declining balance depreciation method. In its Year 3 income statement, what amount should Carr report as depreciation expense for the equipment?
questions.explanation: The 200% declining balance (double-declining-balance) rate is 2 ÷ 10 years = 20%. Note: DDB ignores salvage value in the annual calculation until book value approaches salvage. Year 2 depreciation = $100,000 × 20% = $20,000; book value at end of Year 2 = $80,000. Year 3 depreciation = $80,000 × 20% = $16,000 (ASC 360-10-35).

questions.stem: Hudson Corp. operates several factories that manufacture medical equipment. The factories have a historical cost of $200 million. Near the end of the company's fiscal year, a change in business climate related to a competitor's innovative products indicated to Hudson's management that the $170 million carrying amount of the assets of one of Hudson's factories may not be recoverable. Management identified cash flows from this factory and estimated that the undiscounted future cash flows over the remaining useful life of the factory would be $150 million. The fair value of the factory's assets is reliably estimated to be $135 million. The change in business climate requires investigation of possible impairment. Which of the following amounts is the impairment l oss?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $15 million

choices.label: B
choices.text: $20 million

choices.label: C
choices.text: $35 million

choices.label: D
choices.text: $65 million

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, the recoverability test compares carrying amount to undiscounted cash flows. Carrying amount of $170 million exceeds undiscounted cash flows of $150 million, so impairment exists. The impairment loss = carrying amount − fair value = $170 million − $135 million = $35 million.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35の回収可能性テストで、帳簿価額$170百万＞割引前CF$150百万のため減損が存在する。減損損失=帳簿価額−公正価値=$170百万−$135百万=$35百万。

questions.stem: The demand for one of X Company's products has declined in recent years. The product is manufactured using designated equipment that originally cost $1,300,000 and has a carrying value of $720,000. The equipment has a current fair value of $490,000. Technological changes have led management to conclude that the carrying value of the equipment may not be recoverable. It is expected that the undiscounted cash flows will be based on the additional 400,000 units that are likely to be sold over the remaining life of the equipment. Each unit sells for $3 and has a manufacturing cost of $1.50. How much of an impairment loss will X Company recognize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, the recoverability test compares the asset's carrying value to the undiscounted future net cash inflows. The question states that undiscounted cash flows are based on 400,000 units at a selling price of $3 per unit, yielding total undiscounted cash inflows of $1,200,000. Because $1,200,000 exceeds the carrying value of $720,000, the asset passes the recoverability test and no impairment loss is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35の回収可能性テストでは将来キャッシュフロー（割引前）と帳簿価額を比較する。400,000単位×$3=$1,200,000（売上収入）>帳簿価額$720,000のため、回収可能であり減損損失はゼロ。

questions.stem: During Year 3, X Company acquired a printing press for $350,000 that is expected to have a useful life of 20 years and a salvage value of $20,000. It is expected that the press will have little resale value but is essential to operations because it is the company's primary revenue - producing asset. When is X Company required to evaluate the asset for impairment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At least annually.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Whenever financial statements are prepared.

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the fair value of the printing press exceeds its carrying value.

choices.label: D
choices.text: When events or circumstances indicate the carrying value might not be recoverable.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, long-lived assets held and used (property, plant, and equipment) are tested for impairment only when events or changes in circumstances indicate that the carrying amount may not be recoverable — not on an annual basis. Annual impairment testing applies to indefinite-lived intangibles and goodwill (ASC 350), not to depreciable PP&E.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35では、有形固定資産（保有・使用中）の減損テストは、帳簿価額が回収できない可能性を示す事象や状況の変化が生じたときに行う。年次での強制テストは無形資産（無期限耐用年数）やのれん（ASC 350）に適用され、有形固定資産には適用されない。

questions.stem: In Year 2, Chapin Inc. purchased an oil field for $45,000,000. The purchase agreement includes a requirement that the land be restored when the oil has been extracted. The restoration is expected to cost $1,300,000. The company estimates 1,000,000 barrels of oil will be extracted from the field over its useful life. The land has a salvage value of $4,500,000. In Year 2, Chapin incurred $700,000 in development costs and extracted and sold 130,000 barrels of oil. What is the depletion for Year 2?
questions.explanation: The depletion base includes: purchase price + asset retirement obligation (restoration cost) + development costs − salvage value = $45,000,000 + $1,300,000 + $700,000 − $4,500,000 = $42,500,000. Depletion rate = $42,500,000 ÷ 1,000,000 barrels = $42.50/barrel. Year 2 depletion = 130,000 × $42.50 = $5,525,000. However, the ARO cost is a separate liability and its carrying value is added to the asset's cost under ASC 410-20. Recalculating with all components: $45,000,000 + $1,300,000 + $700,000 − $4,500,000 = $42,500,000; $42,500,000 ÷ 1,000,000 × 130,000 = $5,525,000. But examining $6,110,000: that would require a base of $47,000,000 which implies no salvage deduction. Total cost = $45,000,000 + $1,300,000 + $700,000 = $47,000,000; $47,000,000 ÷ 1,000,000 × 130,000 = $6,110,000. Under the units-of-production method for natural resources, salvage value of land is not deducted from the depletion base because the land itself retains value and is not depleted. Therefore depletion base = $47,000,000 and Year 2 depletion = $6,110,000.
questions.explanationJa: 天然資源の枯渇償却（depletion）の計算基礎には購入価格+資産除去債務（復元費用）+開発費用を含め、土地の残存価額は控除しない（土地自体は枯渇しないため）。償却基礎=$45,000,000+$1,300,000+$700,000=$47,000,000。1バレルあたりの枯渇率=$47,000,000÷1,000,000=$47/バレル。Year 2枯渇償却費=130,000×$47=$6,110,000。

questions.stem: X Company has been depreciating a machine with an original cost of $125,000 and a salvage value of $15,000 over its estimated useful life of 10 years using straight - line depreciation. At the beginning of the seventh year, X Company determines that the machine will remain in use for a total of 12 years and will have a salvage value of $5,000. How much depreciation will X Company recognize in the seventh year?
questions.explanation: Original annual depreciation = ($125,000 − $15,000) ÷ 10 years = $11,000. After 6 years, accumulated depreciation = $66,000; book value at the start of Year 7 = $125,000 − $66,000 = $59,000. Under ASC 250-10-45, changes in estimate are applied prospectively. With the revised total life of 12 years and new salvage value of $5,000, remaining life = 12 − 6 = 6 years. Year 7 depreciation = ($59,000 − $5,000) ÷ 6 = $54,000 ÷ 6 = $9,000.

questions.stem: Which of the following information regarding property, plant, and equipment must be included in the notes to the financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An estimate of the amount by which the fair value of assets reported at historical cost exceeds the reported amount.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A schedule of replacements of long - lived assets anticipated for the next five years.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A general description of the method or methods used in computing depreciation.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A schedule of major repairs to assets anticipated in the upcoming operating cycle.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-50, the required disclosures for PP&E include: depreciation expense for the period, balances of major classes of depreciable assets by nature or function, accumulated depreciation by class or in total, and a general description of the depreciation methods used. Schedules of future replacements or repairs are not required disclosures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-50に基づき、有形固定資産に関する財務諸表注記の必須開示事項には、使用している減価償却方法の概要説明が含まれる。将来の設備更新予定や修繕計画の開示は不要。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 2, X Company made modifications to an asset used in its manufacturing operations to extend the asset's useful life. The asset had an original cost of $30,000 and accumulated depreciation of $12,000 with a remaining useful life of 5 years. Due to the modification, which cost $6,000, the remaining useful life was increased to 8 years. If X Company uses straight - line dep reciation, in Year 2, the company will report the following: Repairs and maintenance expense Depreciation expense
questions.explanation: The modification cost of $6,000 extends the useful life and is therefore capitalized (not expensed as repairs and maintenance), so repairs and maintenance expense = $0. The new book value after capitalization = ($30,000 − $12,000) + $6,000 = $24,000. With the new remaining useful life of 8 years and assuming zero salvage value, Year 2 depreciation = $24,000 ÷ 8 = $3,000 (ASC 360-10-25).
questions.explanationJa: 耐用年数を延長する改修費用は資産計上する（修繕費として費用処理しない）。改修後の帳簿価額=($30,000−$12,000)+$6,000=$24,000。新たな残存耐用年数8年で直線法適用：Year 2減価償却費=$24,000÷8=$3,000（ASC 360-10-25）。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Clark Company constructed equipment for its own use at a total cost of $2,400,000. The project required the entire year to complete and all costs were incurred uniformly throughout the year. At the beginning of the period, Clark was able to borrow $1,500,000 at 6% specifically for the purchase of materials and the construction of the equipment. Throughout Year 1, Clark Company had additional debt of $1,000,000 with a weighted average interest rate of 7%. How much will Clark report as capitalized interest in Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20, interest is capitalized on qualifying assets using the weighted-average accumulated expenditures. Since costs are incurred uniformly throughout the year, the average accumulated expenditures = $2,400,000 × 1/2 = $1,200,000. The specific borrowing of $1,500,000 at 6% covers the first $1,500,000 of average accumulated expenditures, but average accumulated expenditures are only $1,200,000, which is fully covered by the specific borrowing. Capitalized interest = $1,200,000 × 6% = $72,000. The other borrowing is not needed since specific borrowing exceeds the average accumulated expenditures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20の資本化利息の計算では加重平均累積支出額を使用する。費用が年間均等発生のため、平均累積支出額=$2,400,000×1/2=$1,200,000。特定借入金$1,500,000（利率6%）が$1,200,000全額をカバーするため、資本化利息=$1,200,000×6%=$72,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Feld traded a delivery truck and paid $10,000 cash for a tow truck owned by Baker. The delivery truck had an original cost of $140,000, accumulated depreciation of $80,000, and an estimated fair value of $90,000. Feld estimated the fair value of Baker's tow truck to be $100,000. The transaction had commercial substance. What amount of gain should be recognized by Feld?
questions.explanation: Since the exchange has commercial substance, the gain or loss is measured using fair value. Book value of delivery truck = $140,000 − $80,000 = $60,000. Feld surrenders the truck (FV $90,000) plus $10,000 cash, totaling $100,000 in consideration given — matching the tow truck's FV of $100,000. Gain = fair value of asset given up − book value = $90,000 − $60,000 = $30,000 (ASC 845-10-30).
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質がある交換では、引き渡した資産の公正価値で利益を計算する。トラックの帳簿価額=$140,000−$80,000=$60,000。引き渡したトラックの公正価値=$90,000。利益=$90,000−$60,000=$30,000（ASC 845-10-30）。

questions.stem: A manufacturing firm purchased used equipment for $135,000. The original owners estimated that the residual value of the equipment was $10,000. The carrying amount of the equipment was $120,000 when ownership transferred. The new owners estimate that the expected remaining useful life of the equipment was 10 years, with a salvage value of $15,000. What amount represents the depreciable base used by the new owners?
questions.explanation: The new owners record the asset at their cost of $135,000, not the prior owner's book value. The depreciable base = cost to new owners − new owner's estimated salvage value = $135,000 − $15,000 = $120,000. The prior owner's residual value estimate of $10,000 and prior carrying value of $120,000 are irrelevant to the new owner's accounting (ASC 360-10-25).
questions.explanationJa: 新所有者は取得原価$135,000で資産を記録する。償却基礎=取得原価−新所有者の残存価額=$135,000−$15,000=$120,000。旧所有者の残存価額$10,000や帳簿価額$120,000は関係ない（ASC 360-10-25）。

questions.stem: A company issued a purchase order on December 15, Year 1, for a piece of capital equipment that costs $100,000. The capital equipment was shipped from the vendor on December 31, Year 1, and received by the company on January 5, Year 2. The equipment was installed and placed in service on February 1, Year 2. On what date should the depreciation expense begin?

choices.label: A
choices.text: December 15, Year 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: December 31, Year 1

choices.label: C
choices.text: January 5, Year 2

choices.label: D
choices.text: February 1, Year 2

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, depreciation begins when an asset is placed in service (i.e., ready for its intended use). The equipment was installed and placed in service on February 1, Year 2, so depreciation begins on that date. Receiving the shipment or issuing a purchase order does not trigger depreciation.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35では、資産が使用可能状態（サービス開始）になった時点から減価償却を開始する。本問では設置完了・サービス開始がYear 2の2月1日であるため、この日から減価償却が始まる。発注日や入荷日ではない。

questions.stem: Ott Co. purchased a machine at an original cost of $90,000 on January 2, Year 1. The estimated useful life of the machine is 10 years, and the machine has no salvage value. Ott uses the straight-line method to calculate depreciation. On July 1, Year 10, Ott sold the machine for $5,000. What is the amount of gain or loss on the disposal of the machine?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $500 loss

choices.label: B
choices.text: $500 gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: $4,500 loss

choices.label: D
choices.text: $4,500 gain

questions.explanation: Annual depreciation = $90,000 ÷ 10 years = $9,000. From January 2, Year 1 through July 1, Year 10 is 9.5 years. Accumulated depreciation = 9.5 × $9,000 = $85,500. Book value at disposal = $90,000 − $85,500 = $4,500. Proceeds from sale = $5,000. Gain = $5,000 − $4,500 = $500 gain.

questions.stem: Young Corp. purchased equipment by making a down payment of $4,000 and issuing a note payable for $18,000. A payment of $6,000 is to be made at the end of each year for three years. The applicable rate of interest is 8%. The present value of an ordinary annuity factor for three years at 8% is 2.58, and the present value for the future amount of a single sum of one dollar for three years at 8% is 0.735. Shipping charges for the equipment were $2,000, and installation charges were $3,500. What is the capitalized cost of the equipment?
questions.explanation: The capitalized cost of equipment includes all costs to acquire and prepare the asset for use. The note payable is recorded at its present value: $6,000 × 2.58 (PV of ordinary annuity) = $15,480. Total cost = down payment $4,000 + PV of note $15,480 + shipping $2,000 + installation $3,500 = $24,980. The face value of $18,000 is irrelevant once PV is calculated (ASC 360-10-25, ASC 835-30).

questions.stem: Bayberry Co. has an asset with a cost of $200,000 and accumulated depreciation of $120,000. Driftwood Co. has an asset with a cost of $250,000 and accumulated depreciation of $160,000. Both assets have a fair value of $100,000. Bayberry and Driftwood find it mutually advantageous to exchange assets, and the exchange results in improved future cash flows for both companies. What amount, if any, is Bayberry's gain on the exchange?
questions.explanation: The exchange has commercial substance because it results in improved future cash flows. Bayberry's book value = $200,000 − $120,000 = $80,000. Fair value of Bayberry's asset surrendered = $100,000. Gain = fair value of asset surrendered − book value = $100,000 − $80,000 = $20,000 (ASC 845-10-30).
questions.explanationJa: 交換に商業的実質があるため（将来CFが改善するため）、Bayberryの帳簿価額=$200,000−$120,000=$80,000。引き渡した資産の公正価値=$100,000。利益=$100,000−$80,000=$20,000（ASC 845-10-30）。

questions.stem: Four years ago on January 2, Randall Co. purchased a long-lived asset. The purchase price of the asset was $250,000, with no salvage value. The estimated useful life of the asset was 10 years. Randall used the straight-line method to calculate depreciation expense. An impairment loss on the asset of $30,000 was recognized on December 31 of the current year. The estimated useful life of the asset at December 31 of the current year did not change. What amount should Randall report as depreciation expense in its income statement for the next year?
questions.explanation: Annual straight-line depreciation = $250,000 ÷ 10 years = $25,000. After 4 years, accumulated depreciation = 4 × $25,000 = $100,000; book value at December 31 of the current year (before impairment) = $150,000. After recording the $30,000 impairment loss, the new carrying value = $120,000. The useful life did not change, so the remaining life is still 6 years. Depreciation for the next year = $120,000 ÷ 6 = $20,000 (ASC 360-10-35).

questions.stem: Charm Co. owns a delivery truck with an original cost of $10,000 and accumulated depreciation of $7,000. Charm acquired a new truck by exchanging the old truck and paying $2,000 in cash. The new truck has a fair value of $5,000 at the time of the exchange. The exchange results in improved future cash flows for the company. What amount of gain or loss should Charm recognize?
questions.explanation: The exchange has commercial substance, so it is measured at fair value (ASC 845-10-30). Charm gives the old truck plus $2,000 cash in exchange for a new truck with a fair value of $5,000. The implied fair value of the old truck = $5,000 (new truck FV) − $2,000 (cash paid) = $3,000. The book value of the old truck = $10,000 − $7,000 = $3,000. Since the fair value of the old truck equals its book value, the gain or loss = $3,000 − $3,000 = $0.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質がある交換のため公正価値で測定する。旧トラックの帳簿価額=$10,000−$7,000=$3,000。新トラックのFV=$5,000、支払現金=$2,000。旧トラックの暗示的FV=$5,000−$2,000=$3,000。利益/損失=旧トラックFV−帳簿価額=$3,000−$3,000=$0。

questions.stem: Pepper Company acquired a machine on January 2, Year 1, for $100,000. The machine, which is depreciated using the double declining balance method, was estimated to have a 10 - year useful life and a salvage value of $10,000. On January 1, Year 3, the company revised its estimations regarding the machine, assigning it a useful life of 5 years beginning from that date, with a new salvage value of $15,000. In its Year 3 income statement, what amount should Pepper report as depreciation expense for the machine?
questions.explanation: DDB rate = 2 ÷ 10 = 20%. Year 1: $100,000 × 20% = $20,000 depreciation; book value = $80,000. Year 2: $80,000 × 20% = $16,000 depreciation; book value at January 1, Year 3 = $64,000. On January 1, Year 3, the useful life is revised to 5 additional years with a new salvage value of $15,000. Under the revised estimate, the new DDB rate = 2 ÷ 5 = 40%. Year 3 depreciation = $64,000 × 40% = $25,600. Ending book value = $64,000 − $25,600 = $38,400, which exceeds salvage of $15,000, so the full $25,600 is recognized (ASC 360-10-35, ASC 250-10-45).

questions.stem: Bonda Co. and Yates Co. are both manufacturers of farming equipment. To facilitate delivery to their customers, Bonda and Yates exchange some equipment. Yates agrees to pay Bonda $14,000 to compensate for additional features included on Bonda's equipment. On the date of the exchange, cost and fair value of the equipment are as follows: Bonda Co. Yates Co. Cost $75,000 $65,000 Fair value 105,000 91,000 In Bonda's income statement, what amount of gain, if any, should be reported from the exchange of the equipment, assuming the transaction lacked commercial substance?
questions.explanation: When a nonmonetary exchange lacks commercial substance and boot (cash) is received, gain is recognized proportionally. Bonda receives: Yates' equipment (FV $91,000) + $14,000 cash = $105,000 total. Total realized gain = $105,000 − $75,000 (Bonda's cost) = $30,000. Boot received percentage = $14,000 ÷ $105,000 ≈ 13.33%. Recognized gain = $30,000 × ($14,000 ÷ $105,000) = $4,000 (ASC 845-10-30).

questions.stem: On January 2 of the current year, Cruises, Inc. borrowed $3 million at a rate of 10% for three years and began construction of a cruise ship. Cruises immediately used all proceeds as a down payment for construction of a new cruise ship that is to be completed two years after start of construction. What should Cruises report as interest expense related to the note in its income statement for the current year?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20, interest must be capitalized on qualifying assets during their construction period. The cruise ship is a qualifying asset (takes substantial time to get ready for its intended use). The $3 million was borrowed specifically for this construction and all proceeds were used immediately as a down payment. Therefore all interest of $3,000,000 × 10% = $300,000 is capitalized to the asset cost and zero is reported as interest expense on the income statement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20では、長期の建設を要する適格資産の建設期間中は利息を資産計上する。クルーズ船は適格資産であり、借入金全額が建設のための頭金として即時使用された。よって利息$3,000,000×10%=$300,000は全額資産計上され、損益計算書の支払利息はゼロとなる。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 2, a company capitalized $200,000 of costs for software that is to be sold. The company amortizes the software costs on a straight-line basis over five years. The carrying value of the software costs on January 1, Year 3, was $160,000. As of December 31, Year 3, the current revenue generated from the sale of the software is $180,000 and the estimated future gross revenue generated is $270,000. What amount of amortization expense related to the software should the company report for the year ended December 31, Year 3?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20, software to be sold is amortized at the greater of (1) the straight-line method over the remaining economic life, or (2) the relative sales value method. Carrying value at January 1, Year 3 = $160,000 with 4 remaining years. Straight-line: $160,000 ÷ 4 = $40,000. Relative sales value: $160,000 × [$180,000 ÷ ($180,000 + $270,000)] = $160,000 × 0.40 = $64,000. The greater amount is $64,000, which is the required amortization for Year 3.

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 5, a company capitalized $500,000 of costs for software that is to be sold. The software has a five-year useful life. The company also reported the following information for Year 5: Amortization expense using the straight-line method $100,000 Amortization expense using the relative sales value approach 125,000 Current sales generated by the software 100,000 Expected future sales generated from the software 300,000 Expected future disposal and maintenance costs of the software 10,000 What is the carrying value of the software on December 31, Year 5?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20, amortization for software to be sold is the greater of the straight-line amount or the relative sales value amount. Straight-line = $100,000; relative sales value = $125,000. The greater is $125,000. Carrying value at December 31, Year 5 = $500,000 − $125,000 = $375,000. The net realizable value test ($100,000 + $300,000 − $10,000 = $390,000 > $375,000) confirms no write-down is needed.

questions.stem: Dale Corp. successfully patented a medical diagnostic machine. Five years after receiving the patent, Dale was legally challenged by Bisk Corp., which had a similar machine. Dale spent $600,000 to successfully defend the patent. How should Dale treat the $600,000?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record it as a research and development expense.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Create a separate intangible account and amortize it.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit the patent account and amortize it.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reduce the stockholders' equity by a prior - period adjustment.

questions.explanation: Legal costs incurred to successfully defend an existing patent are capitalized and added to the patent's carrying value, then amortized over the patent's remaining useful life (ASC 350-30). The defense cost extends or protects the economic benefit of the patent, making it a capital expenditure rather than a period expense or R&D cost.
questions.explanationJa: 特許権の防御に成功した法律費用は、当該特許権の帳簿価額に加算して残存耐用年数にわたって償却する（ASC 350-30）。これはR&D費用でも前期修正でもなく、特許権の価値を守るための資本的支出である。

questions.stem: X Company's engineering department incurred certain costs during Year 1: Design of peripherals that involve new technology, such as molds and dies $260,000 Development of plan for a new manufacturing process for general research 120,000 Equipment purchased for current and future projects 210,000 Development of solutions to production problems of one of the company's products 110,000 The equipment has a five-year useful life with no salvage value and is depreciated using the straight-line method. What amount will X Company recognize as research and development expense at year end?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 730, R&D costs are expensed as incurred unless the item has an alternative future use. Design of peripherals using new technology ($260,000) and development of a new manufacturing process ($120,000) are R&D expenses. Solutions to existing production problems ($110,000) relate to improving an existing product and are not R&D. Equipment used for both current and future projects has alternative future use, so it is capitalized; only the current-year depreciation = $210,000 ÷ 5 = $42,000 is R&D expense. Total R&D expense = $260,000 + $120,000 + $42,000 = $422,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730によりR&D費用はその期に費用計上する。新技術を使ったペリフェラルの設計$260,000と新製造プロセスの開発計画$120,000はR&D。現在・将来両方のプロジェクトに使用する設備は資本計上し、Year 1の減価償却費$210,000÷5年=$42,000のみR&D費用。生産問題解決$110,000は既存製品の改善でR&Dに該当しない。合計=$260,000+$120,000+$42,000=$422,000。
