questions.stem: On September 29, Year 5, Wall Co. paid $860,000 for all the issued and outstanding common stock of Hart Corp. On that date, the carrying amounts of Hart's recorded assets and liabilities were $800,000 and $180,000, respectively. Hart's recorded assets and liabilities had fair values of $840,000 and $140,000, respectively. In Wall's September 30, Year 5, balance sheet, what amount should be reported as goodwill?
questions.explanation: Goodwill = purchase price − fair value of net identifiable assets acquired (ASC 805). Fair value of net assets = FV of assets ($840,000) − FV of liabilities ($140,000) = $700,000. Goodwill = $860,000 − $700,000 = $160,000. Book values are irrelevant for goodwill calculation under the acquisition method.
questions.explanationJa: のれん=購入価格−識別可能純資産の公正価値（ASC 805）。純資産の公正価値=$840,000−$140,000=$700,000。のれん=$860,000−$700,000=$160,000。帳簿価額はのれん計算に使用しない。

questions.stem: Wind Co. incurred organization costs of $6,000 at the beginning of its first year of operations. How should Wind treat the organization costs in its financial statements in accordance with U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Capitalize as an indefinite-life asset.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Amortize over five years.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Amortize over 40 years.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Expense immediately.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 720-15, start-up costs (including organization costs) are expensed as incurred under U.S. GAAP. This is a departure from prior guidance that allowed amortization over up to 40 years. The five-year amortization option applies under tax rules (IRC Section 195), not U.S. GAAP financial reporting.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 720-15（旧SOP 98-5）により、開業費（組織設立費用を含む）は発生時に費用計上する。かつての40年以内での償却や税務上の5年償却とは異なり、US GAAPでは即時費用計上が唯一の処理方法である。

questions.stem: Which of the following methods should be used to account for research and development costs with no alternative future use?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Charging all costs to expense when incurred.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Capitalizing all costs when incurred.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalizing costs specified by management and charging all other costs to expense.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Accumulating all costs in a separate component of stockholders' equity until the existence of future benefits can be determined.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 730-10, all research and development costs with no alternative future use must be expensed as incurred. This treatment is mandatory — R&D costs cannot be capitalized, deferred, or accumulated in stockholders' equity under U.S. GAAP, regardless of the likelihood of future economic benefits.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730-10により、代替的将来使用価値のないR&D費用はすべて発生時に費用計上しなければならない。資本計上、繰延、または株主持分への蓄積は認められない。

questions.stem: On December 31, 20X3, Moon, Inc. authorized Luna Co. to operate as a franchisee for an initial franchise fee of $100,000. Luna paid $40,000 on signing the agreement and signed an interest-free note to pay the balance in three annual installments of $20,000 each, beginning December 31, 20X4. On December 31, 20X3, the present value of the note, appropriately discounted, is $48,000. Services for the initial fee will be performed in 20X4. In its December 31, 20X3, balance sheet, what amount should Moon report as unearned franchise fees?
questions.explanation: The franchise fee is recognized as revenue only when the services are performed (in 20X4). At December 31, 20X3, the total consideration received or receivable that has not yet been earned is recorded as unearned franchise fees. The cash received = $40,000 and the PV of the note = $48,000, totaling $88,000. The full $100,000 face amount is not used; the note is measured at present value per ASC 835-30. Thus unearned franchise fees = $40,000 + $48,000 = $88,000.
questions.explanationJa: フランチャイズ手数料はサービス提供時（20X4年）に収益認識する。20X3年12月31日時点では未収益フランチャイズ料として計上する。受取現金$40,000+手形の現在価値$48,000=$88,000（手形の額面$60,000ではなく現在価値を使用、ASC 835-30）。

questions.stem: In Year 1, X Company recognized an impairment loss on the trade name for its beverage product, reducing the carrying value from $485,000 to $350,000. During Year 3, the trade name's fair value rose to $650,000. While preparing its Year 3 financial statements, X company has determined that the year end fair value of the trade name is $500,000. What amount will X Company report for the trade name on its Year 3 balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under U.S. GAAP (ASC 350-30), impairment losses on intangible assets (other than goodwill) are not reversed — once an impairment is recognized, the written-down carrying value becomes the new cost basis. Even if the fair value subsequently recovers to $500,000 or $650,000, the trade name remains at its post-impairment carrying value of $350,000 (less any subsequent amortization if applicable). The reversal of impairment losses is prohibited under U.S. GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAP（ASC 350-30）では、無形資産（のれん以外）の減損損失は戻し入れを認めない。減損後の帳簿価額$350,000が新たな取得原価基準となり、その後公正価値が回復しても帳簿価額は$350,000のまま（その後の償却があれば控除後）。減損の戻し入れはUS GAAPで禁止されている。

questions.stem: X Company acquired the following equipment on January 2 of the current year for its engineering department's research and development activities. Computer equipment used for current and future projects $320,000 Storage equipment used for current year projects only 240,000 Machine used for current year project, then in manufacturing operations 180,000 All of the equipment has a five-year useful life with no salvage value and is depreciated using the straight- line method. What amount should X recognize as research and development expense at year end?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 730, equipment acquired for R&D is expensed in full if it has no alternative future use; if it has an alternative future use, it is capitalized and only annual depreciation is included in R&D expense. Computer equipment (current and future R&D projects) has alternative future use: capitalized, R&D expense = $320,000 ÷ 5 = $64,000. Storage equipment (current year only, no future use): expensed in full at $240,000. Machine (R&D then manufacturing operations) has alternative future use: capitalized, R&D expense = $180,000 ÷ 5 = $36,000. Total R&D expense = $64,000 + $240,000 + $36,000 = $340,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730に基づき、代替的将来使用価値の有無で処理が異なる。コンピュータ機器（現在・将来両方）→資本計上、R&D費用は減価償却費$320,000÷5=$64,000。保管機器（現年のみ）→代替使用なし→$240,000全額費用計上。製造設備（R&D後、製造に転用）→代替使用あり→資本計上、R&D費用は$180,000÷5=$36,000。合計=$64,000+$240,000+$36,000=$340,000。

questions.stem: Which of the following is an intangible asset that is subject to the recoverability test?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A patent.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Goodwill.

choices.label: C
choices.text: R&D costs for a patent.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A trademark with indefinite useful life.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, the recoverability test (comparing carrying value to undiscounted future cash flows) applies to long-lived assets held and used with finite useful lives, such as patents. Goodwill and indefinite-lived intangibles (trademarks with indefinite life) are tested for impairment under ASC 350 using a fair-value-based test — not the recoverability test. R&D costs are expensed as incurred and are not capitalized assets subject to impairment testing.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35の回収可能性テスト（割引前将来CFと帳簿価額の比較）は、有限耐用年数を持つ長期資産（特許権など）に適用される。のれんおよび無期限耐用年数の無形資産（無期限商標権）はASC 350の公正価値ベースのテストで評価する。R&D費用は資本計上されないため減損テストの対象外。

questions.stem: Which of the following expenditures qualifies for asset capitalization?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cost of materials used in prototype testing.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Costs of testing a prototype and modifying its design.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Salaries of engineering staff developing a new product.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Legal costs associated with obtaining a patent on a new product.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 730, R&D costs — including materials used in prototypes, prototype testing, design modifications, and engineering salaries related to new product development — are expensed as incurred. However, legal costs directly associated with obtaining a patent (filing fees, attorney fees) are capitalized as an intangible asset (ASC 350-30) because they represent the cost to acquire a legal right.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730により、試作品材料費・プロトタイプテスト費・設計修正費・エンジニア給与等のR&D費用は発生時に費用計上する。一方、特許権取得に直接関連する法律費用（出願費・弁護士費用）は無形資産として資本計上する（ASC 350-30）。これは法的権利の取得コストに該当するためである。

questions.stem: Northstar Co. acquired a registered trademark for $600,000. The trademark has a remaining legal life of five years but can be renewed every 10 years for a nominal fee. Northstar expects to renew the trademark indefinitely. What amount of amortization expense should Northstar record for the trademark in the current year?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-30, intangible assets with indefinite useful lives are not amortized. Since Northstar intends to renew the trademark indefinitely at a nominal cost, the trademark has an indefinite useful life and is not subject to amortization. Instead, it is tested for impairment annually (ASC 350-30-35). The legal life of five years is irrelevant when renewal is expected indefinitely.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-30では、耐用年数が無期限の無形資産は償却しない。Northstarが名目的なコストで商標権を無期限に更新する予定のため、耐用年数は無期限と判断され、償却は行わない。代わりに年次で減損テストを実施する（ASC 350-30-35）。法定存続期間5年は無期限更新の意図があれば無関係。

questions.stem: A company began developing computer software to be sold as a separate product on January 1, Year 1. During the planning, coding, and testing phases, the company incurred $1,300,000 of costs. On June 30, Year 1, the product was determined to be technologically feasible. The company began producing product masters of the software and incurred an additional $750,000 of costs from July 1, Year 1, through September 30, Year 1. After the software was available for release on October 1, Year 1, the company incurred an additional $275,000 of costs relating to maintenance and customer support. What amount of software-related costs should be capitalized?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20 (software to be sold), costs incurred prior to establishing technological feasibility ($1,300,000) are expensed as R&D. Costs incurred from technological feasibility to the product being available for release ($750,000) are capitalized. Post-release costs for maintenance and customer support ($275,000) are expensed when incurred. Therefore only $750,000 is capitalized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 985-20により、販売用ソフトウェアは技術的実行可能性の確立前のコスト($1,300,000)はR&D費用として費用計上。技術的実行可能性確立後からリリース可能日までのコスト($750,000)は資本計上。リリース後の維持・顧客サポートコスト($275,000)は発生時費用計上。よって資本計上額は$750,000のみ。

questions.stem: Which of the following statements is correct concerning start-up costs?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Costs of start-up activities, including organization costs, should be expensed as incurred.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Costs of start-up activities, including organization costs, should be capitalized and expensed only if an impairment exists.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Costs of start-up activities, including organization costs, should be capitalized and amortized on a straight-line basis over the lesser of the estimated economic life of the company or 60 months.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Costs of start-up activities should be capitalized and amortized on a straight-line basis over the lesser of the estimated economic life of the company or 60 months, while organization costs should be expensed as incurred.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 720-15, all start-up costs — including organization costs — must be expensed as incurred under U.S. GAAP. There is no option to capitalize, defer, or amortize start-up costs. The 60-month amortization described in some choices reflects outdated guidance or tax accounting rules, not current U.S. GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 720-15により、開業費（組織設立費用を含む全ての開業活動コスト）はUS GAAPでは発生時に費用計上しなければならない。資本計上・繰延・償却は認められない。60ヶ月償却は過去の会計基準や税務ルールであり、現行US GAAPには存在しない。

questions.stem: Tech Co. bought a trademark on January 2, Year 2. The intangible was being amortized over its useful life of 40 years. The carrying value at the beginning of Year 2 was $38,000. It was determined during the current year that the cash flow from the trademark will be generated indefinitely at the current level. What amount should Tech report as amortization expense on its December 31, Year 4, income statement?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-30, when a company determines that an intangible asset previously assigned a finite useful life now has an indefinite useful life, amortization ceases and the asset is reclassified as an indefinite-lived intangible. The question states this determination was made 'during the current year' (Year 4). Going forward (including Year 4's income statement), no amortization is recorded. The asset is instead tested annually for impairment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-30により、有限耐用年数で償却していた無形資産の耐用年数が無期限と判断された場合、その時点から償却を中止し無期限耐用年数資産に再分類する。Year 4（当年）にこの判断がなされたため、Year 4の損益計算書に計上する償却費はゼロ。以後は年次減損テストを実施する。

questions.stem: Crestman Co. exchanged inventory with Dandy Co. in a transaction that lacks commercial substance. Crestman's inventory had a cost of $50,000 and a fair value of $80,000. Crestman received inventory with a fair value of $56,000 plus $24,000 cash. What amount of gain should Crestman recognize on this exchange?
questions.explanation: When an exchange lacks commercial substance, gains are generally deferred; however, when monetary consideration (cash) is received, a proportional gain is recognized. Total gain = fair value received ($80,000) − book value given ($50,000) = $30,000. Proportion of cash received = $24,000 ÷ $80,000 = 30%. Recognized gain = 30% × $30,000 = $9,000.
questions.explanationJa: 商業的実質を欠く交換では、原則として利益は繰り延べますが、対価の一部として現金を受け取る場合は、その割合に応じた利益を認識します。総利益＝受取公正価値($80,000)－帳簿価額($50,000)＝$30,000。現金受取割合＝$24,000÷$80,000＝30%。認識利益＝30%×$30,000＝$9,000。

questions.stem: A company moved its operations to a new building. The old building was actively marketed for sale and is expected to be sold within 4 months. Each of the following statements is correct regarding the old building, EXCEPT:

choices.label: A
choices.text: It will be reclassified as an asset held for sale.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It will be classified as a current asset.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It will no longer be depreciated.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It will be valued at historical cost.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-45, when a long-lived asset meets held-for-sale criteria (actively marketed, expected sale within 12 months), it is reclassified as an asset held for sale, classified as a current asset, and depreciation ceases. However, the asset is NOT valued at historical cost; it is measured at the lower of its carrying amount or fair value less costs to sell. Valuing at historical cost is therefore incorrect.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-45に基づき、長期資産が売却保有分類の要件（積極的な売却活動、12ヶ月以内の売却見込み）を満たす場合、売却目的保有資産に再分類され、流動資産として表示され、減価償却が中止されます。ただし、帳簿価額（歴史的原価ではなく）と公正価値から売却費用を差し引いた金額のいずれか低い方で評価されます。歴史的原価での評価は誤りです。

questions.stem: Cole Co. is constructing a building. During construction, interest on specific construction debt amounted to $50,000. Interest on other borrowings amounted to $20,000. The interest computed on the weighted-average accumulated expenditures was $40,000. How much interest should Cole capitalize?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20, the amount of interest to capitalize equals the lesser of actual interest incurred or avoidable interest. Actual total interest = $50,000 (specific debt) + $20,000 (other borrowings) = $70,000. Avoidable interest, computed on the weighted-average accumulated expenditures, is $40,000. Since $40,000 < $70,000, Cole capitalizes $40,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20に基づき、資本化利息＝実際発生利息と回避可能利息のいずれか少ない方です。実際発生利息＝$50,000（特定借入）＋$20,000（一般借入）＝$70,000。加重平均累積支出額に基づく回避可能利息＝$40,000。$40,000<$70,000のため、$40,000を資本化します。

questions.stem: Ocean Corp. carried comprehensive insurance on its waterfront warehouse. The warehouse was destroyed by a storm. The insurance policy had a deductible of $50,000. Dismantling cost was $20,000. The replacement cost of the warehouse was $1,100,000 and its carrying amount was $300,000. What amount should Ocean report as a gain, presented as a separate component of income from continuing operations?
questions.explanation: The insurance proceeds equal the replacement cost less the deductible: $1,100,000 − $50,000 = $1,050,000. The gain on involuntary conversion = insurance proceeds − carrying amount − dismantling costs = $1,050,000 − $300,000 − $20,000 = $730,000. This gain is reported as a separate component of income from continuing operations.
questions.explanationJa: 保険金収入＝取替原価－免責額＝$1,100,000－$50,000＝$1,050,000。非自発的転換による利益＝保険金収入－帳簿価額－解体費用＝$1,050,000－$300,000－$20,000＝$730,000。この利益は継続事業からの利益の独立した構成要素として報告します。

questions.stem: A company's fiscal year ends June 30. Construction began April 1. Construction-in-progress balances were: April 30 = $500,000; May 31 = $800,000; June 30 = $1,500,000. The applicable interest rate is 5% for the April–June period. What amount of interest should be capitalized at June 30?
questions.explanation: The weighted-average accumulated expenditures are approximated using the average of the three month-end CIP balances: ($500,000 + $800,000 + $1,500,000) ÷ 3 = $933,333. Capitalized interest for the three-month period (April–June) at 5% = $933,333 × 5% × 3/12 = $11,667 (rounded to $11,666).

questions.stem:
Quick Co. acquired several assets from a competitor for a lump sum of $200,000. The fair values of the individual assets were:

| Asset | Fair Value |
|---|---:|
| Inventory | $50,000 |
| Land | $50,000 |
| Building | $150,000 |

What amount should Quick record as the cost of the building?
questions.explanation: When multiple assets are acquired for a lump-sum price, the cost is allocated based on relative fair values. Total fair value = $50,000 + $50,000 + $150,000 = $250,000. Building's proportion = $150,000 ÷ $250,000 = 60%. Cost allocated to building = 60% × $200,000 = $120,000.
questions.explanationJa: 一括購入した複数資産の原価は、各資産の公正価値の比率に基づいて配分します。公正価値合計＝$50,000＋$50,000＋$150,000＝$250,000。建物の割合＝$150,000÷$250,000＝60%。建物に配分される原価＝60%×$200,000＝$120,000。

questions.stem: Finch Co. had an asset retirement obligation (ARO) balance at the beginning of the year of $257,000. During the year, Finch acquired new obligations with a discounted value of $68,000, paid $87,000 toward settlement of existing obligations, and recorded accretion expense of $26,000. What is Finch's ARO balance at year end?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 410-20, the asset retirement obligation balance changes as follows: beginning balance $257,000, plus new obligations at fair value (discounted) $68,000, minus settlements paid $87,000, plus accretion expense $26,000 = $257,000 + $68,000 − $87,000 + $26,000 = $264,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20に基づき、資産除去債務残高の変動：期首残高$257,000＋新規取得（割引現在価値）$68,000－支払決済額$87,000＋利息費用（アクリーション）$26,000＝$264,000。

questions.stem: Sen Inc. incurred the following costs related to land: Purchase price $600,000 (includes existing building valued at 10% of purchase price); Demolition of existing building $15,000; Debris removal and grading $8,000; Fine for failure to obtain demolition permit $2,000; Surveying $12,000; Excavation for basement of new building $20,000; Building permits $10,000. What amount should Sen capitalize to land?
questions.explanation: Land costs include the purchase price ($600,000), demolition of the existing structure ($15,000), debris removal and grading ($8,000), and surveying ($12,000), totaling $635,000. The fine for failure to obtain a demolition permit ($2,000) is not capitalizable because it provides no future economic benefit. Excavation for the new building's basement ($20,000) and building permits ($10,000) are costs of the new building, not the land.
questions.explanationJa: 土地原価には、取得価格$600,000、既存建物の取壊費$15,000、がれき撤去・整地費$8,000、測量費$12,000が含まれ合計$635,000です。解体許可未取得の罰金$2,000は将来の経済的便益を生まないため資本化できません。新建物の地下掘削費$20,000と建築許可証$10,000は建物の取得原価です。

questions.stem: Both an asset group and goodwill assigned to a reporting unit need impairment testing. Which of the following statements is correct regarding the order and treatment of impairment testing?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The other asset group should be tested for an impairment loss before goodwill is tested.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Impairment testing may be conducted concurrently.

choices.label: C
choices.text: If the other asset group is impaired, the loss should not be recognized prior to goodwill being tested.

choices.label: D
choices.text: If goodwill is impaired, the loss should be recognized prior to testing the other assets.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20-35, when both other long-lived asset groups and goodwill in a reporting unit require impairment testing, the other asset group must be tested and any impairment loss recognized first. This is because reducing the asset group's carrying amount affects the overall net assets of the reporting unit, which then feeds into the goodwill impairment test.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-20-35に基づき、長期資産グループとのれんの両方について減損テストが必要な場合、まず長期資産グループの減損テストを実施し、減損損失があれば先に認識します。これは、資産グループの帳簿価額の減少が報告単位の純資産に影響し、その後ののれん減損テストの前提となるためです。

questions.stem: Newt Co. sold a warehouse that had been used in its operations and used the proceeds to purchase a new warehouse. The excess of the proceeds over the carrying amount of the old warehouse should be reported as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Reduction of the cost of the new warehouse.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Gain from discontinued operations, net of income taxes.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Part of continuing operations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Part of other comprehensive income.

questions.explanation: The gain on the sale of a warehouse used in operations is recognized in income from continuing operations in the period of sale. There is no basis under U.S. GAAP to net the gain against the cost of the replacement asset, report it as discontinued operations (which requires disposal of a component of an entity), or include it in other comprehensive income.
questions.explanationJa: 事業で使用していた倉庫の売却益は、売却が行われた期間の継続事業からの収益として認識します。米国GAAPでは、売却益を新資産の取得原価から相殺したり、非継続事業（事業の構成部分の処分が必要）として報告したり、その他包括利益に含めたりする根拠はありません。

questions.stem:
Are restorations of carrying value permitted for long-lived assets if fair value increases after an impairment loss has been recognized?

                Held-for-use    Held-for-disposal

choices.label: A
choices.text: Yes               No

choices.label: B
choices.text: No                Yes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Yes               Yes

choices.label: D
choices.text: No                No

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, impairment losses on long-lived assets held for use are not reversed — no restoration of carrying value is permitted. For long-lived assets classified as held for sale (held for disposal), subsequent increases in fair value less costs to sell are recognized as a gain, but only up to the amount of the previously recognized impairment loss. Therefore, restoration is not permitted for held-for-use assets but is permitted for held-for-disposal assets.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35に基づき、使用目的で保有する長期資産の減損損失は戻入れできません。一方、売却目的保有（処分目的保有）に分類された資産については、公正価値（売却費用控除後）の上昇を利益として認識できますが、過去に認識した減損損失の累計額を上限とします。したがって、使用目的保有資産は回復不可、売却目的保有資産は回復可能です。

questions.stem: A company received donated nonmonetary assets in a nonreciprocal transfer. Which of the following statements is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The donated assets are reported as part of operating income.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The donated assets are recorded at the historical cost of the asset received.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The donated assets are recorded at the fair value of the asset received.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The donated assets result in recognition of a gain or loss.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 845-10-30, nonmonetary assets received in a nonreciprocal transfer (donation) are recorded at the fair value of the asset received at the date of the donation. They are not recorded at the donor's historical cost, and the credit is to revenue or contribution income rather than a gain or loss.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 845-10-30に基づき、非互恵的移転（寄付）で受け取った非金融資産は、寄付日の公正価値で記録します。寄付者の帳簿価額では記録せず、対応する貸方は損益（利益・損失）ではなく収益または贈与収入として計上します。

questions.stem: A company borrowed $300,000 at 10% on January 1, Year 1, specifically to finance construction of an office building. On January 1, the company paid $120,000 for land on which to build. On September 1, a progress payment of $150,000 was made to the contractor. What amount of interest should be capitalized for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20, interest is capitalized on weighted-average accumulated expenditures for qualifying assets. The land ($120,000 on January 1) is outstanding the full 12 months; the September 1 progress payment ($150,000) is outstanding 4 months. Weighted-average accumulated expenditures = $120,000 × 12/12 + $150,000 × 4/12 = $120,000 + $50,000 = $170,000. Capitalized interest = $170,000 × 10% = $17,000, which is less than actual interest of $30,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20に基づき、利息は適格資産の加重平均累積支出額に対して資本化します。土地代$120,000（1月1日支払）は12ヶ月間計上、工事代金$150,000（9月1日支払）は4ヶ月間計上。加重平均累積支出額＝$120,000×12/12＋$150,000×4/12＝$120,000＋$50,000＝$170,000。資本化利息＝$170,000×10%＝$17,000（実際利息$30,000より少ない）。

questions.stem: A company placed an offshore oil platform into service. The platform cost $400 million and must be dismantled in 10 years at an estimated cost of $20 million. The discounted present value of the dismantling obligation at the credit-adjusted risk-free rate is $9 million. What liability and what expense should be recorded when the platform is placed in service?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Liability $0 / Expense $0

choices.label: B
choices.text: Liability $9,000,000 / Expense $0

choices.label: C
choices.text: Liability $9,000,000 / Expense $9,000,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Liability $20,000,000 / Expense $20,000,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 410-20, an asset retirement obligation (ARO) is initially measured at fair value — the discounted present value of $9,000,000 — and recorded as a liability. The corresponding debit increases the carrying amount of the asset (not an expense). Therefore, at initial recognition, a liability of $9,000,000 is recorded and no expense is recognized; the $9,000,000 is capitalized into the asset and will be depreciated over the asset's useful life.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20に基づき、資産除去債務（ARO）は公正価値（割引現在価値$9,000,000）で最初に認識し、負債として計上します。対応する借方は資産の帳簿価額の増加（費用ではない）です。したがって、当初認識時に$9,000,000の負債を計上し、費用は認識しません。$9,000,000は資産原価に算入され、耐用年数にわたって減価償却されます。

questions.stem: At the beginning of the year, an asset retirement obligation had a fair value of $100,000. The risk-free rate is 5% and the credit-adjusted risk-free rate is 10%. What is the accretion expense for the year?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 410-20-35, accretion expense is calculated by applying the credit-adjusted risk-free interest rate to the beginning balance of the asset retirement obligation. Accretion expense = $100,000 × 10% = $10,000. The risk-free rate (5%) is not used; the rate locked in at initial recognition — the credit-adjusted risk-free rate of 10% — is applied.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20-35に基づき、アクリーション費用＝期首の資産除去債務残高×信用調整後リスクフリーレート。アクリーション費用＝$100,000×10%＝$10,000。リスクフリーレート（5%）ではなく、当初認識時に確定した信用調整後リスクフリーレート（10%）を使用します。

questions.stem: Which of the following is LEAST likely to be an indicator of potential impairment of a long-lived asset?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An operating segment is projecting continuing losses.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Independently verified fair value of similar assets is comparable to the asset's carrying value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: New laws are causing the company to dispose of the asset earlier than expected.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Competitive pressures are causing technological obsolescence of the asset.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, indicators of potential impairment include significant declines in market value, adverse changes in the business or legal environment, current or projected operating losses, and expected early disposal. When independently verified fair value of similar assets is comparable to the asset's carrying value, there is no gap between fair value and book value — this is the least likely indicator of impairment among the choices.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35における減損の兆候には、市場価値の大幅な下落、事業・法的環境の悪化、営業損失の発生・見込み、早期処分の予定などが含まれます。同様の資産の第三者確認済み公正価値が帳簿価額と同程度である場合、公正価値と帳簿価額の差がなく減損の可能性が最も低いです。

questions.stem: Sylvester Co. borrowed $900,000 at 12% on December 3, Year 4 specifically to construct a building. At the beginning of Year 5, the construction-in-progress balance was $536,000. During Year 5, additional costs were incurred: $240,000 on March 1 and $100,000 on December 31. Actual interest paid during Year 5 was $108,000. How much interest should Sylvester capitalize for Year 5?
questions.explanation: Weighted-average accumulated expenditures: $536,000 beginning balance × 12/12 = $536,000; March 1 addition $240,000 × 10/12 = $200,000; December 31 addition $100,000 × 0/12 = $0. Total WAAE = $736,000. Avoidable interest = $736,000 × 12% = $88,320. Since avoidable interest ($88,320) is less than actual interest paid ($108,000), Sylvester capitalizes $88,320.

questions.stem: Lyle Co. purchased a manufacturing facility on January 1. After remodeling, the facility was ready for use on March 1, and production began April 1. Interest costs were as follows: January 1–March 1: building $10,000, remodeling $2,000. March 1–April 1: building $5,000, remodeling $3,000. How much interest should Lyle capitalize?
questions.explanation: Interest capitalization continues until the asset is substantially complete and ready for its intended use, which occurred on March 1 when remodeling was completed. Building interest from January 1 to March 1 = $10,000; remodeling interest from January 1 to March 1 = $2,000; total capitalized = $12,000. The $5,000 building interest and $3,000 remodeling interest incurred after March 1 are expensed because the facility was already ready for use.
questions.explanationJa: 利息の資本化は、資産が意図した使用に実質的に完成した時点（改装が完了した3月1日）で終了します。1月1日～3月1日の建物利息＝$10,000、改装利息＝$2,000、資本化合計＝$12,000。3月1日以降の建物利息$5,000および改装利息$3,000は、施設がすでに使用可能状態にあるため費用処理します。

questions.stem: A corporation purchased 10 acres of land. The following costs were incurred: purchase price $1,000,000; real estate taxes in arrears $15,000; debt issuance costs $2,000; attorney fee for title search $5,000. At what amount should the corporation record the land?
questions.explanation: Land costs include the purchase price ($1,000,000), real estate taxes in arrears assumed by the buyer ($15,000 — a cost of acquiring clear title), and attorney fees for the title search ($5,000), totaling $1,020,000. Debt issuance costs ($2,000) are costs associated with obtaining financing, not acquiring the land, and are capitalized separately as a contra-liability and amortized under ASC 835-30.
questions.explanationJa: 土地原価には、取得価格$1,000,000、買主が負担する滞納固定資産税$15,000（権利確定のための取得原価）、権原調査の弁護士費用$5,000が含まれ合計$1,020,000です。借入費用$2,000はファイナンス調達に関するコストであり土地取得原価ではなく、ASC 835-30に基づき負債のコントラ勘定として別途資本化・償却します。

questions.stem: Last year, Katt Co. reduced the carrying value of its long-lived assets from $120,000 to $100,000 due to an impairment loss. In the current year, the fair value of those assets increased to $130,000. What amount should Katt record as a restoration of the previously recognized impairment loss?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 360-10-35, impairment losses recognized for long-lived assets held for use are not reversed if the asset's fair value subsequently increases. Katt's assets are held for use in operations, so even though fair value increased to $130,000, no restoration of the previously recognized $20,000 impairment loss is permitted. The carrying amount remains at $100,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 360-10-35に基づき、使用目的で保有する長期資産について認識した減損損失は、その後に公正価値が回復しても戻入れできません。Kattの資産は事業での使用目的であるため、公正価値が$130,000に上昇しても、過去に認識した$20,000の減損損失の回復を記録することはできません。帳簿価額は$100,000のままです。

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest – Specific Borrowing with Offsetting Investment Income
questions.stem: Herr Inc. has a fiscal year ending April 30. On May 1, of the previous year, Herr borrowed $10,000,000 at 15% to finance construction of its own building. Repayments of the loan are to commence the month following completion of the building. During the current year ended April 30, expenditures for the partially completed structure totaled $6,000,000. These expenditures were incurred evenly throughout the year. Interest earned on the unexpended portion of the loan amounted to $400,000 for the year. How much should be shown as capitalized interest on Herr ' s financial statements at April 30?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20 (formerly SFAS 34), interest cost to be capitalized equals the avoidable interest on the average accumulated expenditures. Since the $6,000,000 was incurred evenly throughout the year, the average accumulated expenditures = $6,000,000 ÷ 2 = $3,000,000. Interest to capitalize = $3,000,000 × 15% = $450,000. However, under the specific-borrowing rule, interest earned on the temporarily invested unexpended portion of the borrowing ($400,000) must be offset against the interest cost to be capitalized. Net capitalized interest = $450,000 – $400,000 = $50,000.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20（旧SFAS 34）の下、資本化すべき利息費用は、平均累積支出額に対する回避可能利息として計算する。$6,000,000が期中均等に発生したため、平均累積支出額＝$6,000,000÷2＝$3,000,000。資本化すべき利息＝$3,000,000×15%＝$450,000。ただし、特定借入金規定では、未使用残額を一時的に運用して得た利息（$400,000）を資本化利息から控除する。純資本化利息＝$450,000－$400,000＝$50,000。

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Interest – Average Accumulated Expenditures Basis
questions.stem: A company is constructing an asset for its own use. Construction began in the previous year. The asset is being financed entirely with a specific new borrowing. Construction expenditures were made last year and this year at the end of each quarter. The total amount of interest cost capitalized in the current year should be determined by applying the interest rate on the specific new borrowing to the

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total accumulated expenditures for the asset in both years

choices.label: B
choices.text: Average accumulated expenditures for the asset in both years

choices.label: C
choices.text: Average expenditures for the asset in the current year

choices.label: D
choices.text: Total expenditures for the asset in the current year (1847) 17 Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ FIXED ASSETS: I M PROVEMENTS A. Improvements  Capitalize improvement or expense improvement B. Terms 1. Betterments (putting on a new roof): Always 2. Additions, extensions, enlargements: Always 3. Extraordinary repairs (a new motor): Always C. Rule: You must capitalize an improvement if it does any one of four things: 1. Extends the 2. Increases the number 3. Increases the quality 4. Increases the efficiency D. Expense the improvement if none of the four criteria to capitalize are met Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 18 FIXED ASSETS: IMPAIRMENTS A. Impairments 1. To long-lived assets and/or intangibles 2. Three broad categories B. Held for use 1. It is impaired if the carrying value on the books is greater than any 2. Debit ; take to the income statement 3. Credit ;write down to fair value 4. That fair value becomes the 5. That will be depreciated over asset ' s remaining life 6. If it recovers in value, recoveries are C. Held for sale: Sale is probable within 12 months 1. It is impaired if the carrying value on the books is greater than the 2. Debit ;take to the income statement 3. Credit ;write down to fair value 4. Depreciation D. Held for disposal other than by sale: Plan to abandon, exchange, or distribute 1. Treat as held for use until 2. Use the same rules as held for use Independently answer multiple-choice questions 14 - 15 . Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 19 MULTIPLE-CHOICE QUESTIONS 14 - 15

questions.explanation: Under ASC 835-20, the amount of interest to capitalize each year is determined by applying the borrowing rate to the average accumulated expenditures for the qualifying asset during the capitalization period. Average accumulated expenditures include both prior-year and current-year expenditures weighted by the fraction of the year they were outstanding during the current period. Because expenditures were made at the end of each quarter in both years, the current-year calculation must incorporate the weighted-average of all accumulated expenditures in both years, not just the current year's expenditures.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 835-20の下、各年の資本化利息は、資本化期間中の適格資産に対する平均累積支出額に借入利率を適用して計算する。平均累積支出額は前年度・当年度両方の支出を、当期中に存在した期間で加重平均したものを含む。両年度とも各四半期末に支出が行われているため、当期の計算では当年度の支出額だけでなく、両年度の全累積支出額の加重平均を使用する必要がある。

questions.subtopic: Nonmonetary Exchange – Commercial Substance, Fair Value Recording
questions.stem: In an exchange with commercial substance, Vey Co. traded equipment with an original cost of $100,000 and accumulated depreciation of $40,000 for similar productive equipment with a fair value of $120,000. In addition, Vey received $30,000 cash in connection with this exchange. What should be Vey ' s carrying amount for the new equipment received on the day of exchange?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $120,000 (2586) Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 24 DEPRECIATION Illustrative Problem: Depreciation Company buys a machine for $20,000. E stimate there is salvage of $5,000. E stimate the useful life of the machine is 5 years. E stimate the machine will product 15,000 units over its life. A. Straight-Line (SL) Depreciation 1. Does back out salvage 2. (Cost - salvage) ÷ number of years of life = depreciation amount per year B. Productive Output Depreciation 1. Sometimes called the 2. Does back out salvage 3. (Cost-salvage) ÷ number of units machine is expected to product over its life = depreciation amount for every unit produced during the year C. Accelerated Methods of Depreciation 1. Take more depreciation in the early years of a fixed asset life and less depreciation in the later years of a fixed asset life 2. Depreciation is a method of allocation  It ' s a method of allocating the cost of a fixed asset to all the accounting periods that will benefit from that fixed asset Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 25 ACCELERATED DEPRECIATION Illustrative Problem: Depreciation Company buys a machine for $20,000. Estimate there is salvage of $5,000. Estimate the useful life of the machine is 5 years. Estimate the machine will produce 15,000 units over its life. A. Sum-of-the-Years ' -Digits (SYD) Depreciation 1. Add up the digits of the fixed asset useful life to get the sum  Shortcut : n (n + 1) / 2 = sum, where n = number of years of useful life 2. Does back out salvage 3. (Cost - salvage) × n / sum = depreciation amount for the first year  Subtract 1 from n, in the formula above, for each successive year B. Double-Declining-Balance (DDB) Depreciation 1. Double the straight-line rate × an ever - declining balance 2. Does not back out salvage  Buy only depreciate down to salvage; when you get to salvage, stop C. 150%-Declining-Balance Depreciation 1. One and a half times the straight-line rate × an ever - declining balance 2. Does not back out salvage  But only depreciate down to salvage; when you get to salvage, stop Independently answer multiple-choice questions 18 - 19 . Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 26 MULTIPLE-CHOICE QUESTIONS 18 - 19

questions.explanation: In an exchange with commercial substance, both gains and losses are recognized in full, and the new asset is recorded at its fair value under ASC 360. The fair value of the old equipment surrendered = fair value of assets received = fair value of new equipment ($120,000) + cash received ($30,000) = $150,000. Book value of old equipment = $100,000 – $40,000 = $60,000. Gain = $150,000 – $60,000 = $90,000 (recognized in full). The new equipment is recorded at its fair value of $120,000.
questions.explanationJa: 商業実質のある交換では、損益を全額認識し、新資産を公正価値で記録する（ASC 360）。引き渡した旧設備の公正価値＝受取資産の公正価値＝新設備の公正価値（$120,000）＋受取現金（$30,000）＝$150,000。旧設備の帳簿価額＝$100,000－$40,000＝$60,000。認識利益＝$150,000－$60,000＝$90,000（全額認識）。新設備は公正価値$120,000で記録する。

questions.stem: Spiro Corp. uses the sum-of-the-years ' -digits method to depreciate equipment purchased in January of the current year for $20,000. The estimated salvage value of the equipment is $2,000 and the estimated useful life is four years. What should Spiro report as the asset ' s carrying amount as of December 31 in the third year?
questions.explanation: Sum-of-the-years'-digits for a 4-year life: SYD = 4+3+2+1 = 10. Depreciable amount = $20,000 – $2,000 = $18,000. Year 1 depreciation = $18,000 × 4/10 = $7,200. Year 2 depreciation = $18,000 × 3/10 = $5,400. Year 3 depreciation = $18,000 × 2/10 = $3,600. Accumulated depreciation at end of Year 3 = $7,200 + $5,400 + $3,600 = $16,200. Carrying amount = $20,000 – $16,200 = $3,800.

questions.stem: Rye Co. purchased a machine with a four-year estimated useful life and an estimated 10% salvage value for $80,000 on January 1 of the current year. In its income statement for the third year, what would Rye report as the depreciation expense using the double-declining-balance method?

choices.label: D
choices.text: $20,000 (5573) Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 27 GROUP & COMPOSITE DEPRECIATION A. Depreciation  To save bookkeeping costs, some companies don ' t depreciate individual assets but instead they depreciate B. Group Depreciation 1. A grouping of similar assets 2. Example: Group delivery trucks into a group and depreciate the group C. Composite Depreciation 1. A grouping of dissimilar assets 2. Example: Group delivery trucks, automobiles, and computers into a group and depreciate the group D. Group / Composite Depreciation 1. All the calculations are the same with group and composite depreciation  The only difference is grouping of similar vs. dissimilar assets 2. Always start by calculating the straight-line depreciation for each asset in the group 3. (Total cost for all the assets in group - total salvage value for all the assets in group) ÷ total straight - line depreciation for all the as sets in the group = the group or co mposite life 4. Total straight-line depreciation for all the assets in the group ÷ total cost for all the assets in group = the group or composite rate 5. With both group and composite, there is never any gain or loss from the sale of an asset Answer multiple-choice questions 20 - 21 . Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 28 MULTIPLE-CHOICE QUESTIONS 20 - 21

questions.explanation: Double-declining-balance (DDB) rate = 2 ÷ 4 years = 50%. DDB does not deduct salvage value in the annual calculation but stops depreciating when the book value reaches salvage value ($80,000 × 10% = $8,000). Year 1: $80,000 × 50% = $40,000; book value = $40,000. Year 2: $40,000 × 50% = $20,000; book value = $20,000. Year 3: $20,000 × 50% = $10,000; however, the resulting book value would be $10,000, which is above the $8,000 salvage floor, so the full $10,000 is deducted. Year 3 depreciation = $10,000.
questions.explanationJa: 定率法（DDB）の償却率＝2÷4年＝50%。DDBは年次計算で残存価額を控除しないが、帳簿価額が残存価額（$80,000×10%＝$8,000）に達したら減価償却を停止する。Year 1：$80,000×50%＝$40,000、帳簿価額$40,000。Year 2：$40,000×50%＝$20,000、帳簿価額$20,000。Year 3：$20,000×50%＝$10,000、結果の帳簿価額$10,000は残存価額$8,000を上回るため、$10,000全額を計上する。Year 3の減価償却費＝$10,000。

questions.stem: A schedule of machinery owned by Lester Manufacturing Company is presented below: Total Cost Estimated Salvage Value Estimated Life in Years Machine A $550,000 $50,000 20 Machine B 200,000 20,000 15 Machine C 40,000 ─ 5 Lester computes depreciation on the straight-line method. Based upon the information presented, the composite life of these assets (in years) should be
questions.explanation: Composite life = (Total depreciable cost) ÷ (Total annual straight-line depreciation). Machine A: ($550,000 – $50,000) ÷ 20 = $25,000/yr. Machine B: ($200,000 – $20,000) ÷ 15 = $12,000/yr. Machine C: ($40,000 – $0) ÷ 5 = $8,000/yr. Total annual SL depreciation = $45,000. Total depreciable cost = $500,000 + $180,000 + $40,000 = $720,000. Composite life = $720,000 ÷ $45,000 = 16.0 years.

questions.explanation: Under both composite and group depreciation methods, when an individual asset is retired, no gain or loss is recognized. Instead, the accumulated depreciation account is debited for the difference between the original cost of the asset and any salvage or residual proceeds received. The asset account (cost) is credited, and the proceeds (if any) are debited to cash. This treatment applies equally to composite depreciation (dissimilar assets grouped together) and group depreciation (similar assets grouped together).
questions.explanationJa: 複合法・グループ法のいずれにおいても、個々の資産が除却された際に損益は認識されない。代わりに、減価償却累計額が資産の取得原価と残存回収額の差額（原価マイナス残存回収）で借方計上される。資産勘定（原価）は貸方に、受取額（ある場合）は現金として借方に計上する。この処理は異種資産をグループ化した複合法にも、同種資産をグループ化したグループ法にも等しく適用される。

questions.subtopic: R&D Costs – Equipment with No Alternative Future Use
questions.stem: On January 1, Jambon purchased equipment for use in developing a new product. Jambon uses the straight-line depreciation method. The equipment could provide benefits over a 10-year period. However, the new product development is expected to take five years, and the equipment can be used only for this project. Jambon ' s expense for the year equals

choices.label: A
choices.text: The total cost of the equipment.

choices.label: B
choices.text: One-fifth of the cost of the equipment.

choices.label: C
choices.text: One-tenth of the cost of the equipment.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Zero. (2540) I ndependently answer multiple-choice questions 26 - 27 . Inventory, Fixed Assets & Intangibles ￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣￣ 33 MULTIPLE-CHOICE QUESTIONS 26 - 27

questions.explanation: Under ASC 730-10, equipment acquired solely for use in a specific R&D project with no alternative future use is expensed in full as research and development cost in the period incurred. Because the equipment can only be used for this one product development project and has no alternative future uses, the entire cost must be expensed immediately — not capitalized and depreciated. The fact that the equipment 'could provide benefits over a 10-year period' is irrelevant when it has no alternative future use beyond this single project.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730-10の下、特定のR&Dプロジェクト専用で代替的将来使用がない設備は、取得した期間に全額研究開発費として費用処理する。この設備は1つの製品開発プロジェクトにしか使用できず代替的将来使用がないため、全費用を即時に費用計上しなければならない（資本化して減価償却しない）。設備が「10年間便益を提供し得る」という事実は、当該単一プロジェクト以外の代替的将来使用がない場合には無関係となる。

questions.stem: On June 30, Union Inc. purchased goodwill of $125,000 when it acquired the net assets of Apex Corp. During the year, Union incurred additional costs of developing goodwill, by training Apex employees ($50,000) and hiring additional Apex employees ($25,000). Union ' s December 31 balance sheet should report goodwill of
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-20, only goodwill acquired in a business combination is recognized and capitalized on the balance sheet. Internally generated goodwill — including costs to develop, maintain, or restore goodwill — is expensed as incurred. Training existing employees ($50,000) and hiring additional employees ($25,000) are costs of internally developing goodwill and must be expensed, not capitalized. Therefore, only the $125,000 of purchased goodwill from the acquisition of Apex Corp. is reported on the balance sheet.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-20の下、企業結合で取得したのれん（購入のれん）のみが貸借対照表に資産計上される。自己創設のれん（のれんの開発・維持・回復のためのコスト）は発生時に費用処理する。既存従業員の研修費（$50,000）と追加雇用費（$25,000）は自己創設のれんのコストであり費用計上が必要で、資本化できない。したがって、貸借対照表に計上されるのはApex Corp.の買収で取得した$125,000の購入のれんのみ。

questions.stem: In Pitt ' s December 31 balance sheet, what amount should be capitalized as software cost, subject to amortization?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 350-40 and ASC 985-20, software costs incurred after technological feasibility is established (and before the product is available for general release) are capitalized. Costs incurred before technological feasibility — such as planning, research, and preliminary project activities — are expensed as incurred. In the standard AICPA version of the Pitt scenario, only $54,000 of costs were incurred after technological feasibility was established, making $54,000 the amount to capitalize and subsequently amortize. Costs totaling more than $54,000 include pre-feasibility amounts that must be expensed.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 350-40およびASC 985-20の下、技術的実行可能性が確立された後（製品が一般公開される前まで）に発生したソフトウェアコストは資本化される。技術的実行可能性確立前のコスト（計画・調査・予備プロジェクト活動など）は発生時に費用処理する。AICPAのPittシナリオの標準版では、技術的実行可能性確立後に$54,000のコストが発生しており、$54,000が資本化（その後償却）すべき金額となる。$54,000を超えるコストには技術的実行可能性確立前のコストが含まれており費用処理が必要。

questions.subtopic: Capitalized Software Costs – Amortization and NRV Test
questions.stem: On December 31, year 1, Bit Co. had capitalized costs for a new computer software product with an economic life of five years. Sales for year 2 were 30 percent of expected total sales of the software. At December 31, year 2, the software had a net realizable value equal to 90 percent of the capitalized cost. What percentage of the original capitalized cost should be reported as the net amount on Bit ' s December 31, year 2 balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 985-20, capitalized software costs are amortized using the greater of: (1) the ratio of current-year gross revenues to total expected gross revenues over the life of the product, or (2) the straight-line method over the remaining estimated economic life. The ratio method gives 30% amortization (30% of total expected sales). The straight-line method gives 1/5 = 20% per year. The greater amount is 30%, so 30% is amortized in Year 2. Remaining net carrying amount = 100% – 30% = 70%. The net realizable value (NRV) test requires that the carrying amount not exceed NRV (90%). Since 70% < 90%, the NRV floor is not breached, and the balance sheet amount is 70% of original capitalized cost.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 985-20の下、資本化ソフトウェアコストの償却額は次の大きい方を使用する：(1) 当期総収益÷製品ライフ全体の期待総収益の比率法、または(2) 残存経済的耐用年数にわたる定額法。比率法：当期売上30%÷総期待売上＝30%の償却。定額法：1/5＝20%。大きい方は30%であるためYear 2に30%を償却する。純帳簿価額＝100%－30%＝70%。正味実現可能価額（NRV）テスト：帳簿価額がNRV（90%）を超えてはならない。70%＜90%のためNRVを下回っており、貸借対照表上の金額は原資本化コストの70%。

questions.stem: Oak Co., a newly formed corporation, incurred the following expenditures related to land and building: County assessment for sewer lines $ 2,500 Title search fees 625 Cash paid for land with building to be demolished 135,000 Excavation for construction of basement 21,000 Removal of old building $21,000 less salvage of $5,000 16,000 At what amount should Oak record the land?
questions.explanation: All costs necessary to acquire land and prepare it for its intended use are capitalized as part of the cost of land. The land cost includes: purchase price $135,000 + county sewer assessment $2,500 + title search fees $625 + net cost of demolishing the old building ($21,000 − $5,000 salvage) $16,000 = $154,125. The excavation for the basement is a cost of constructing the building, not of the land, and is excluded from the land account (ASC 360-10-25).
questions.explanationJa: 土地の取得と使用準備に必要なすべての費用は土地原価として資産計上します。土地原価 = 購入代金$135,000 + 下水道評価$2,500 + 権原調査費$625 + 旧建物解体費純額($21,000 − $5,000)$16,000 = $154,125。地下室掘削費は建物建設コストに分類されるため土地には含まれません（ASC 360-10-25）。

questions.stem: Cody Corp. incurred the following costs during the year: Design of tools, jigs, molds, and dies involving new technology $125,000 Modification of the formulation of a process $160,000 Trouble-shooting in connection with breakdowns during commercial production $100,000 Adaptation of an existing capability to a particular customer ' s need as part of a continuing commercial activity $110,000 In its year-end income statement, Cody should report research and development expense of
questions.explanation: Under ASC 730-10, research and development costs must be expensed as incurred. Design of tools, jigs, molds, and dies involving new technology ($125,000) qualifies as R&D because it involves developing new technology. Modification of the formulation of a process ($160,000) is an R&D activity. Together these total $285,000. Trouble-shooting during commercial production ($100,000) is excluded from R&D because it relates to ongoing production operations, not new development. Adaptation of an existing capability for a specific customer as part of continuing commercial activity ($110,000) is also excluded because it is a commercial activity, not research or development (ASC 730-10-25).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 730-10の下、研究開発コストは発生時に費用処理します。新技術を使用した工具・金型等の設計$125,000と工程処方の改良$160,000はR&Dに該当し、合計$285,000がR&D費用となります。商業生産中のトラブルシューティング$100,000は生産活動のため除外、既存能力を特定顧客向けに適応する継続的商業活動$110,000も除外です（ASC 730-10-25）。

questions.stem: During the year, Jase Co. incurred research and development costs of $136,000 in its laboratories relating to a patent that was granted on July 1. Costs of registering the patent equaled $34,000. The patent ' s legal life is 20 years, and its estimated economic life is 10 years. In its December 31 balance sheet, what amount should Jase report as patent, net of accumulated amortization?
questions.explanation: The $136,000 of R&D costs incurred in the laboratory are expensed as incurred under ASC 730-10 and are not capitalized as part of the patent. Only the $34,000 registration cost is capitalized. A patent is amortized over the shorter of its legal life (20 years) and its estimated economic life (10 years), so the amortization period is 10 years. The patent was granted on July 1, so amortization covers 6 months in the current year: $34,000 ÷ 10 × 6/12 = $1,700. Net patent value at December 31 = $34,000 − $1,700 = $32,300 (ASC 350-30-35).
questions.explanationJa: 研究開発コスト$136,000はASC 730-10に基づき発生時費用処理され、特許原価に含まれません。資産計上されるのは登録費用$34,000のみです。特許の償却は法定耐用年数20年と経済的耐用年数10年の短い方の10年で行います。特許付与は7月1日のため当期の償却は6ヶ月分：$34,000 ÷ 10 × 6/12 = $1,700。期末純額 = $34,000 − $1,700 = $32,300（ASC 350-30-35）。

questions.stem: In Pitt ' s December 31 balance sheet, what amount should be reported in inventory?
questions.explanation: For computer software developed for sale, costs after technological feasibility are capitalized, while duplication and packaging costs are classified as inventory. Under ASC 350-40/985-20, once the product master is complete, the costs to duplicate (copy) and package the software units constitute inventory. The duplication costs of $25,000 and packaging costs of $9,000 total $34,000, which should be reported as inventory. The product masters ($15,000) and post-feasibility coding/testing costs are capitalized software assets, not inventory.
questions.explanationJa: 販売目的ソフトウェアでは、技術的実現可能性確立後のコストは資産計上され、複製・梱包コストは棚卸資産に分類されます（ASC 985-20）。製品マスターから複製する費用$25,000と梱包費$9,000の合計$34,000が棚卸資産として報告されます。製品マスター$15,000や可能性確立後のコーディング・テスト費用は無形資産（資本化ソフトウェア）であり棚卸資産ではありません。
